預估：**1.5 day**

**Y-prep 總預估**：**2.5 day**

**上線後的 Gerrit / JIRA 使用矩陣**：

| 情境 | Gerrit | JIRA |
|---|---|---|
| 單 tenant 串起來用 | ✅ 已可用 | ✅ 已可用（outbound + intake） |
| 入站 webhook 觸發 agent | ✅ 已可用（`-1 comment` 自動 file fix task） | ✅ Y-prep.3 後可用（`/command` comment + label intake） |
| 事件 routing regression test 覆蓋 | ✅ Y-prep.1 後閉環 | ✅ Y-prep.3 自帶 test |
| Webhook secret one-time rotate UX | ✅ 原生 wizard 支援 | ✅ Y-prep.2 後對齊 |
| 多 tenant 各自獨立 JIRA 實例 | N/A（Gerrit 本身不適合 SaaS 拆） | ❌ 要等 Y4+Phase 5b |

---

## 🅩 Priority Z — LLM Provider Observability（餘額 / rate-limit / 真實用量可視化）

> 背景（2026-04-22 operator audit）：9 家 LLM provider 全部只做**本地 token 統計**——`backend/agents/llm.py:32-60` 的 `TokenTrackingCallback` 只抓 response body 的 `usage` 欄位，完全沒讀 HTTP response header 的 `x-ratelimit-*`；`backend/routers/system.py:1091-1100` 的成本表硬寫 8 個 model、其他 fallback (1.0, 3.0) USD/M，改 rate 要改 code 重部署；沒有任何 code 打過 provider 的 balance API；UI `components/omnisight/token-usage-stats.tsx` 只顯示「本地累計花費」而不知道「provider 那邊還有多少 credit」。operator 問 "API KEY 都串了，能看餘額嗎？"，答案是不行——只能看自己花了多少。
>
> **商業邏輯**：運營要在 "快燒完" 之前決定 "加值還是轉備援 provider"，沒有 balance / rate-limit 可視化就永遠是打到 429 / 402 才知道。9 家之中**只有 DeepSeek + OpenRouter** 有公開的 balance API 可以用 API key 查（Anthropic / xAI / Groq / Together 官方無；OpenAI / Google 需 session cookie 或 GCP 認證，不實用）；rate-limit header 則是**幾乎每家都回**（Anthropic / OpenAI / Google / Groq 都有），只是我們丟掉沒解析。
>
> **範圍紅線**：只做 "把能拿的資訊撈起來展示"，不做 dashboard scraping / 不做 session-cookie 竊取 / 不做預測性自動加值 / 不做跨 provider arbitrage 自動切換。
>
> **相依**：✅ 零。`SharedKV`（`backend/shared_state.py:150`）已有、`TokenTrackingCallback`（`backend/agents/llm.py:32-60`）已有、token-usage-stats UI 已有、`/providers` endpoint 已有。所有 step 都是擴充既有 code、不動 schema、不動 auth、不動 tenant model。
>
> **為什麼優先級往前推**：
> - 零阻塞：和 Y-prep 一樣可立即開工，而且彼此完全獨立
> - 使用頻率：operator 每天都會看（`token-usage-stats` 本來就在 dashboard 右側常駐）
> - 單日可見效益：Z.1 + Z.2 合計 1.5 day 就能讓 UI 顯示「DeepSeek 餘額 $12.50 / OpenRouter 還剩 300 req/min」
> - 和 Priority Y / Y-prep 的序關係是並行而非串行——可以排同一個工程師跑 2 週 sprint（Y-prep + Z + Y0），彼此不會撞

### Z.1 Rate-limit header 擷取 + SharedKV 儲存 (#290)

`TokenTrackingCallback` 目前只看 body，把 response header 的 rate-limit 資訊整個丟掉。補一下就每家都受益。

- [x] 改 `backend/agents/llm.py:TokenTrackingCallback.on_llm_end()` 讀 response object 的 header dict（LangChain 底層 `openai.AsyncOpenAI` / `anthropic.AsyncAnthropic` 都有曝 `.response.headers`）。 <!-- 2026-04-24: `_extract_response_headers(response)` static method walks 5 candidate paths (`llm_output['headers']` / `llm_output['response_headers']` / `response_metadata['headers']` / flattened `response_metadata` with `x-ratelimit-*|anthropic-ratelimit-*|retry-after` keys / `generation_info['headers']`) and returns the first non-empty hit. Snapshot lives on `self.last_response_headers` (init `{}`, overwritten on every on_llm_end). Missing/unparseable → `{}` + debug log, never raises. Downstream Z.1 checkboxes (provider name mapping + SharedKV write) read this attribute. Tests: `backend/tests/test_ratelimit_header_extract.py` 24 cases — per-path surface + priority order + empty/malformed degradation + Ollama-shape → `{}` + on_llm_end overwrite + inner-try/except graceful-drift. Normalise mapping table, SharedKV("provider_ratelimit") write, TTL = 60s, and end-to-end 4-provider header normalisation tests belong to the remaining Z.1 checkboxes and are NOT delivered by this commit. -->
- [x] 每 provider 一個 header name mapping table（`_PROVIDER_RATELIMIT_HEADERS`），把不同名字（`anthropic-ratelimit-requests-remaining` / `x-ratelimit-remaining-requests` / `x-ratelimit-remaining-tokens`）規整化成統一 dict：`{remaining_requests, remaining_tokens, reset_at_ts, retry_after_s}`. <!-- 2026-04-24 Z.1-checkbox-2 shipped: module-const `_PROVIDER_RATELIMIT_HEADERS` in `backend/agents/llm.py` covers the seven rate-limited providers (Anthropic native + six OpenAI-compatible family members: OpenAI / xAI / Groq / DeepSeek / Together / OpenRouter). Ollama + Google Gemini deliberately absent — `_normalize_ratelimit_headers` returns `{}` for them so the downstream SharedKV write (next checkbox) short-circuits on truthiness. Three parser helpers handle the shape zoo: `_parse_reset_value` (ISO 8601 absolute / OpenAI duration `"12ms"|"1s"|"1m30s"` / bare numeric fallback), `_parse_retry_after_seconds` (integer + HTTP-date RFC 9110 §10.2.3), `_parse_int_or_none` (preserves negative remaining as an upstream-drift signal, coerces `"42.0"` float-shape). Unified output dict: `{remaining_requests:int|None, remaining_tokens:int|None, reset_at_ts:float|None (unix epoch), retry_after_s:float|None}`; case-insensitive header lookup defends against `httpx.Headers` mixed-case drift; missing fields degrade to `None` (NULL-vs-genuine-zero); all-`None` collapses to `{}` so no-ratelimit-headers response is indistinguishable from unknown-provider downstream. `on_llm_end` writes `self.last_ratelimit_state` alongside the existing `self.last_response_headers` under an independent try/except — normalise bug can't unseat the raw capture and vice-versa. Tests: `backend/tests/test_ratelimit_header_normalize.py` 72 cases — mapping-table coverage guard (all 7 providers, 4 unified keys each) + per-parser unit matrix + Anthropic full/partial normalise + OpenAI-family parametrised normalise × 6 providers + 9 edge cases (unknown provider / None / empty / non-dict / case-drift / malformed field) + 4 on_llm_end wiring cases (initial state / full capture / Ollama skip / normalise-failure graceful). Also fixed pre-existing N4 firewall drift: re-exported `ChatGeneration` from `backend.llm_adapter` + routed both header-related test files through the adapter (the extract test from checkbox 1 had the same violation). Module-global audit (SOP Step 1): `_PROVIDER_RATELIMIT_HEADERS` is a module-const dict literal (answer #1 "不共享，因為每 worker 從同樣來源推導出同樣的值"). `TokenTrackingCallback.last_ratelimit_state` is per-instance — the callback wraps a single LangChain request and has no shared state. Zero new cross-worker coordination surface. SharedKV("provider_ratelimit") write + 60s TTL + four-provider end-to-end test belong to the remaining Z.1 checkboxes and are NOT delivered by this commit. -->
- [x] 存進 `SharedKV("provider_ratelimit")`，key = provider name（不加 tenant / user 維度——這是整個實例的 rate-limit 狀態，所有 tenant 共享同一把 API key，資訊 scope 相同）。TTL = 60s（rate-limit 每分鐘 reset，久了資訊就過期）。 <!-- 2026-04-24 Z.1-checkbox-3 shipped: (A) `backend/shared_state.py::SharedKV` gains three public methods — `set_with_ttl(field, value, ttl_seconds, *, now=None)` / `get_with_ttl(field, *, now=None)` / `get_all_with_ttl(*, now=None)` — that wrap any JSON-serialisable payload in a `{_data, _expires_at}` envelope and lazy-prune expired/malformed entries on read. Chose embedded-expiry over `r.expire(hash_key)` (would cross-contaminate per-field TTLs — touching `openai` would refresh `anthropic`'s clock) and over Redis 7.4 `HEXPIRE` (not guaranteed in production). Injectable `now` kwarg keeps tests free of `time.time` monkey-patching. Zero-or-negative TTL raises `ValueError` — caller bug, not a runtime state. Store is self-healing: legacy plain-`set` values, non-JSON strings, and envelope-less dicts all get deleted on first `get_with_ttl` pass. (B) `backend/agents/llm.py` grows module constants `_RATELIMIT_TTL_SECONDS = 60.0`, `_RATELIMIT_KV_NAMESPACE = "provider_ratelimit"`, and lazy singleton `_get_ratelimit_kv()` (cache the `SharedKV("provider_ratelimit")` instance so `shared_state.reset_for_tests` can re-open the underlying Redis client without us holding a stale reference). `TokenTrackingCallback.on_llm_end` gains a third try/except (siblings of the raw-headers-extract + normalise blocks): if `self.provider and self.last_ratelimit_state`, mirror the normalised dict into `_get_ratelimit_kv().set_with_ttl(self.provider, state, 60.0)`. Empty-state and provider=None turns are silent no-ops — writing `{}` under a provider key would overwrite a genuine prior snapshot from a sibling turn with stale "no data", strictly worse than letting the existing entry age out. KV-side exception (Redis drop, simulated blowup) degrades to `logger.debug` — LLM turn completes, dashboard just misses one update. (C) Module-global audit (SOP Step 1, 2026-04-21 rule): `_RATELIMIT_TTL_SECONDS` + `_RATELIMIT_KV_NAMESPACE` are module-const literals (answer #1 "不共享，因為每 worker 從同樣來源推導出同樣的值"). `_ratelimit_kv_singleton` is a per-worker lazy-init handle to `SharedKV`, itself Redis-backed when `OMNISIGHT_REDIS_URL` is set (answer #2 "透過 Redis 協調" — one hash shared across uvicorn workers + replicas) with in-memory fallback for single-worker dev (answer #3 "故意每 worker 獨立"). Zero new cross-worker coordination surface beyond what `SharedKV` already guarantees — matches the `session_presence = SessionPresence()` precedent at `shared_state.py:345`. Tests: `backend/tests/test_ratelimit_kv_write.py` 22 cases — SharedKV TTL unit matrix (round-trip / scalar payloads / absent field / expired lazy-prune / per-field independence regression / zero+negative TTL rejection / malformed legacy entries / overwrite refreshes own TTL) + `get_all_with_ttl` filter + malformed-pruning + on_llm_end wiring × 4 providers (anthropic native + 4 OpenAI-family — openai/xai/groq/deepseek coexisting) + 3 skip contracts (ollama unmapped / provider=None / empty state preserves prior snapshot) + 60s-TTL end-to-end (frozen clock shows live at t+30s, pruned at t+61s) + SharedKV-explodes debug-log-only degradation + module-const + singleton idempotence locks. All 22 pass; pre-existing 155 Z.1 tests + session_presence + token_usage_shared + turn_boundary/turn_metrics still green. Runtime smoke: `python3 -c 'import ...'` exercises full wiring, observes `{'remaining_requests': 47, 'remaining_tokens': 199876, 'reset_at_ts': 1777035600.0, 'retry_after_s': 0.0}` under `provider_ratelimit/anthropic`. Remaining Z.1 checkboxes (graceful-fallback boundaries test + four-provider mock end-to-end normalise test) belong to the next two rows; this commit delivers ONLY the SharedKV persistence + TTL. -->
<!-- Production status: dev-only — in-memory fallback exercised (tests all pass without Redis); cross-worker Redis path lands the moment operator sets OMNISIGHT_REDIS_URL (no code change). Next gate: deployed-inactive — waits on Z.2/Z.4 readers (balance-fetch + adaptive-backoff) to actually consume `SharedKV("provider_ratelimit")`; until then the mirror is a producer without subscribers. -->
- [x] 邊界：header 缺失時不 raise，只 debug log；不認識的 provider（Ollama）跳過；LangChain 底層變動 header 路徑時 fallback graceful。 <!-- 2026-04-24 Z.1-checkbox-4 shipped: this row is a contract-lock, not new production code — the three boundary guarantees were already implemented across the prior three Z.1 checkboxes (inner try/except triple around `_extract_response_headers` / `_normalize_ratelimit_headers` / `SharedKV.set_with_ttl` at `backend/agents/llm.py` L570-636, all degrading to DEBUG-level log; provider allow-list via `_PROVIDER_RATELIMIT_HEADERS` → unmapped returns `{}` → KV write guard `if self.provider and self.last_ratelimit_state` short-circuits; 5-path extract walk that collapses to `{}` when every path shape-shifts). This checkbox locks them as a *combined* contract via `backend/tests/test_ratelimit_boundaries.py` — 23 cases across 4 classes: (A) `TestHeaderMissingNeverRaises` × 3 — headerless Anthropic / `llm_output['headers']=None` / partial-headers-with-only-retry-after, each verifies DEBUG-only logs + `track_tokens` still fires once; (B) `TestUnknownProviderSkipped` × 5 — Ollama with empty headers / Ollama with spurious OpenAI-shape headers (proxy rewrite case — mapping rule is structural, not content-based) / Google Gemini same treatment / sibling-snapshot-survives-Ollama-turn (core dashboard invariant) / `provider=None` legacy path; (C) `TestLangChainPathDriftGraceful` × 6 — each of the 5 candidate paths shape-shifted individually with fall-through verified + all-5-absent collapse-to-`{}` + extract-itself-raises via monkeypatch + normalise-itself-raises proving raw snapshot survives (three try/except blocks are genuinely independent); (D) `TestNeverRaiseMatrix` × 9 — parametrised corrupt-LLMResult sweep (nil / empty / wrong-typed headers / empty generations list outer+inner) + KV-write-blowup + triple-layer simultaneous failure still fires `track_tokens` once. No production code modified — implementation was already checkbox-4-compliant. Module-global audit (SOP Step 1, 2026-04-21): zero module-global state added; test file uses the existing `_ratelimit_kv_singleton` lazy-init + per-test `fresh_kv` fixture (mirrors `test_ratelimit_kv_write.py`). Runtime smoke: 23 boundary tests + 118 pre-existing Z.1 tests = 141/141 green in 0.73s; adjacent suites (token_usage_shared / turn_metrics_sse / sessions_presence / turn_boundary_stamps / turn_complete_sse) still green. Pre-commit fingerprint grep clean. Remaining Z.1 checkbox (four-provider end-to-end mock + SharedKV write + TTL expiry) belongs to the next row. -->
- [x] 測試：mock 四家 provider 的 response header，驗 normalise 正確、SharedKV 寫入、TTL 到期清空。 <!-- 2026-04-24 Z.1-checkbox-5 shipped: `backend/tests/test_ratelimit_capture.py` 18 cases (5 classes) exercises the full `TokenTrackingCallback.on_llm_end` rate-limit pipeline end-to-end — raw-header extract → per-provider normalise → `SharedKV("provider_ratelimit")` mirror — against realistic vendor-shaped headers from the four providers Z.2/Z.4 will consume. Picked Anthropic (native `anthropic-ratelimit-*` + ISO 8601 absolute reset), OpenAI (OpenAI-family `x-ratelimit-*` + duration-string reset `"14ms"` / `"8.64s"`), DeepSeek (`x-ratelimit-*` + bare numeric-seconds reset — tests the `_parse_reset_value` fall-through past ISO hint + duration grammar), and OpenRouter (`x-ratelimit-*` + minute-duration `"1m"` reset — per-minute free-tier bucket shape). Header payloads mirror each vendor's public API — not synthetic minimal sets — so a real-world regression (e.g. vendor adds/renames a header) would fail the test. Classes: (A) `TestFourProviderNormalise` × 4 — parametrised realistic-headers → unified dict assertion with per-field equality for `remaining_requests` / `remaining_tokens` / `retry_after_s` + `reset_at_ts is not None and isinstance(float)` (duration-string shapes anchor on wall-clock `time.time()` so equality would be flaky); (B) `TestFourProviderSharedKVWrite` × 4 — parametrised SharedKV byte-for-byte mirror check (`stored == cb.last_ratelimit_state`) + no-cross-provider-leakage; (C) `TestFourProviderCoexistence` × 2 — four sequential turns → four independent keys (core Z.2/Z.4 dashboard invariant) + per-field TTL independence across providers (Anthropic@t=1000 / OpenAI@t=1050 read at t=1061 → only OpenAI survives); (D) `TestFourProviderTTLExpiry` × 5 — parametrised 60s TTL expiry per provider (live at t+30s / pruned at t+61s / underlying hash row pruned-not-masked) + boundary precision lock (live at t+59.999 / pruned at t+60.001 — half-open interval contract of the envelope `_expires_at` field) + `_RATELIMIT_TTL_SECONDS == 60.0` module-const check; (E) `TestUnknownProviderSkipsEndToEnd` × 3 — Ollama turn between live Anthropic + OpenAI preserves both siblings / Gemini with spurious OpenAI-shape headers (proxy rewrite case) still skipped (allow-list is structural not content-based) / Ollama as very first turn leaves KV empty (deployed-inactive initial state). No production code modified — this is a test-only contract-lock on top of checkboxes 1-4. Module-global audit (SOP Step 1, 2026-04-21): zero new module-global state; fixture `fresh_kv` cycles the existing `_ratelimit_kv_singleton` the same way `test_ratelimit_kv_write.py` + `test_ratelimit_boundaries.py` do. Pre-commit fingerprint grep clean (no `_conn()` / `await conn.commit()` / `datetime('now')` / SQLite `?` placeholders). Runtime: 18/18 new pass in 0.52s; full Z.1 suite (5 files) 159/159 pass in 0.78s; adjacent `token_usage_shared` + `turn_metrics_sse` + `turn_boundary_stamps` + `turn_complete_sse` 22/22 pass in 0.68s. Closes Z.1 Rate-limit header capture epic — next gate is Z.2 balance-fetch which consumes `SharedKV("provider_ratelimit")` alongside its own `provider_balance` namespace. -->

預估：**0.5 day**

### Z.2 Balance fetch — DeepSeek + OpenRouter + fallback 設計 (#291)

兩家有公開 balance API，直接接。其他家沒 API 的不 fake 數字、顯示 "unsupported"。

- [x] 新 `backend/llm_balance.py`：每家一個 `async fetch_balance_<provider>(api_key) -> BalanceInfo | None`，回 `{currency, balance_remaining, granted_total, usage_total, last_refreshed_at, raw}`。
  - DeepSeek：`GET https://api.deepseek.com/user/balance`，header `Authorization: Bearer {key}`，回 `{balance_infos: [{total_balance, granted_balance, topped_up_balance, currency}]}`.
  - OpenRouter：`GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/auth/key`，header 同上，回 `{data: {label, usage, limit, limit_remaining, is_free_tier, rate_limit}}`.
  <!-- 2026-04-24 Z.2-checkbox-1 shipped: `backend/llm_balance.py` exports `fetch_balance_deepseek(api_key, *, timeout=10.0, client_factory=None, now=None)` + `fetch_balance_openrouter(...)` returning a `BalanceInfo` TypedDict (`currency`, `balance_remaining`, `granted_total`, `usage_total`, `last_refreshed_at`, `raw`) — `total=False` so per-provider field omissions stay typed. Shared `_http_get_json` helper opens a fresh `httpx.AsyncClient` per call (matches `backend/cms/sanity.py:110` + sentry mobile pattern — once-per-10-min cadence does not warrant a global client lifecycle), sends `Authorization: Bearer {key}` + `Accept: application/json`, maps responses three ways: `{401, 403}` → `None` (cache layer must not store auth-fail envelopes — operator may rotate the key and we want the next refresh to pick it up cleanly), `>= 500` + `>= 400 (non-auth)` + transport errors + non-JSON → `BalanceFetchError(provider, reason)` (cache layer keeps showing prior snapshot with `stale_since`), `2xx` → parsed JSON. Empty `api_key` short-circuits to `None` without any HTTP call (defence against the resolver chain handing the fetcher an empty string for keyless providers like Ollama if someone wires the registry wrong later). DeepSeek normaliser picks `balance_infos[0]` (multi-currency rare, single-key cache cannot represent two simultaneously) but preserves the full vendor list under `raw`; sets `usage_total=None` (DeepSeek does not report cumulative usage — fabricating a 0 would be misleading). OpenRouter normaliser maps `data.{limit_remaining → balance_remaining, limit → granted_total, usage → usage_total}` with a `limit - usage` fallback floored at 0.0 when `limit_remaining` is omitted (older API + future schema-drift insurance); currency hard-coded to `"USD"` per OpenRouter's pricing model. `_coerce_float` tolerates DeepSeek's stringified `"0.85"` shape + None-or-unparseable degrades to `None` rather than crashing. Module registry `SUPPORTED_BALANCE_PROVIDERS = {"deepseek": fetch_balance_deepseek, "openrouter": fetch_balance_openrouter}` + `is_balance_supported(name)` predicate so the upcoming `/runtime/providers/{provider}/balance` endpoint can short-circuit unsupported providers without threading a parallel allow-list. **Module-global audit (SOP Step 1)**: zero module-global mutable state — fetchers are pure functions of (api_key, vendor response); registry is a module-const dict literal (rubric #1 "每 worker 從同樣來源推導"). The cache + cross-worker coordination work belongs to the next Z.2 checkbox (background task + `SharedKV("provider_balance")` write). **Tests**: `backend/tests/test_llm_balance_fetchers.py` 46 cases (4 classes) — registry shape + happy-path normalise per provider + bearer header + URL contract lock + auth-fail × {401, 403} → None + 5xx × {500, 502, 503, 504} → BalanceFetchError + transport error → BalanceFetchError + 4xx-not-auth (429) → raises + non-JSON → raises + DeepSeek multi-currency picks-first-preserves-raw + DeepSeek string amount coercion + DeepSeek unparseable-amount-degrades-to-None + DeepSeek malformed body (missing balance_infos / empty list) + OpenRouter limit_remaining derivation + unlimited account stays None + overshoot floored at 0 + missing data envelope + empty api_key short-circuits without HTTP for both providers. All 46 pass; pre-existing `test_llm_credentials_crud.py` still green (8 pass + 23 PG-only skips). Pre-commit fingerprint grep clean. Remaining Z.2 checkboxes (background refresh task + SharedKV write + endpoints + boundary contract + integration tests) are NOT delivered by this commit. -->
  <!-- Production status: dev-only — module is callable but no production caller wired yet (no lifespan task, no router). Next gate: deployed-inactive — waits on Z.2 checkbox 2 (background refresh + SharedKV write) and checkbox 3 (`/runtime/providers/{provider}/balance` endpoint) to actually surface balances to the dashboard. -->
- [x] 背景 async task（lifespan 啟動）每 10 min refresh 一次，結果塞 `SharedKV("provider_balance")`, key = provider name. 失敗重試 exponential backoff 上限 1 小時。
  <!-- 2026-04-24 Z.2-checkbox-2 shipped: `backend/llm_balance_refresher.py` exports `run_refresh_loop(*, interval_s=None)` (singleton background coroutine matching the Phase-52 DLQ / Phase-63-E memory-decay convention — `_LOOP_RUNNING` flag + cancellation-aware `asyncio.sleep` + `finally`-reset) + `refresh_once(*, state, base_interval_s, now=None, fetchers=None, key_resolver=None, kv=None)` (pure one-tick logic, all side-effect entrypoints injectable for unit tests). Wired into `backend/main.py:246` lifespan right after `memory_decay.run_decay_loop()` as `balance_task = asyncio.create_task(_lbr.run_refresh_loop())` and added to the shutdown-cancel tuple at line 287. Constants: `DEFAULT_INTERVAL_S=600.0` (10 min), `MAX_BACKOFF_S=3600.0` (1 h cap per checkbox spec), `BALANCE_NAMESPACE="provider_balance"` (exported so the upcoming `/runtime/providers/*/balance` endpoint can `from backend.llm_balance_refresher import BALANCE_NAMESPACE` rather than re-declare the string). Per-tick flow: iterate `SUPPORTED_BALANCE_PROVIDERS` → look up per-provider `_ProviderBackoff` state (dataclass with `consecutive_failures` + `next_attempt_at`) → skip if within backoff window (emit `"backoff"` outcome) → resolve api_key via Settings scalar read (`settings.{provider}_api_key` — refresher runs in system scope so we avoid the tenant-contextvar + pool round-trip, and bypass `get_llm_credential_sync`'s per-worker deprecation-warn) → if no key, skip silently with `"no_key"` outcome (no HTTP, no backoff advance — next tick re-evaluates so operator setting the key mid-run is picked up immediately) → call fetcher → on `BalanceFetchError` bump `consecutive_failures` and set `next_attempt_at = now + min(base × 2^failures, 3600s)`, cache untouched (prior snapshot keeps serving, upcoming endpoint surfaces `stale_since`) → on `None` (401/403) same backoff treatment, cache untouched (operator may rotate the key; don't stamp over with auth_failed envelope) → on unexpected exception same fetch_error path (defensive — one bad vendor schema cannot kill the loop) → on success `SharedKV("provider_balance").set(provider, json.dumps(BalanceInfo))` and reset backoff to `now + base`. Boot policy: one immediate `refresh_once` call before the first sleep so dashboards are not blank for the first 10 min post-startup; initial-tick exceptions logged+swallowed so a bad-key misconfig doesn't crash lifespan. Backoff formula locked: delays after N failures = [1200, 2400, 3600 (cap), 3600, ...] seconds — verified in `TestProviderBackoff::test_record_failure_doubles_then_caps`. **Module-global audit (SOP Step 1)**: `_LOOP_RUNNING` (rubric #1 "每 worker 從同樣來源推導" — flag presence identical per worker). Per-provider backoff state is **not** module-global on purpose — held in the `state` dict owned by `run_refresh_loop`'s stack frame (rubric #3 "故意每 worker 獨立"); in N-worker deployments each worker fetches independently and `SharedKV.set` is the cross-worker coordination point (last-writer-wins is fine because all writers produce the same logical datum from the same vendor). Vendor API traffic scales linearly with worker count (~N×12 req/hour/provider at 2-worker × 2-provider config — well under every documented rate limit). **Read-after-write audit**: only write is `SharedKV.set` which composes atomically (Redis HSET atomic; in-memory fallback uses `threading.Lock`). **Tests**: `backend/tests/test_llm_balance_refresher.py` 29 cases (9 classes) — happy path writes SharedKV with correct envelope + backoff resets to base on success + JSON round-trippable for upcoming endpoint + missing key skips silently without fetch or backoff + empty string treated as missing + fetch error does NOT overwrite prior cache + fetch error bumps next_attempt to base×2 + repeated failures double delay [1200, 2400, 3600, 3600] with cap + non-BalanceFetchError exception also caught + auth-fail does not overwrite cache + auth-fail applies same exponential backoff + within-backoff-window skips fetch (no resolver/fetcher call) + past-backoff-window retries + providers independent (one fails other succeeds) + each provider resolves own key + `_ProviderBackoff.reset` clears counters + `record_failure` cap math exact + serialiser preserves None numeric + serialiser degrades non-JSON via `default=str` + resolver reads settings scalar + empty/whitespace/unknown-provider → None + `run_refresh_loop` exits cleanly on cancel with `_LOOP_RUNNING=False` + second-start singleton guard short-circuits + initial tick runs before first sleep + initial-tick exception does not crash loop + default fetcher set is SUPPORTED_BALANCE_PROVIDERS (regression guard against silently dropping a provider). All 29 pass; pre-existing 46 `test_llm_balance_fetchers.py` cases still green (75 combined). Pre-commit fingerprint grep clean. `backend.main` imports clean with the new lifespan wiring. Remaining Z.2 checkboxes (endpoints + boundary contract + integration tests) not delivered by this commit. -->
  <!-- Production status: dev-only — lifespan task is wired but no production deploy verification yet. Next gate: deployed-inactive — waits on Z.2 checkbox 3 (`/runtime/providers/{provider}/balance` endpoint) to surface the refresher's output to the dashboard + a live restart of the backend image with Settings scalars populated for at least one supported provider. -->

- [x] 新 `GET /runtime/providers/{provider}/balance` endpoint，回快取值（優先 SharedKV）；SharedKV 空 → 主動觸發一次 fetch。不支援 provider 回 `{status: "unsupported", reason: "provider does not expose a public balance API with API-key authentication"}`.
  <!-- 2026-04-24 Z.2-checkbox-3 shipped: `backend/routers/llm_balance.py` exports `resolve_balance(provider, *, kv=None, fetchers=None, key_resolver=None)` (pure async service-layer function — all side-effect entrypoints injectable for unit tests) + `get_provider_balance(provider)` (thin FastAPI route wrapper that rejects unknown provider names with HTTP 400 before dispatching). Router: `APIRouter(prefix="/runtime/providers", tags=["runtime-providers"], dependencies=[Depends(_auth.require_admin)])` — balance data reveals billing, so matches the `require_admin` gate used by `backend/routers/llm_credentials.py` rather than the dashboard's `current_user` baseline. Wired into `backend/main.py:916` under `settings.api_prefix` → `/api/v1/runtime/providers/{provider}/balance`. Envelope contract: three top-level shapes keyed on `status` — `ok` carries `{status, provider, currency, balance_remaining, granted_total, usage_total, last_refreshed_at, source: "cache"|"live", raw}` (exact 9-key set locked by `TestEnvelopeShape::test_ok_envelope_keys`); `unsupported` carries `{status, provider, reason}` with the Z.2-spec verbatim reason string "provider does not expose a public balance API with API-key authentication"; `error` carries `{status, provider, message}`. Service flow: (1) `is_balance_supported` gate → early-return unsupported envelope, **no** key resolution, **no** fetch (efficiency + "unsupported" doesn't need a Settings scalar read for providers like ollama/together/anthropic); (2) `SharedKV("provider_balance").get(provider)` → JSON-decode with graceful-fallback-to-live on malformed/non-dict payloads (defence-in-depth so a single bad refresher write cannot permanently 500 the dashboard); (3) cache-hit → `source="cache"` ok envelope, key-resolver/fetcher NOT invoked (hot-path optimisation verified by `TestCacheHit::test_cache_hit_key_resolver_not_invoked`); (4) cache-miss → `_resolve_api_key(provider)` → if None/empty, `{status: "error", message: "no API key configured for this provider"}` with **no** cache write (next refresher tick picks up when operator sets the key); (5) fetcher-call → `BalanceFetchError` → `{status: "error", message: "fetch failed: {reason}"}`, auth-fail `None` → `{status: "error", message: "authentication failed — key may be missing or revoked"}`, unexpected exception (e.g. `RuntimeError`) caught + surfaces as `{status: "error", message: "unexpected error: {type}"}` — all three error paths keep the cache untouched per the "operator may rotate the key" rule documented in the fetcher / refresher layers; (6) fetcher success → `SharedKV.set(provider, _serialise_balance(info))` (atomic HSET / in-memory `threading.Lock`) + `source="live"` ok envelope; SharedKV write failure is logged but does not fail the request (operator sees the live value, next refresher tick retries). **Router-level 400**: path-param outside `_VALID_PROVIDER_NAMES = {anthropic, google, openai, xai, groq, deepseek, together, openrouter, ollama}` raises HTTP 400 "Unknown provider {...}" rather than silently emitting the `unsupported` envelope — caller-error must be loud. Note: "unsupported" stays the envelope for keyed-but-no-balance-API providers (anthropic/openai/google/xai/groq/together/ollama); "400" is reserved for typo'd path params outside the service registry entirely. Re-uses `_resolve_api_key` + `_serialise_balance` + `BALANCE_NAMESPACE` from `backend.llm_balance_refresher` so cache-key format + Settings-scalar resolution stay in lock-step with the writer; avoids a parallel implementation that could drift. **Module-global audit (SOP Step 1)**: zero new module-globals in the router — `resolve_balance` takes kv/fetchers/key_resolver as kwargs, production defaults resolve lazily to `SharedKV(BALANCE_NAMESPACE)` + `SUPPORTED_BALANCE_PROVIDERS` + `_resolve_api_key`. Cross-worker coordination: the cache lives in SharedKV (qualified answer #2 "via Redis"); the on-demand fetch path is qualified answer #3 ("intentionally per-worker") — in worst case N workers all get a cache miss at the same moment on cold-start and each issue one vendor request, which at 2-worker × 2-provider is ~2 req/worker/cold-start = 4 total req, well under every documented rate limit. **Read-after-write audit**: the only writer site is `kv.set(provider, _serialise_balance(info))`; SharedKV.set is atomic so concurrent readers see either the old or the new snapshot, never torn. **Tests**: `backend/tests/test_llm_balance_endpoint.py` 27 cases (7 classes) — `TestUnsupportedProvider`: 7 unsupported providers parametrised (anthropic/openai/google/xai/groq/together/ollama) all return verbatim-reason envelope without touching fetcher, plus unsupported-no-cache-write; `TestCacheHit`: cached BalanceInfo deserialises + 9-key ok envelope + `source=cache` + fetcher-not-called, plus key_resolver-not-called efficiency lock; `TestCacheMissLiveFetch`: exactly-one-fetch with resolved key + SharedKV populated after + second request hits cache (fetcher call counter stays at 1); `TestCacheMissNoKey`: `None` + empty-string both map to "no API key configured" without fetch or cache write; `TestCacheMissFetcherFailure`: auth-fail/BalanceFetchError/unexpected `RuntimeError` all → `error` envelope with no cache write (explicit `kv.get == ""` assertion per branch); `TestMalformedCachePayload`: non-JSON string + JSON-but-non-dict both fall through to live fetch (defence-in-depth); `TestRouterSurface`: unknown-provider → HTTP 400, router prefix + route path shape locked, `require_admin` dependency present in `router.dependencies`, `/api/v1/runtime/providers/{provider}/balance` registered on `backend.main.app.routes`; `TestEnvelopeShape`: exact key-set lock for each of ok/unsupported/error. All 27 pass; pre-existing 75 balance tests still green (102 combined). Pre-commit fingerprint grep clean. `backend.main` imports clean with the new router wiring. Remaining Z.2 checkboxes (batch endpoint + boundary `stale_since`/error-no-cache contract + integration tests) not delivered by this commit. -->
  <!-- Production status: dev-only — endpoint is callable but no production deploy verification yet. Next gate: deployed-inactive — waits on Z.2 checkbox 4 (batch endpoint) + checkbox 5 (boundary `stale_since` on 5xx) to complete the dashboard contract, plus a live restart of the backend image to surface the router at `/api/v1/runtime/providers/*/balance`. -->
- [x] 新 `GET /runtime/providers/balance` batch endpoint，一次回全部 provider 的 balance summary（支援 / 不支援都列出，前端一次渲染）。
  <!-- 2026-04-24 Z.2-checkbox-4 shipped: `backend/routers/llm_balance.py` now exports `resolve_all_balances(*, kv=None, fetchers=None, key_resolver=None)` (pure async service-layer function that delegates per-provider to existing `resolve_balance`) + `get_providers_balance_batch()` (HTTP route handler). Registered on the existing `APIRouter(prefix="/runtime/providers", dependencies=[Depends(_auth.require_admin)])` as `GET /balance` → wires to `/api/v1/runtime/providers/balance`. Registered in source order *before* `GET /{provider}/balance`; segment-count differs (0 vs 2 after prefix) so FastAPI disambiguates structurally regardless of order, but source-order reflects general-before-specific per style. No path-param conflict. Response envelope: `{"providers": [<envelope>, <envelope>, ...]}` — each inner envelope is exactly what `GET /{provider}/balance` would return for that provider (`ok` / `unsupported` / `error` — same 9/3/3 key-sets, NO extra wrapper keys added to per-provider envelopes so frontend can share a single `ProviderBalance` render component). Ordering deterministic: `sorted(_VALID_PROVIDER_NAMES)` → `[anthropic, deepseek, google, groq, ollama, openai, openrouter, together, xai]` so dashboard renders rows in stable order without client-side sort. All 9 providers surface regardless of support/key/cache state (both supported and unsupported listed per Z.2 spec "前端一次渲染"). Per-provider calls run concurrently via `asyncio.gather(*[resolve_balance(p, ...) for p in names], return_exceptions=True)` — independent vendors + independent `SharedKV` slots make this safe; warm-cache path issues zero HTTP calls (all 9 hit cache-hit short-circuit); cold-start worst case issues 2 parallel HTTP calls (deepseek + openrouter) because the other 7 exit at the `is_balance_supported` gate before any fetch attempt. **Single `SharedKV` handle** threaded through all 9 per-provider calls (avoids 9× redundant `SharedKV(BALANCE_NAMESPACE)` constructions per request). **Defence-in-depth exception isolation**: `return_exceptions=True` + a post-gather `isinstance(result, BaseException)` check converts any hypothetical abstraction-leak past `resolve_balance`'s inner except blocks to an `error` envelope for that single provider (`"unexpected error: <ClassName>"`) while letting the other 8 through — so one flaky vendor can never 500 the whole batch. **Module-global audit (SOP Step 1)**: zero new module-globals. `resolve_all_balances` takes all side-effect entrypoints as kwargs for unit tests (matches the checkbox-3 service-layer pattern); production default resolves to `SharedKV(BALANCE_NAMESPACE)` + `SUPPORTED_BALANCE_PROVIDERS` + `_resolve_api_key`. `_VALID_PROVIDER_NAMES` (frozenset, module-const) is the only read — qualified answer #1 (each worker derives the same value). Cross-worker coordination stays identical to checkbox-3: the cache lives in SharedKV (qualified answer #2 "via Redis"); on-demand fetch path is qualified answer #3 ("intentionally per-worker") — at N workers the worst-case cold-start issues N×2 = 4 requests at 2-worker × 2-supported config, well under every documented rate limit. **Read-after-write audit**: writer sites unchanged (still `_store.set(provider, _serialise_balance(info))` inside `resolve_balance`); `asyncio.gather` runs 2 potential writes concurrently for different SharedKV slots (no collision — different keys) and at most 1 write per key per request (no self-concurrency). **Auth gate**: batch route inherits `require_admin` from the router-level `dependencies` parameter (no per-route override needed) — balances reveal billing, so `require_admin` stays the correct gate, not `current_user`. **Tests**: `backend/tests/test_llm_balance_endpoint.py` +14 new cases (3 new classes appended after existing 27) — `TestBatchEndpoint` (8 cases): all-9-providers-covered-exactly-once + alphabetical-ordering-lock + mixed-supported-unsupported-in-single-payload (deepseek cache-hit + openrouter live-fetch + 7 unsupported) + supported-no-key-returns-error-while-unsupported-stays-unsupported + partial-failure-isolation (one fetcher raises, other succeeds) + shared-single-KV-handle (both writes land on the same KV instance readable after batch) + second-call-is-all-cache (warm-up then verify call counters don't increment on second round) + unexpected-resolve_balance-exception-isolated (monkeypatches `resolve_balance` to raise for one provider, verifies `return_exceptions=True` converts to error envelope without collapsing batch); `TestBatchRouterSurface` (4 cases): batch route registered on router + on app at `/api/v1/runtime/providers/balance` + coexists with single route (no shadow) + inherits `require_admin`; `TestBatchEnvelopeShape` (2 cases): top-level `{providers: [...]}` shape + each inner envelope matches the ok/unsupported/error key-sets from the single endpoint. All 41 endpoint tests pass; 116 combined Z.2 balance tests still green (46 fetchers + 29 refresher + 41 endpoint). Pre-commit fingerprint grep clean on both router + test files. `backend.main` imports clean with existing router wiring (no changes to main.py — batch route registers via the already-included router). Remaining Z.2 checkboxes (boundary `stale_since` on 5xx + integration tests with mocked DeepSeek/OpenRouter HTTP) not delivered by this commit. -->
  <!-- Production status: dev-only — batch endpoint is callable but no production deploy verification yet. Next gate: deployed-inactive — waits on Z.2 checkbox 5 (boundary `stale_since` contract) + live restart of backend image for dashboard consumers to surface `/api/v1/runtime/providers/balance` under `settings.api_prefix`. -->

- [x] 邊界：API key 格式錯或被 provider 作廢 → 回 `{status: "error", message: "..."}` 不 cache（下次正常 key 要能立刻 pick up）；DeepSeek / OpenRouter API 本身 5xx → 回快取值並標 `stale_since`。
  <!-- 2026-04-24 Z.2-checkbox-5 shipped: boundary contract implemented across `backend/llm_balance_refresher.py` + `backend/routers/llm_balance.py`. New SharedKV namespace `STALE_NAMESPACE = "provider_balance_stale"` (distinct from the cache namespace `"provider_balance"`) holds per-provider `"<epoch_float>"` timestamps — written on the fetcher's 5xx / transport / malformed / unexpected-exception paths (BalanceFetchError + bare `Exception` catch), cleared on successful fetch, **never** touched on the auth-fail (`None`) path or the `no_key` path. New helpers in `llm_balance_refresher.py`: `_stale_kv()` → `SharedKV(STALE_NAMESPACE)`, `_write_stale_marker(stale_kv, provider, now)` → `stale_kv.set(provider, f"{now:.6f}")`, `_clear_stale_marker(stale_kv, provider)` → `stale_kv.delete(provider)`, `_read_stale_marker(stale_kv, provider)` → `float | None` with self-healing delete on unparseable entries (so a corrupted slot cannot permanently mask a provider behind a "stale" render that never clears). `refresh_once` now accepts `stale_kv` kwarg (defaults to `_stale_kv()`) and wires the four paths per the contract above — defensive `try/except Exception` around every stale SharedKV call so a flaky Redis here cannot cascade into an uncaught exception that skips the backoff reset or the cache write. `resolve_balance` in the router mirrors the same contract on the cache-miss branch: 5xx writes marker, auth-fail leaves it untouched, success clears it; on cache-hit the marker is read and surfaced via `stale_since` in the envelope. Auth-fail path also preserves the existing cache per Z.2 spec "下次正常 key 要能立刻 pick up" — operator rotating a key does not invalidate prior good snapshots, so the next successful refresh lands cleanly without a gap. `_ok_envelope` gained a `stale_since: float | None` field (always emitted for shape stability, `None` when fresh, epoch timestamp when marker is set). Cache-hit short-circuits the fetcher even when `stale_since` is present — the refresher owns the retry cadence, and endpoint-side re-fetch would both hammer the vendor and race the backoff state. `resolve_all_balances` threads a single `stale_kv` handle through all 9 per-provider calls (same pattern as the cache handle from checkbox 4) so a marker set by one provider's fetcher-raise path is visible to a concurrent cache-hit read in the same batch round; envelope ordering + shape unchanged (`ok` envelopes grow `stale_since`; `unsupported` and `error` shapes stay at 3 keys). **Module-global audit (SOP Step 1)**: no new module-globals — the only additions are `STALE_NAMESPACE` (module-const string, rubric #1 "每 worker 從同樣來源推導") + the four pure helper functions that take `stale_kv` as an argument. Cross-worker coordination: the stale marker lives in SharedKV just like the cache (qualified answer #2 "via Redis") so marker writes from worker A are read by worker B on the next cache-hit; in-memory fallback is per-worker and matches the cache's own fallback semantics so single-worker dev behaves identically. **Read-after-write audit**: three write sites (`_stale.set` via `_write_stale_marker`, `_stale.delete` via `_clear_stale_marker`, cache `_store.set`). SharedKV.set / .delete are atomic (Redis HSET/HDEL atomic, in-memory fallback uses `threading.Lock`). The endpoint does NOT read-its-own-writes within one request — cache-hit path reads marker first then returns, cache-miss path writes marker on failure then returns; there is no sequence where our write has to be visible to our read in the same call. **Tests**: 25 new cases across the two suites. `backend/tests/test_llm_balance_endpoint.py` +13 cases (5 new classes appended): `TestBoundaryStaleSinceOnCacheHit` (4) — cache hit without marker → `stale_since=None`; cache hit with marker → `stale_since=<epoch>` AND cached balance still served (locks the "回快取值並標 stale_since" half of the spec verbatim); unparseable marker self-heals; cache-hit short-circuits fetcher even with marker; `TestBoundaryStaleMarkerLifecycle` (3) — live fetch success clears prior marker; live fetch 5xx writes marker at `now`; live fetch unexpected exception also writes marker (defence-in-depth); `TestBoundaryAuthFailDoesNotMarkStale` (3) — auth-fail must NOT write marker; prior marker survives auth-fail; auth-fail on one provider does not touch another provider's cache slot; `TestBoundaryNoKeyPath` (1) — no-key branch does not write marker; `TestBoundaryBatchAggregation` (2) — batch endpoint propagates per-provider `stale_since` to cache envelopes with distinct/fresh per-provider markers; unsupported envelopes have no `stale_since` key (only `ok` shape carries it). Envelope shape tests updated: `test_ok_envelope_keys` now locks the 10-key set (+stale_since); `test_batch_each_envelope_matches_single_endpoint_shape` same. `backend/tests/test_llm_balance_refresher.py` +13 cases (2 new classes appended before the existing `TestRegistryIntegration`): `TestRefreshOnceStaleMarker` (8) — fetch_error writes marker at now; unexpected exception writes marker; success clears prior marker; auth-fail does not touch existing marker; auth-fail does not synthesise marker where none existed; no-key does not touch marker; backoff-gated skip does not touch marker; multi-provider markers independent (one 5xx's marker does not bleed to the other's slot); `TestStaleMarkerHelpers` (5) — round-trip write+read; read-empty returns None; clear removes entry; unparseable self-heals; `STALE_NAMESPACE != BALANCE_NAMESPACE` (regression guard against someone collapsing the two namespaces back to one). Totals: fetchers 46 + refresher 42 + endpoint 54 = 142 all green (pre-checkbox: 46+29+41=116 → +26 new cases). `backend.main` imports clean, pre-commit fingerprint grep (`_conn()`/`await conn.commit()`/`datetime('now')`/`VALUES (?, ?, ...)`) clean on both router + refresher + both test files. Next Z.2 checkbox (integration tests with mocked DeepSeek/OpenRouter HTTP responses) remains `[ ]` and is not delivered by this commit. -->
  <!-- Production status: dev-only — boundary contract is code-complete + unit-tested but no production deploy verification yet. Next gate: deployed-inactive — waits on Z.2 checkbox 6 (integration test of HTTP mocks) + live restart of backend image so the stale marker namespace actually gets written on a real 5xx in production Redis. -->

- [x] 測試：mock DeepSeek + OpenRouter API response（valid balance / auth fail / 5xx），驗 SharedKV 更新、batch endpoint 聚合、unsupported provider 行為。
  <!-- 2026-04-24 Z.2-checkbox-6 shipped: `backend/tests/test_llm_balance_integration.py` — 36 new integration cases across 6 classes driving the full stack (mocked vendor HTTP via `respx` → **real** `fetch_balance_deepseek` / `fetch_balance_openrouter` → `refresh_once` → `SharedKV` → `resolve_balance` / `resolve_all_balances`). Deliberate distinction from the three pre-existing unit files: `test_llm_balance_fetchers.py` / `test_llm_balance_refresher.py` / `test_llm_balance_endpoint.py` each lock one layer in isolation and the refresher/endpoint suites drive **injected fake fetchers**, so they never exercise the real fetcher ↔ refresher ↔ SharedKV ↔ endpoint wiring end-to-end; this file does, with respx intercepting only at the transport layer so the BalanceInfo shape and serialise/deserialise format round-trip through real code on both sides. **Classes + cases**: `TestIntegrationValidBalance` (4) — 200 response → refresher populates SharedKV for both providers with the full BalanceInfo envelope (currency/balance_remaining/granted_total/usage_total/None-for-deepseek), endpoint subsequent cache-hit renders `source="cache"` + `stale_since=None`, endpoint cache-miss → live HTTP fetch hit + SharedKV populated, backoff reset after success (consecutive_failures → 0, next_attempt_at pushed by base_interval). `TestIntegrationAuthFail` (6) — parametrised 401/403 for each of deepseek + openrouter → refresher outcome `"auth_fail"`, SharedKV cache **empty** (no stale auth_failed envelope), stale marker **untouched** (auth-fail is operator-side not provider-down), backoff still advances; auth-fail preserves prior cached snapshot (下次正常 key 要能立刻 pick up 契約); endpoint cache-miss + 403 surfaces the spec-verbatim "authentication failed — key may be missing or revoked" message with cache + stale marker both clean. `TestIntegration5xx` (10) — parametrised 500/502/503/504 for each of deepseek + openrouter → refresher outcome `"fetch_error"`, cache untouched, stale marker written at `now`; canonical lifecycle test (tick-1 success at now=1000 → tick-2 503 at now=2000 with clock advanced past backoff window) locks cache-preserved + stale_since surfaces on subsequent endpoint cache-hit at `now=2000.0`; endpoint cache-miss + 502 writes marker at `now` even though nothing to serve (concurrent-worker coherence). `TestIntegrationBatchAggregation` (5) — `resolve_all_balances` under real HTTP: mixed-success aggregates 2 ok + 7 unsupported (alphabetical order locked), mixed 200/500 aggregates 1 ok + 1 error + 7 unsupported (only failing provider gets stale marker + only succeeding provider gets cache write), both-supported-auth-fail surfaces 2 error + 7 unsupported with zero KV/stale writes, second-call-is-all-cache proves respx call_count stays at 1 after warm-up, cache-hit-with-stale-marker propagates `stale_since` through batch to the deepseek envelope while openrouter stays fresh. `TestIntegrationUnsupportedProvider` (9) — 7 unsupported providers parametrised (anthropic/google/groq/ollama/openai/together/xai) → `unsupported` envelope with spec-verbatim reason, no HTTP attempted (respx would reject unregistered routes), no cache/stale touch, resolver never consulted (short-circuit before key resolution); refresher default iteration is supported-only (anthropic/etc. never appear in outcomes even when resolver returns `sk-{p}` for all); batch-with-unsupported-entry-in-fetchers-dict proves `is_balance_supported` gate fires before fetcher dispatch (anthropic's injected fetcher never called despite being present in fetchers kwarg). `TestIntegrationRefresherToEndpoint` (2) — cross-layer regression guard: refresher write is round-trip readable by endpoint (full 10-key envelope exact match including `raw` vendor body), concatenated lifecycle (refresher success at t=1000 → refresher 503 at t=5000 → endpoint reads cache with stale_since=5000) anchors the whole Z.2 story. **Module-global audit (SOP Step 1)**: zero shared state between tests — respx.mock context per-case, SharedKV namespaces via `uuid.uuid4` so in-memory fallback dicts cannot cross-pollute even without Redis; refresher `_LOOP_RUNNING` never touched (tests drive `refresh_once` directly, not `run_refresh_loop`); every test passes own `kv`/`stale_kv`/`key_resolver` kwargs and lets `fetchers=None` so the real `SUPPORTED_BALANCE_PROVIDERS` fetcher code path is exercised (this is the key distinction from unit siblings). **Read-after-write audit**: two write sites (`_store.set` on success, `_stale.set` on 5xx); both atomic (Redis HSET / in-memory `threading.Lock`); within-test write→read happens in same coroutine so ordering is trivial, not timing-sensitive. **Test results**: 36/36 pass (0.96s). Combined with pre-existing balance tests: 46 fetchers + 42 refresher + 54 endpoint + 36 integration = **178/178 pass** (3.40s, 0 regression). Pre-commit fingerprint grep (`_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]`) clean on the new file. No source-code changes — this commit is test-only (+ TODO.md status flip + HANDOFF.md entry). Z.2 (#291) all 6 checkboxes now shipped. -->

Next gate: deployed-inactive — waits on a live restart of the backend image so the lifespan refresh loop writes to production Redis against real DeepSeek/OpenRouter keys in Settings; this Z.2 row no longer gates that (all code paths now covered), so deployment can proceed whenever the backend image is next rebuilt. -->

預估：**1 day**

### Z.3 Price table 外置化（YAML + hot-reload） (#292)

現在硬寫 8 個 model 的 in/out rate 在 Python code 裡，改 rate 要改 code 重部署。拆成 YAML + `/reload` endpoint 就可以即時跟 provider 調價。

- [x] 新 `config/llm_pricing.yaml`：覆蓋 9 家主要 model（each `(input_per_mtok, output_per_mtok)` USD），結構如：
  ```yaml
  providers:
    anthropic:
      claude-opus-4: {input: 15, output: 75}
      claude-sonnet-4: {input: 3, output: 15}
    deepseek:
      deepseek-chat: {input: 0.14, output: 0.28}
    openrouter:
      "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4": {input: 3, output: 15}  # aggregator prefix
  defaults: {input: 1, output: 3}
  metadata:
    updated_at: 2026-04-22
    source: <https://...>
  ```
  <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.3-checkbox-1 shipped: new `config/llm_pricing.yaml` (~95 lines). Covers all 9 providers surfaced by `_VALID_PROVIDER_NAMES` from Z.2 (anthropic / openai / google / xai / groq / deepseek / together / openrouter / ollama) with 2–4 representative models each plus a per-provider `_default` fallback tier, a global `defaults: {input: 1, output: 3}` (deliberately higher than the cheapest real provider so unknown-model billing is visible in dashboards rather than silently charged near-free), and `metadata` (`updated_at: 2026-04-25` / `source: <issue #292>` / `schema_version: 1` / multi-line `notes`). **Scope discipline**: file-only delivery — no code changes to `backend/routers/system.py` (the `_PRICING` dict stays authoritative until checkbox 2 wires `backend/pricing.py::get_pricing`), no new Python module, no reload endpoint, no tests; those are later Z.3 checkboxes and would expand blast radius beyond a single row. **Price-neutral migration contract**: all 8 models currently hard-coded in `backend/routers/system.py:1094-1103` (`claude-opus-4-7` / `claude-opus-4-20250514` / `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` / `gpt-4o` / `gemini-1.5-pro` / `grok-3-mini` / `llama-3.3-70b-versatile` / `deepseek-chat`) carry values **bit-identical** to the hard-coded dict, verified by cross-reference in the commit; this honours Z.3 checkbox 7 up-front — "既有 token usage 紀錄不重算成本，保留當時計費，只影響未來計價" — and means the later cutover can't accidentally shift "future" billing either at the exact moment of migration. New models added beyond those 8 (claude-haiku-4-5 / gpt-5 / gpt-5-mini / gpt-4o-mini / gemini-2.5-pro / gemini-2.5-flash / grok-4 / llama-3.1-8b-instant / deepseek-reasoner / 2 together-ai models / 4 openrouter aggregator entries / 3 ollama local models) draw from each vendor's public pricing page as of 2026-04-22; they are not currently billed by `track_tokens` (which only looks up exact-match in `_PRICING`) so adding them here has zero effect until checkbox 2 wires the lookup. **Relationship to `configs/provider_pricing.yaml`**: the pre-existing file is blended per-tier (premium/default/budget) used by `backend/forecast.py` for dollar-estimate forecasting; this file is exact per-model (input, output) for accurate post-hoc billing. The two files are intentionally **not** merged — merging would force either forecasts to lose tier granularity or billing to lose input/output split; header comment in `config/llm_pricing.yaml` documents the distinction so future contributors don't collapse them. **YAML contract tested by `python3 -c "import yaml; …"`**: parses cleanly, 9 providers, every model has numeric `input` + `output`, no missing keys. **Pre-commit fingerprint grep** `_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]` → 0 hits (YAML file, no SQL surface). **Module-global audit**: no Python code touched, no new module-globals, no cross-worker coherence concerns this commit; when checkbox 2 lands `backend/pricing.py` the load+cache path will need its own rubric #1 / rubric #2 answer (each worker loads same file at boot = rubric #1; reload fan-out = rubric #2 via SharedKV version counter). Directory `config/` is new (singular) and sits next to existing `configs/` (plural, unrelated) — this intentional per the Z.3 spec text `新 config/llm_pricing.yaml`. Commit co-author includes env-user + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->
  <!-- Production status: dev-only — YAML is on disk but no code reads it yet. Next gate: deployed-inactive — waits on Z.3 checkbox 2 (`backend/pricing.py::get_pricing` + startup load) + checkbox 4 (`POST /runtime/pricing/reload`) to actually route `track_tokens` lookups through this file. Until then, `backend/routers/system.py::_PRICING` remains the authoritative billing source and this file is unreferenced config. -->
- [x] 改 `backend/routers/system.py:1091-1100` 硬寫 dict，改成 `backend/pricing.py::get_pricing(provider, model) -> tuple[float, float]`，啟動時 load YAML、hot-reload via `/runtime/pricing/reload`. <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.3-checkbox-2 shipped: new module `backend/pricing.py` (~210 LOC) wires the YAML at `config/llm_pricing.yaml` (the singular `config/` dir from checkbox 1) into `backend/routers/system.py::track_tokens`. **Public API**: `get_pricing(provider: str | None, model: str) -> tuple[float, float]` — never raises, always returns floats. Lookup chain: (1) `providers[provider][model]` exact, (2) provider known + model unknown → `providers[provider]._default`, (3) provider unknown / `provider=None` with no scan hit → global `defaults` from YAML, (4) YAML missing/corrupt → `_HARD_CODED_FALLBACK` per-model dict (bit-identical to the pre-Z.3 dict that lived at system.py:1094-1103) → `_HARD_CODED_GLOBAL_DEFAULT = (1.0, 3.0)`. **Provider auto-detect**: `track_tokens(model, ...)` callers (LLM callback at backend/agents/llm.py:675, tests in backend/tests/test_token_budget.py) only know the model id, so passing `provider=None` triggers a first-hit-wins scan across all provider tables — preserves the legacy single-arg call shape. **Wiring at system.py**: replaced the 9-line `_PRICING = {...}` dict with `from backend.pricing import get_pricing as _get_pricing`; the `_PRICING.get(model, (1.0, 3.0))` call inside `track_tokens` becomes `_get_pricing(None, model)`. No call-site signature change → backend/agents/llm.py + tests untouched. **Price-neutral verified end-to-end**: smoke script exercised `track_tokens('claude-opus-4-7', 1_000_000, 1_000_000, 100)` → cost = `$30.00` (5 + 25), bit-identical to pre-Z.3; `track_tokens('totally-unknown-model', 1M, 1M, 50)` → `$4.00` matching the (1.0, 3.0) historical fallback. All 8 pre-Z.3 hard-coded models cross-checked through `get_pricing(None, ...)` → tuples bit-identical to the EXPECT dict harvested from the original lines 1094-1103. **Hot-reload primitive**: `reload()` clears the local-process cache and returns `{loaded_from_yaml, providers, metadata}` for the future `POST /runtime/pricing/reload` endpoint (Z.3 checkbox 4) to call before broadcasting via SharedKV. **Side-API**: `get_metadata()` returns the YAML's metadata block (updated_at / source / schema_version / notes) so checkbox 5's `GET /runtime/pricing` can render it; `reset_cache_for_tests()` mirrors `backend/context_limits.py`'s test hook. **Module-global state audit (per SOP Step 1)**: `_PRICING_CACHE` is module-level dict but each uvicorn worker derives identical state from the same on-disk YAML at first call → matches answer 1 of the three valid cross-worker-coherence answers ("derived from a shared static source"); the YAML is bundled in the production image, read-only at runtime; `reload()` only clears local-process cache (cross-worker fan-out is checkbox 4's domain — explicit scope split documented in pricing.py docstring). **Boot resilience**: tested missing-file path (`_PRICING_PATH.exists() is False` → cache populated with hard-coded fallback + global default + `_loaded_from_yaml=False`); a corrupt YAML (`yaml.safe_load` raises) is caught broadly, logged at WARNING, falls through to the same path. Z.3 checkbox 6 (the test row) will assert this contract; not exercised here to keep blast radius single-row. **Pre-existing test sweep**: `backend/tests/test_token_budget.py` 6/6 green after the wiring change — confirms `track_tokens` arithmetic, `track_tokens` budget-flip, and the cost-rounding all unchanged. **Pre-commit fingerprint grep** `_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]` on the new file: 0 hits. The single hit on `backend/routers/system.py:1444` is pre-existing prose in a comment about `alembic_pg_compat`, not introduced or touched by this commit. **Scope discipline — what this checkbox does NOT do** (deferred to its own row): no per-day-throttled fallback warning logging (checkbox 3); no `POST /runtime/pricing/reload` endpoint (checkbox 4); no SharedKV broadcast for cross-worker reload sync (checkbox 4); no `GET /runtime/pricing` (checkbox 5); no test row dedicated to YAML-corrupt boot resilience (checkbox 6); no migration row about historical token_usage cost preservation (checkbox 7 — already honoured implicitly by the price-neutral cutover). Each is a separate `- [ ]` row in Z.3 — going further would expand blast radius beyond a single checkbox. Commit co-authors include env-user + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->
  <!-- Production status: deployed-inactive — code shipped to main, YAML actively read by `track_tokens`, but the `/runtime/pricing/reload` endpoint that exposes hot-reload to operators is checkbox 4 (next). Until then, picking up a price change still requires a worker restart (which on uvicorn `--workers N` means a Caddy-level rolling restart). Next gate: deployed-active — needs Z.3 checkbox 4 (`POST /runtime/pricing/reload` + SharedKV broadcast) to flip; ETA single-checkbox commit, no maintenance window required since the underlying loader is already live. -->
- [x] 更新 fallback 策略：provider 知道但 model 不知道 → 用該 provider 的 `_default`；都不知道 → 用全域 `defaults`。每次 fallback 都 log warning（一次/天/provider，不要洗 log）。 <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.3-checkbox-3 shipped: `backend/pricing.py` gains throttled fallback warnings on the two fallback arms the Z.3 spec names — `provider known + model unknown → providers[<provider>]._default` and `both unknown → defaults`. The lookup chain itself was already wired by checkbox 2; this commit adds the observability layer. **Throttle mechanics**: new module-globals `_FALLBACK_WARN_TS: dict[str, float]` + `_FALLBACK_WARN_LOCK: threading.Lock()` + constant `_WARN_THROTTLE_SECONDS = 86400.0`; new private helper `_maybe_warn_fallback(throttle_key, fmt, *args)` does check-then-update under the lock and only calls `logger.warning` when ≥24h since the last emit for that key. **Key convention**: `{arm}:{provider_key}` — four distinct arms (`provider_default:<provider>` / `global_default:<provider_or_"<unknown>">` / `hardcoded_global:<provider_or_"<unknown>">`, plus implicit "no log" arms for exact + scan hits) so two different arms on the same provider each get their own clock, and two different providers on the same arm each get their own clock (both behaviours locked by `test_different_providers_log_independently` + `test_different_arms_same_provider_log_independently`). **What does NOT log**: exact `providers[<provider>][<model>]` hit and `provider=None` scan hit (scan-hit is successful disambiguation, not fallback) — locked by `test_exact_hit_does_not_warn` + `test_provider_none_scan_hit_does_not_warn`. **Warning payload** (examples caught by caplog): `llm_pricing fallback: provider='anthropic' known but model='claude-unknown-model' unknown; billing at providers[anthropic]._default=(3.0, 15.0) (throttled 1/day/provider per worker)` / `llm_pricing fallback: no provider/model match for provider=None model='totally-unknown-model'; billing at global defaults=(1.0, 3.0) (throttled 1/day/provider per worker)`. Operator sees exactly which provider + which model first tripped the arm + which rate table served the request, so dashboards can correlate "expensive unknown" cost spikes back to the YAML gap that needs filling. **Module-global state audit (per SOP Step 1, answer 3 "故意每 worker 獨立")**: `_FALLBACK_WARN_TS` is module-level and deliberately not coordinated across uvicorn workers. Rationale explicitly documented in the module docstring: at 2 workers × 2 replicas = 4 aggregate workers, the ceiling is 4 WARNING lines per day per (arm, provider) key — far below any reasonable "log spam" threshold, and a SharedKV/Redis round-trip on every price lookup would be wildly disproportionate to the observability gain. Rolling restarts reset per-worker cleanly, which is the desired behaviour (fresh deploy should re-announce which providers are tripping fallbacks). A `threading.Lock` guards the dict so concurrent async callers in the same worker can't race on check-then-update. **reset_cache_for_tests contract extended**: now also clears `_FALLBACK_WARN_TS` under the lock so every test gets a pristine "first emit" window — this keeps `test_pricing.py`'s autouse fixture honest regardless of what earlier tests in the same process already did. **Tests (new `backend/tests/test_pricing.py`, 25 cases, 4 TestCase classes)**: `TestFallbackStrategy` (6) locks the lookup chain itself — exact-hit / provider-default arm / global-default arm / `provider=None` scan-hit / `provider=None` miss / provider case-insensitive; `TestFallbackWarningEmission` (5) confirms each arm emits exactly one WARNING with the expected content and that exact-hit / scan-hit emit nothing; `TestFallbackWarningThrottle` (6) locks the 24h throttle semantics including cross-provider independence, cross-arm independence, and the monkeypatched `pricing.time.time` test that advances beyond `_WARN_THROTTLE_SECONDS` to prove re-emission works; `TestPriceNeutralityAcrossFallbacks` (8 parametrised, one per pre-Z.3 model) re-asserts the checkbox-2 bit-identical contract under the new warning machinery to catch regressions where the log path accidentally short-circuits the lookup. **Future checkbox 6 + Z.5 will extend this same `test_pricing.py` file** with YAML-corrupt boot / reload endpoint / cross-worker sync cases. 25/25 green on the new file in ~0.24s. **Regression sweep**: `test_token_budget.py` 6/6 + `test_forecast.py` 6/6 + `test_context_limits.py` 25/25 = 37/37 neighbour tests green after the pricing.py edit, confirming (a) the `track_tokens` arithmetic is untouched (same `get_pricing(None, model)` return values), (b) `forecast.py`'s `configs/provider_pricing.yaml` loader is completely separate and unaffected, (c) `context_limits.py`'s sibling `configs/context_window_limits.yaml` loader has no shared state with `backend/pricing.py`. **Pre-commit fingerprint grep** (`_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]`) on `backend/pricing.py` + `backend/tests/test_pricing.py`: 0 matches on both. **Scope discipline — what this checkbox does NOT do** (deferred): no `POST /runtime/pricing/reload` endpoint (checkbox 4); no SharedKV cross-worker fan-out for reload (checkbox 4); no `GET /runtime/pricing` (checkbox 5); no YAML-corrupt-boot dedicated test row (checkbox 6); no historical token_usage migration row (checkbox 7 — already honoured by checkbox 2's price-neutral cutover). Commit co-author includes env-user + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->
  <!-- Production status: deployed-inactive — code shipped to main, throttle is active inside every running worker as soon as the image is rolled, but no operator is yet looking at the WARNING lines because the ops runbook entry about "when you see llm_pricing fallback warnings in backend logs, edit config/llm_pricing.yaml and POST /runtime/pricing/reload" lives alongside the reload endpoint (Z.3 checkbox 4, next). The fallback arms themselves have been **live since checkbox 2** (the code was already returning provider._default and global defaults); this checkbox just makes those silent fallbacks visible. Next gate: deployed-active — needs Z.3 checkbox 4 (reload endpoint + SharedKV broadcast) so operators can respond to the warning without a rolling restart; ETA single-checkbox commit, no maintenance window. -->

- [x] 新 `POST /runtime/pricing/reload`（require_admin）：重新讀 YAML，SharedKV 廣播 reload 事件讓所有 worker 同步。 <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.3-checkbox-4 shipped: new endpoint `POST /api/v1/runtime/pricing/reload` in `backend/routers/system.py` (~36 LOC), gated by `_REQUIRE_ADMIN = [Depends(_auth.require_role("admin"))]` (the same shared dep list at system.py:63 used by `/runtime/deploy`, `/runtime/release`, `/runtime/forecast/recompute`, `/runtime/token-budget/reset` — uniform admin-write convention across the router). **Local + cross-worker reload contract**: the endpoint (a) calls `pricing.reload()` synchronously to clear THIS worker's `_PRICING_CACHE` and re-read `config/llm_pricing.yaml`, then (b) calls `publish_cross_worker(pricing.PRICING_RELOAD_EVENT, {"origin_worker": <pid>})` so every peer worker's `_on_pricing_reload_event` callback (registered at module-import time in `backend/pricing.py`, mirroring the `events.py:265-269` precedent against `shared_state._pubsub_callbacks`) clears its own cache. Each peer's next `get_pricing()` call lazily re-reads the same YAML — no synchronous fan-out RPC required, the loader is the broadcast amplifier. **Response body**: `{"status": "reloaded", "loaded_from_yaml": bool, "providers": [...sorted...], "metadata": {updated_at, source, schema_version, notes}, "broadcast": "redis_pubsub" | "local_only"}` — operator can inspect at a glance whether the YAML actually loaded (catches typos that produce empty providers + `loaded_from_yaml=False`), which providers landed, and whether the cross-worker fan-out succeeded (degraded "local_only" surfaces missing Redis without erroring). **Module-global state audit (per SOP Step 1)**: `_PRICING_CACHE` mutation now has a coordinated cross-worker invalidation path → matches answer 2 of the three valid audit answers ("Redis-coordinated"). Documented in `pricing.py` module docstring including the small reload-propagation window: worker A is reloaded, worker B has not yet processed the pubsub message (bounded by `pubsub.get_message(timeout=1.0)` in `shared_state.start_pubsub_listener`); a token-tracking call landing on B during that window bills at the OLD rates, which is acceptable per Z.3 checkbox 7 — "既有 token usage 紀錄不重算成本，保留當時計費，只影響未來計價" — both rates are valid "future" prices in those sub-second windows. **Read-after-write timing audit**: not relevant — the reload doesn't change a serialised operation to a parallel one, only invalidates a cache; existing `track_tokens()` callers don't read-after-write across workers in a single transaction. **Constant `pricing.PRICING_RELOAD_EVENT = "pricing_reload"`** is the wire-format string published over Redis pub/sub. Locked by a dedicated `test_event_constant_is_stable` because changing it during a rolling deploy would silently break already-deployed workers listening for the old name. **Origin filtering deliberately absent** in `_on_pricing_reload_event`: invalidating a stale cache costs ~one disk read on the next `get_pricing()` call, much cheaper than the bookkeeping required to suppress the originator's self-message. `events.py::_on_cross_worker_event` does filter, but it has different semantics (the SSE bus would deliver duplicates to local subscribers without filtering); pricing's reload is idempotent. **Tests (9 new in `backend/tests/test_pricing.py`, brings file from 25 → 34 cases)**: `TestCrossWorkerCallbackRegistration` (5) — callback is in `shared_state._pubsub_callbacks` at import time / event constant is the wire-format `"pricing_reload"` / callback clears `_PRICING_CACHE` when invoked / callback ignores unrelated events like `"sse"` (regression guard against accidentally widening the trigger) / callback tolerates malformed non-dict payloads. `TestReloadPricingEndpoint` (4) — endpoint returns 200 with the documented status shape + 9 providers from the live YAML; endpoint picks up YAML edits via `monkeypatch.setattr(pricing, "_PRICING_PATH", fake)` (reading 99/999 from the temp file post-reload locks the actual reload-from-disk semantic, not just cache invalidation); endpoint survives YAML missing at reload time (returns 200, `loaded_from_yaml: False`, falls back to hard-coded `claude-opus-4-7 = (5.0, 25.0)`); `test_endpoint_route_requires_admin` inspects the route's `dependencies` list and asserts both `current_user` (router-level baseline) and `_dep` (the closure produced by `require_role("admin")`) are wired — a route-definition lock that survives even if the test conftest's "open" auth-mode default would otherwise mask a missing gate. 34/34 green on `test_pricing.py` in ~3.0s. **Regression sweep**: 96/96 neighbour tests green across `test_token_budget.py` (6) + `test_forecast.py` (6) + `test_context_limits.py` (25) + `test_shared_state.py` (59 — confirms the new callback registration doesn't break the existing `register_cross_worker_callback` contract or the SSE callback in events.py). Pre-existing master failures in `test_event_system.py::TestReplayEndpoint` (3) and `test_release.py` (4) verified via `git stash && pytest && git stash pop` — both fail identically on the master baseline (db_pool init ordering + release fixture issues), unrelated to this checkbox. **Pre-commit fingerprint grep** (`_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]`) on `backend/pricing.py` + `backend/routers/system.py` + `backend/tests/test_pricing.py`: 0 hits introduced; the single grep hit at `backend/routers/system.py:1444` is pre-existing prose in the alembic_pg_compat comment, untouched by this commit. **Live smoke** (asyncio.run on `reload_pricing_table()` outside the ASGI stack): returns the documented dict with broadcast=`local_only` (no Redis in dev shell — exactly the documented degradation path), 9 providers, full metadata block. **Scope discipline — what this checkbox does NOT do** (deferred): no `GET /runtime/pricing` (checkbox 5 — the next row); no dedicated YAML-corrupt-boot test row (checkbox 6 — covered transitively by `test_endpoint_survives_yaml_missing_at_reload` here, but checkbox 6 will add a startup-path version + a live-Redis cross-worker integration test); no historical token_usage migration row (checkbox 7 — already honoured by checkbox 2's price-neutral cutover). Commit co-author includes env-user + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->
  <!-- Production status: deployed-inactive — endpoint code is on main, route registers at boot (`POST /api/v1/runtime/pricing/reload` with admin gate), the cross-worker callback is registered against shared_state at every worker's import time. Operator runbook entry "when you see llm_pricing fallback warnings in backend logs, edit config/llm_pricing.yaml and POST /runtime/pricing/reload" still needs to be added to docs/ops/ alongside the next live POST in production (the dev shell exercise above used asyncio.run, not an HTTPS round-trip through Caddy + Cloudflare). Z.3 checkbox 5 (`GET /runtime/pricing`) will land alongside the runbook entry so operators can verify the new table state after their POST. Next gate: deployed-active — operator does one live POST against `https://ai.sora-dev.app/api/v1/runtime/pricing/reload` after editing config/llm_pricing.yaml in production and confirms (a) HTTP 200 with the expected providers list, (b) backend logs show the cross-worker reload INFO line on the peer replica (`llm_pricing cross-worker reload received (origin=...)`), (c) a fresh `track_tokens` call after the reload bills at the new rate. ETA single operator POST + 5 min log-grep, no maintenance window required since the underlying loader is already live. -->

- [x] `GET /runtime/pricing` 回當前表 + `updated_at` + source URL。 <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.3-checkbox-5 shipped: new endpoint `GET /api/v1/runtime/pricing` in `backend/routers/system.py` (~17 LOC) backed by new helper `backend/pricing.py::get_pricing_table()` (~40 LOC). **Auth tier**: NO admin gate — pricing is non-sensitive informational data, matches peer GETs like `/runtime/info` and `/runtime/status`; the router-level `Depends(_auth.current_user)` baseline still applies so unauthenticated callers get 401. Locked by dedicated `test_endpoint_route_does_not_require_admin` that inspects the route's `dependencies` list and asserts `current_user` IS present and `_dep` (the closure produced by `require_role("admin")` in `_REQUIRE_ADMIN`) is NOT present — a route-definition lock that survives even if a future contributor accidentally adds `dependencies=_REQUIRE_ADMIN`. **Response shape** (4 top-level keys): `providers` map (per-provider, per-model `{input, output}` USD per 1M tokens, including `_default` rows so dashboards can render the provider-level fallback tier alongside exact rates) + `defaults` `{input, output}` global fallback + `metadata` block (`updated_at` + `source` URL + `schema_version` + `notes` round-tripped from the YAML, satisfying the spec's "回當前表 + `updated_at` + source URL" requirement) + `loaded_from_yaml: bool` so a dashboard can render a degraded-state banner instead of silently showing the boot-safety fallback. **Tuple → dict serialization**: rate pairs in the in-memory cache are tuples `(input, output)`; `get_pricing_table()` converts them to `{"input": X, "output": Y}` dicts to mirror the YAML on-disk shape and keep the JSON wire format self-documenting (a bare 2-element list would lose the in/out distinction — locked by `test_rate_pairs_serialize_as_input_output_dicts`). **Module-global state audit (per SOP Step 1)**: read-only endpoint hitting the existing `_PRICING_CACHE`. No new module-globals introduced — the cache + cross-worker reload coherence already audited and documented by checkboxes 2/4. Each worker derives identical state from the same on-disk YAML at first call (audit answer 1) with `POST /runtime/pricing/reload` providing Redis-coordinated invalidation (audit answer 2). The GET endpoint is observation-only so it cannot drift this. **Read-after-write timing audit**: not applicable — read-only endpoint, no parallel write being introduced. The small reload-propagation window already documented in checkbox 4 (worker B reads stale cache for ≤1s after worker A reloaded) means a GET landing on B during that window returns the OLD table — acceptable per the same Z.3 checkbox 7 rationale ("既有 token usage 紀錄不重算成本，保留當時計費，只影響未來計價"; both old and new are valid prices in those sub-second windows). The operator-facing implication: a GET-after-POST round-trip could see the old table if it lands on a peer worker that hasn't processed the pubsub message yet — this is documented in the get_pricing_table() docstring so future runbook authors know to expect a brief inconsistency window. **Tests (11 new in `backend/tests/test_pricing.py`, brings file from 34 → 45 cases)**: `TestPricingTableHelper` (7) — full provider map with 9 live providers / rate pairs serialise as `{input, output}` dicts not bare lists / `_default` provider rows surface in the snapshot / global `defaults` present / `metadata` carries `updated_at` + `source` URL substring lock / `loaded_from_yaml=True` under normal boot / `loaded_from_yaml=False` + empty providers when YAML missing (degraded state surfaces, doesn't silently render boot-safety fallback). `TestPricingSnapshotEndpoint` (4) — endpoint returns 200 with all 4 top-level keys + live YAML loaded + spot-check on anthropic Opus 4.7 rate; **GET-after-POST round-trip** monkeypatches `_PRICING_PATH` to a fake YAML, POSTs `/pricing/reload`, then GETs `/pricing` and asserts the new providers + new defaults + new `updated_at` + new `source` URL appear (locks the operator workflow "edit YAML → POST reload → GET to verify" end-to-end); endpoint surfaces degraded state with 200 + `loaded_from_yaml: False` + empty providers when YAML missing (so dashboard can render banner); `test_endpoint_route_does_not_require_admin` inspects the route's `dependencies` list and locks the negative assertion (no `_dep` admin gate). 45/45 green on `test_pricing.py` in ~3.7s. **Regression sweep**: 37/37 neighbour tests green across `test_token_budget.py` (6) + `test_forecast.py` (6) + `test_context_limits.py` (25), confirming (a) the new helper does not perturb `_PRICING_CACHE` lookup arithmetic so `track_tokens` still bills bit-identically, (b) `forecast.py`'s `configs/provider_pricing.yaml` loader is completely separate, (c) `context_limits.py`'s sibling YAML loader has no shared state. **Pre-commit fingerprint grep** (`_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]`) on `backend/pricing.py` + `backend/routers/system.py` + `backend/tests/test_pricing.py`: 0 hits introduced (pre-existing hit at `backend/routers/system.py:1444` is the alembic_pg_compat comment prose, untouched). **Live smoke** (asyncio.run on `get_pricing_snapshot()` outside the ASGI stack): returns the documented 4-key dict with all 9 providers, `anthropic.claude-opus-4-7 = {input: 5.0, output: 25.0}`, `defaults = {input: 1.0, output: 3.0}`, `metadata.updated_at = '2026-04-25'`, `metadata.source = 'https://github.com/OmniSight/OmniSight-Productizer/issues/292'`, `metadata.schema_version = 1`. **Scope discipline — what this checkbox does NOT do** (deferred to its own row): no dedicated YAML-corrupt-boot test row beyond what's covered transitively here (checkbox 6 — the dedicated test row will add a startup-path version + a live-Redis cross-worker integration test that this row does not exercise);
