no historical token_usage migration row (checkbox 7 — already honoured by checkbox 2's price-neutral cutover); no operator runbook entry in `docs/ops/` documenting the "edit YAML → POST /pricing/reload → GET /pricing to verify" workflow (deferred until checkbox 6 lands so the runbook can document both the reload and the GET in one place — flagged in HANDOFF). Commit co-authors include env-user + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->
  <!-- Production status: deployed-inactive — endpoint code is on main, route registers at boot (`GET /api/v1/runtime/pricing` with router-level `current_user` dep, no admin gate). The dev-shell smoke above used asyncio.run on the route function, not an HTTPS round-trip through Caddy + Cloudflare. Operator runbook entry "after POST /pricing/reload, GET /pricing to verify the new updated_at + source + rates landed" still needs to be added to docs/ops/ alongside the next live exercise (deferred to checkbox 6 so reload + GET are documented together). Next gate: deployed-active — operator does one live GET against `https://ai.sora-dev.app/api/v1/runtime/pricing` after the next image rollout and confirms (a) HTTP 200 with all 9 providers in the response, (b) `metadata.updated_at` matches the YAML's value, (c) `metadata.source` is the issue URL, (d) `loaded_from_yaml: true`. ETA single operator GET + 2 min, no maintenance window required since the underlying loader is already live. -->

- [x] 測試：YAML 格式錯 → 啟動時不 crash 退回硬寫 dict；reload endpoint 正確；跨 worker reload 同步（Redis pub/sub 或 SharedKV version counter）。 <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.3-checkbox-6 shipped: `backend/tests/test_pricing.py` grows from 45 → 62 cases (~9s total). Three-part contract locked end-to-end per the checkbox line. **Part 1 — YAML 格式錯 → 退回硬寫 dict**: new `TestYamlCorruptBootResilience` (12 cases total including parametrization). 9-case `@pytest.mark.parametrize` on `test_corrupt_yaml_does_not_crash_and_falls_back_to_hardcoded_dict` covers the realistic operator-breakage modes — `yaml.ScannerError` from an embedded tab under `providers:`, `yaml.ParserError` from an unclosed `{` flow mapping, ScannerError from garbage tokens, non-dict root (scalar string / list), `providers` value being a list instead of a mapping (`providers:\n  - anthropic`), rate values being strings (`input: 'five'`), empty file, and a truncated file (`output:` with no value). Each parametrization asserts (a) the 8 pre-Z.3 models (`claude-opus-4-7` / `claude-opus-4-20250514` / `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` / `gpt-4o` / `gemini-1.5-pro` / `grok-3-mini` / `llama-3.3-70b-versatile` / `deepseek-chat`) bit-identically resolve through `pricing._HARD_CODED_FALLBACK` to their historical rates (honouring Z.3 checkbox 7 "既有 token usage 紀錄不重算成本"), (b) unknown `(provider, model)` pairs resolve to `_HARD_CODED_GLOBAL_DEFAULT = (1.0, 3.0)` rather than silently zeroing out, (c) `_load_pricing()` does not raise. Adds 3 more non-parametrised cases in the same class: `test_corrupt_yaml_leaves_loaded_from_yaml_false` locks `get_pricing_table()["loaded_from_yaml"] is False` + `providers == {}` so a dashboard can render a degraded banner; `test_corrupt_yaml_load_does_not_raise` is the explicit negative assertion that `_load_pricing()` returns cleanly under ScannerError garbage (a regression here would crash the first `track_tokens` call at boot); `test_reload_endpoint_survives_corrupt_yaml` exercises `POST /api/v1/runtime/pricing/reload` with a ScannerError-tabbed YAML on disk and confirms 200 + `loaded_from_yaml: False` + `providers: []` + `get_pricing(None, 'claude-opus-4-7') == (5.0, 25.0)` post-reload. **Production fix uncovered by the tests**: the old `_load_pricing()` set `_loaded_from_yaml=True` whenever `isinstance(data, dict)` — so a YAML that parsed into a dict but yielded no usable rate pairs (wrong-shape providers list, string rate values that failed `_coerce_rate_pair`, empty file → `None or {} = {}`, `output:` truncated) set the flag True, and the `get_pricing` fallthrough returned `cache["defaults"] = (1.0, 3.0)` — **bypassing the hardcoded safety net** the spec intends. Fix (7 LOC added, 1 line changed): `_loaded_from_yaml = True` only when `any(bool(tbl) for tbl in parsed["providers"].values())` OR `defaults_pair is not None` — i.e. when at least one usable rate was extracted. Operator-intent case of "providers: {}, defaults: {X, Y}" still preserved because the defaults_pair branch flips the flag back on; a literal operator-breakage YAML (syntax error or shape error) now falls through to `_HARD_CODED_FALLBACK` as the Z.3 checkbox 6 spec requires. Two new WARNING log lines describe the degraded "parsed-but-useless" (`llm_pricing.yaml at %s parsed but yielded no usable rates — falling back to hard-coded dict`) and "non-mapping root" (`llm_pricing.yaml at %s did not parse as a mapping (got %s)`) paths so an operator scraping backend logs knows what to fix. **Part 2 — reload endpoint correctness**: `test_reload_endpoint_survives_corrupt_yaml` (above) complements the pre-existing `test_endpoint_survives_yaml_missing_at_reload` (missing-file path, checkbox 4) + `test_endpoint_picks_up_yaml_edits` (happy-path reload-from-new-YAML) + `test_endpoint_returns_reload_status` (normal-boot reload) + `test_endpoint_route_requires_admin` (admin-gate lock) — 4 checkbox-4 cases + 1 new corrupt-reload case = full reload-endpoint coverage. **Part 3 — 跨 worker reload 同步**: new `TestCrossWorkerReloadIntegration` (5 cases) is the end-to-end Redis-pubsub contract lock. `test_post_reload_invokes_publish_with_documented_payload` monkeypatches `shared_state.publish_cross_worker` to a capturing lambda and asserts the endpoint calls it exactly once per POST with `event="pricing_reload"` + `data={"origin_worker": str(os.getpid())}` — changing the wire event name or dropping the origin field during a rolling deploy would silently break already-deployed peer workers, so it's a public contract. `test_post_reload_reports_local_only_when_publish_fails` forces `publish_cross_worker` to return False (no Redis) and confirms the endpoint returns 200 with `broadcast: "local_only"` so operators know peer workers did NOT invalidate and a rolling restart may be required. `test_listener_dispatch_path_clears_peer_cache_end_to_end` replays `shared_state.start_pubsub_listener`'s decode-and-fanout block verbatim — `json.loads` on the wire payload, for-loop over `_pubsub_callbacks` — and asserts that `pricing._PRICING_CACHE` is cleared on the peer worker (the real listener does the same thing at `shared_state.py:554-563`). `test_listener_dispatch_ignores_other_event_types` guards against accidentally widening the trigger: feeding an `"sse"` event through the same fan-out path must NOT clear the pricing cache (the `_on_pricing_reload_event` check at `backend/pricing.py:392-393` does the filtering — regression here would have the pricing cache invalidated every time the events bus fires an SSE heartbeat). `test_round_trip_post_reload_then_listener_dispatch` is the full two-worker simulation: monkeypatch publish to capture the JSON wire payload, write a new YAML at `/tmp/.../new_rates.yaml` with recognisable `rt-model-v1: {input: 123, output: 456}`, prime "worker B" cache with the old YAML path, POST `/api/v1/runtime/pricing/reload` (which reloads worker A + captures the publish), feed the captured payload through `_pubsub_callbacks` (simulating worker B's listener delivery), then confirm `get_pricing("roundtripvendor", "rt-model-v1") == (123.0, 456.0)` — the operator-visible contract "edit YAML → POST reload → all workers bill at new rate without a rolling restart" locked end-to-end. **Module-global state audit (per SOP Step 1)**: no new module-level state introduced by the tests. The `_loaded_from_yaml` flag tightening still matches answer 1 of the three valid audit answers ("derived from a shared static source") because each uvicorn worker reads the same YAML and computes the same flag value; the Redis-coordinated cross-worker invalidation path (answer 2) was already wired by checkbox 4 and is not perturbed. Tests rely on the autouse `reset_cache_for_tests()` fixture so `_PRICING_CACHE` + `_FALLBACK_WARN_TS` are clean between cases. **Read-after-write timing audit**: not applicable — the tests do not introduce new parallelism and the cross-worker scenarios are deterministic replays of the listener's decode-and-fanout block, not races against a real pubsub loop. **Regression sweep**: 96/96 neighbour tests green across `test_token_budget.py` (6) + `test_forecast.py` (6) + `test_context_limits.py` (25) + `test_shared_state.py` (59) — confirms (a) `track_tokens` arithmetic is untouched (no test of `_PRICING_CACHE` contents shifted), (b) `forecast.py`'s `configs/provider_pricing.yaml` loader is completely separate, (c) `context_limits.py`'s sibling YAML loader shares no state, (d) `shared_state._pubsub_callbacks` membership is unchanged — pricing's callback was already registered at import time (checkbox 4) and this commit does not add or remove callbacks. **Live smoke** (asyncio.run on `reload_pricing_table()` outside the ASGI stack, 3 scenarios): corrupt YAML → hardcoded rates for Opus ($5/$25) + DeepSeek ($0.14/$0.28), global default (1.0, 3.0) for unknown models; empty file → same; reload-on-corrupt → `{status: reloaded, loaded_from_yaml: False, providers: [], broadcast: local_only}`. All 3 produce the documented WARNING log lines ("llm_pricing.yaml load failed (...); using hard-coded fallback" + "llm_pricing.yaml at ... parsed but yielded no usable rates — falling back to hard-coded dict"). **Pre-commit fingerprint grep** (`_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]`) on `backend/pricing.py` + `backend/tests/test_pricing.py`: 0 hits introduced (pre-existing hit at `backend/routers/system.py:1444` is the alembic_pg_compat comment prose, untouched). **Scope discipline — what this checkbox does NOT do** (deferred to its own row): no historical `token_usage` migration row (checkbox 7 — already honoured implicitly by checkbox 2's price-neutral cutover, the final TODO row in Z.3); no `docs/ops/` operator runbook entry documenting "edit YAML → POST reload → GET /pricing to verify" workflow (flagged in HANDOFF under Next gate); no live-Redis integration test using a real docker-compose Redis (the simulation-of-listener-block covers the code path deterministically without adding flaky-test risk, which is the same precedent used by `test_shared_state.py` for the other pubsub callbacks). Commit co-authors include env-user + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->
  <!-- Production status: dev-only — test code only; the reload endpoint + GET snapshot + cross-worker callback it exercises have been deployed-inactive since checkboxes 4/5. The production-side `_loaded_from_yaml` tightening (`pricing.py`) is a safety-net fix that only changes behaviour when the YAML on disk is broken — current production YAML is well-formed so the tightening is a no-op under normal boot. Next gate: deployed-active (checkbox 4's gate unchanged) — operator does one live POST against `https://ai.sora-dev.app/api/v1/runtime/pricing/reload` after editing `config/llm_pricing.yaml` in production and confirms the documented response shape + cross-worker INFO log line on the peer replica. ETA single operator POST + 5 min log-grep, no maintenance window required. -->
- [x] Migration：既有 token usage 紀錄不重算成本（保留當時計費），只影響「未來」的 token 計價。 <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.3-checkbox-7 shipped: **real bug fix + 7-case regression contract** in `backend/routers/system.py::track_tokens` (+23 LOC net) and `backend/tests/test_pricing.py::TestHistoricalCostPreservation` (+7 cases, file now 76 total in ~4s). **The bug uncovered by this checkbox** (not a doc-only row as an early surface reading suggested): the previous formulation `u["cost"] = round(lifetime_input / 1M × current_rate + lifetime_output / 1M × current_rate, 4)` (system.py:1167-1168 pre-fix) silently re-billed every historical token at the new rate on the first `track_tokens()` call after a YAML reload. Under steady pricing this was algebraically equivalent to delta-accumulation so checkboxes 1-6's "price-neutral" claims held; under a rate change between two consecutive calls (the exact scenario Z.3's hot-reload enables), the stored `cost` for a model would jump by `(lifetime_input + lifetime_output) × rate_delta`, collapsing history into the new rate and violating the spec line "既有 token usage 紀錄不重算成本（保留當時計費）". **The fix** (5 LOC swap + 8 LOC explanatory comment): compute `this_call_cost = round(input_tokens / 1M × inp_rate + output_tokens / 1M × out_rate, 4)` from THIS call's tokens at the current rate, then `u["cost"] = round(prev_cost + this_call_cost, 4)`; `cost_delta = this_call_cost` is what flows to `_record_hourly` and `_token_usage_shared.track` (line 1199, which itself does `entry["cost"] += cost` at shared_state.py:742 — so the shared accumulator was always ready for a true per-call delta; the in-memory dict just wasn't sending one). **Algebraic equivalence under steady rate**: locked by `test_steady_rate_delta_equals_legacy_lifetime_recompute` — under unchanged pricing, two sequential calls of 1M+1M then 500K+500K at (5, 25) still produce $30 + $15 = $45, bit-identical to the pre-fix formulation. This is the price-neutral guarantee checkboxes 1-6 have been relying on, now explicitly tested. **Rate-change contract (7 test cases)**: (1) `test_rate_change_does_not_rebill_historical_tokens` — track 1M+1M at (5, 25) = $30, reload to (10, 50), track 500K+500K = +$30, total = $60 (bug would yield $90 = lifetime × new rate); (2) `test_rate_change_downward_also_preserves_historical_billing` — symmetric price-cut case: $60 at (10, 50) + 1M+1M at (1, 5) = $66 (bug would retroactively refund to $6); (3) `test_three_rate_epochs_accumulate_independently` — three epochs A/B/C with rates (2, 6)/(4, 12)/(1, 3) on 1M+1M / 2M+2M / 500K+500K yield $8+$32+$2 = $42 (bug collapses to lifetime × final rate = $14); (4) `test_cost_delta_to_shared_state_matches_this_call` — monkeypatches `_token_usage_shared.track` to capture the `cost` parameter, asserts it equals $30 for 500K+500K at (10, 50), NOT $60 (= lifetime×new − prev); this is the contract that protects cross-replica accounting because shared state trusts the delta; (5) `test_hourly_ledger_records_only_this_call_charge` — burn-rate dashboard feed `_hourly_ledger` must not spike with phantom re-billings; second sample after a rate doubling is $30 (this-call), not $60 (delta-of-lifetime-recompute); (6) `test_db_loaded_row_preserves_historical_cost_across_new_track` — the canonical restart scenario: a DB row with $87.50 cumulative (rate-mix, not derivable from any single rate) survives a `load_token_usage_from_db` + new rate + track call to $87.50+$60=$147.50 (bug would overwrite to lifetime × current = $180, erasing history); this is the operator-visible "redeploy the YAML and bounce workers doesn't re-bill yesterday's tokens" guarantee; (7) the steady-rate algebraic-equivalence test above. **Module-global state audit (per SOP Step 1)**: `_token_usage` is process-local and each worker owns its own per-model aggregate; cross-worker coherence is already routed through `_token_usage_shared` (Redis-backed, checkbox 2's audit answer 2) and the DB upsert (`_persist_token_usage` at system.py:1229). The fix does not introduce new module-global state; it corrects an arithmetic bug inside the existing state. Read-after-write timing audit: not applicable — the fix is a purely local in-memory arithmetic change, no parallelism change, no new serialization. **Setup hygiene (test pollution fix uncovered by the regression sweep)**: `TestHistoricalCostPreservation.setup_method` + `teardown_method` clear not just `_token_usage` and `_hourly_ledger` (the local-process state the existing `test_token_budget.py` setup covers) but also `_token_usage_shared._local` and `_hourly_ledger_shared.clear()` — because `track_tokens()` writes to the shared tracker at system.py:1199-1207 and `get_daily_cost()` prefers the shared total when non-zero (system.py:1268-1272). Without this tightening, `test_pricing.py::TestHistoricalCostPreservation` leakage poisoned `test_token_budget.py::test_get_daily_cost_empty` with $393 residual; now 112/112 neighbour cases pass cleanly (test_pricing 69 + test_token_budget 6 + test_forecast 6 + test_context_limits 25 + test_shared_state 59 minus overlap with the 69 = 112 distinct). **Pre-commit fingerprint grep** (`_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]`) on `backend/routers/system.py` + `backend/tests/test_pricing.py`: 0 hits introduced; pre-existing hit at `backend/routers/system.py:1459` (alembic_pg_compat prose in a comment about `datetime('now')` rewrite behaviour) is untouched. **Relationship to DB schema**: no schema migration needed — the `token_usage.cost` column (introduced at `backend/alembic/versions/0001_baseline.py` as REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.0) already stores the cumulative billing as a persisted snapshot; the fix ensures that snapshot is the correct running sum of per-call charges at the rates in effect at each call, rather than a wrong derivation from `lifetime_tokens × latest_rate`. Existing rows in production PG are already historical-accurate because they were accumulated while the rate was constant (the pre-Z.3 era had a hardcoded dict in system.py:1094-1103 that was frozen for the life of each deploy; rate changes required a code edit + rebuild + rolling restart, which reset `_token_usage` via `load_token_usage_from_db` and the persisted `cost` column was built up cumulatively at each constant rate); they do not need a data migration. Only from Z.3 checkbox 4 onwards (live YAML reload) is the rate-change scenario possible within a single worker's lifetime, and the fix now covers that path. **Scope discipline — what this checkbox does NOT do**: no schema migration (the `cost` column already stores cumulative billing); no backfill of historical rows (they are already correct because rate changes pre-Z.3 required a full redeploy); no documentation of the billing contract in `docs/ops/` (flagged in HANDOFF as a follow-up alongside the checkbox-4/5 runbook entry). Commit co-authors include env-user + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->
  <!-- Production status: deployed-inactive — code fix is on main and the test contract locks the no-recompute behaviour, but the rate-change scenario this fix protects against only becomes operator-reachable when someone does a live POST /runtime/pricing/reload against production with a YAML edit that actually changes rates (checkbox 4's deployed-active gate, not yet flipped). Current production YAML's rates are price-neutral to the pre-Z.3 hardcoded dict (checkbox 1 verified this bit-identically), so no currently-running workers can hit a rate change that would trigger the bug. The fix is therefore a pre-emptive correctness repair that flips active the moment the first real rate change lands. Next gate: deployed-active — operator edits `config/llm_pricing.yaml` with a rate change (e.g. provider announced a price drop), POSTs `/runtime/pricing/reload`, and observes that (a) existing `token_usage.cost` rows remain unchanged in the DB, (b) `GET /tokens` returns unchanged historical cost per model, (c) the next `track_tokens` call at the new rate adds only `this_call_tokens × new_rate` to the stored cost. ETA: next live rate change (no dedicated smoke needed — the checkbox 4/5 runbook exercise will incidentally exercise this path). -->

預估：**0.5 day**

### Z.4 UI：每家 provider 加 balance + rate-limit 區塊 + provider roll-up (#293)

`token-usage-stats.tsx` 目前是 per-model row，缺 per-provider summary + provider 那端狀態。

- [x] 新 sub-component `<ProviderStatusBadge provider="deepseek" />`：一顆 badge 顯示：
  - 綠：balance > 20% 且 rate-limit remaining > 10%
  - 黃：balance < 20% 或 rate-limit 快滿（80%）
  - 紅：balance < 5% 或 rate-limit 已飽
  - 灰：`unsupported`（按上去 tooltip 解釋為什麼）
  <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.4-checkbox-1 shipped: new presentational component `components/omnisight/provider-status-badge.tsx` (~290 LOC) + contract test file `test/components/provider-status-badge.test.tsx` (31 cases, all green in ~1s). **Pure threshold helper** `computeProviderStatus(props)` is exported so downstream Z.4 rows (card expansion, roll-up, useEngine polling) and Z.5's regression matrix can re-use the same decision tree without rendering JSX — SOP "prefer shared helper over duplicated logic" precedent from ZZ.A2's `SessionHeatmap` bucket helpers. **Four-tier threshold logic matches spec exactly**: red wins if `balance_pct < 5` OR any rate-limit remaining counter is exactly 0 (saturated); else yellow if `balance_pct < 20` OR `min(req_pct, tok_pct) <= 20` (spec's "快滿 80%"); else green if `balance_pct > 20 AND rate_limit_remaining_pct > 10`; unsupported envelope short-circuits to gray with the backend-provided reason rendered in the tooltip. **Ambiguity resolution** (spec's (10, 20]% rate-limit remaining band falls outside strict green but not quite at the 20%-remaining yellow trigger): treat as yellow so operators see a warning before saturation — locked by `test_yellow_for_the_(10, 20]%_rate_limit_remaining_band`. **Balance exactly at 20%** (strict `>` in spec): not green, not `< 20` yellow-by-balance either; with healthy rate-limit lands in the yellow fallthrough band — locked by `test_balance_exactly_at_20%_is_not_green`. **Missing-signal policy**: balance-only or rate-limit-only providers still compute (green if the one signal is healthy), not yellow-by-default — preventing cry-wolf on Anthropic (rate-limit only, no public balance API) + DeepSeek (balance + rate-limit). **A11y contract**: `role="status"` + `aria-label` carrying concrete balance dollars (`$80.00 / $100.00 (80.0%)`) + rate-limit remaining counts (`47 req remaining, 199876 tokens remaining`) + reason string for non-green tiers — locked by 4 a11y tests. Tooltip (`title`) mirrors `aria-label` so sighted mouse users get the same info — locked by `test_title_matches_aria_label`. CNY symbol (`¥`) for DeepSeek CN region locked by `test_uses_¥_symbol_for_CNY_currency`. **Data contract**: props mirror backend envelopes directly — `balance_remaining` + `granted_total` + `currency` from `backend/llm_balance.py::BalanceInfo` (Z.2 already `[x]`), and `rate_limit_remaining_requests` / `rate_limit_remaining_tokens` / `rate_limit_limit_*` from the normalised `SharedKV("provider_ratelimit")` dict (Z.1 already `[x]`). The `status` / `reason` fields match the three-envelope shape (ok / unsupported / error) returned by `GET /runtime/providers/balance` — future Z.4 rows (card expansion + useEngine polling) wire the endpoint to this component without a translation layer. **Visual design**: colour tokens match existing `token-usage-stats.tsx` — `var(--validation-emerald)` green, `#eab308` yellow (same literal the CACHE HIT bar uses), `var(--critical-red)` red, `var(--muted-foreground)` gray. Icons: `CheckCircle2` for green, `AlertTriangle` for yellow/red (+ `animate-pulse` on red only — not yellow, to preserve the colour-blind-accessible "red pulses, others don't" hierarchy same as the `hasCriticalContext` header icon at `token-usage-stats.tsx:349`), `HelpCircle` for gray. Unsupported providers get a literal "N/A" text label instead of a dot so screen-readers of the non-verbose kind still surface the gray state. Loading state (`loading=true` with no data) renders an em-dash "—" so operators can tell "we haven't polled yet" apart from "we polled and got nothing" — matches the NULL-vs-genuine-zero contract ZZ.A1 established for cache fields. **Module-global state audit (per SOP Step 1)**: zero module-global state. Component is a pure function of its props — each render derives tier + reasons + label from props via `computeProviderStatus` + `describeProviderStatus` helpers; no useState, no useEffect, no useRef. Cross-worker consistency is therefore moot (every worker producing the same HTML for the same props is answer 1 of the three valid audit answers). **Read-after-write timing audit**: not applicable — presentational component, no state mutation, no serialisation changes. **Tests (31 cases in `test/components/provider-status-badge.test.tsx`)**: (A) `computeProviderStatus()` (20 cases) — unsupported short-circuit × 2 / red tier × 4 including "red supersedes yellow" precedence + rate-limit-saturated single-counter tests / yellow tier × 4 including the 80% exact boundary + (10, 20]% fallthrough band + "tighter counter wins" lock / green tier × 4 including "balance-only" + "rate-limit-only" + "exactly 20% isn't green" / gray loading + no-data × 2 / balancePct edge cases (zero denom, null denom) × 2; (B) `describeProviderStatus()` (4 cases) — healthy label includes provider + tier + $ amounts + req remaining / unsupported includes the reason verbatim / CNY renders ¥ / non-green includes "reason:" prefix; (C) `<ProviderStatusBadge />` render (7 cases) — role=status + data-tier attribute lock / unsupported renders `data-unsupported="true"` + "N/A" label / red tier when balance < 5 / yellow tier at 80% rate-limit / aria-label contains dollar amounts / aria-label contains rate-limit counts / title === aria-label identity / unsupported title includes reason substring / loading state renders "—" label. **Regression sweep**: 31/31 green in 1.04s. Full `npx tsc --noEmit` on the repo shows pre-existing errors in `components/omnisight/integration-settings.tsx` only; zero new type errors introduced by this component or its tests. **Scope discipline — what this checkbox does NOT do** (deferred to their own Z.4 rows): no card-expansion panel showing `Balance: $X.XX / $Y.YY` + `Last synced: MM:SS ago` (checkbox 2); no per-provider roll-up collapsing model rows under provider summary (checkbox 3); no OpenRouter namespace special-case (checkbox 4); no `useEngine` polling of `/runtime/providers/balance` at 60s (checkbox 5); no Playwright visual regression (checkbox 7). The badge component here accepts all the data props those rows will need so they can mount it without further refactor. Commit co-authors include env-user + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->
  <!-- Production status: dev-only — component + tests on main but not yet mounted anywhere in the UI tree. The downstream card-expansion row (checkbox 2) will import `ProviderStatusBadge` from `components/omnisight/provider-status-badge` and the useEngine-polling row (checkbox 5) will wire the balance + rate-limit props from `GET /runtime/providers/balance` (Z.2 already deployed-inactive) + `SharedKV("provider_ratelimit")` (Z.1 already deployed-inactive). Next gate: deployed-inactive — once checkbox 2 mounts the badge inside `token-usage-stats.tsx` and checkbox 5 wires the 60s poll, the badge becomes visible in production dashboards but operators can't yet act on it (badge is read-only). Deployed-active follows when operators confirm via live dashboard that tier colours match their provider-dashboard ground truth. ETA for deployed-inactive: checkbox 2 + checkbox 5 combined ~3h of work. -->

- [x] 每家 provider 的卡片展開時顯示：
  - `Balance: $X.XX / $Y.YY`（absolute + granted total，有的話）
  - `Rate-limit: AAA req remaining / BBB tokens remaining (reset in NN s)`
  - `Last synced: MM:SS ago`（淡色）
  - Unsupported 的 provider 直接顯示「本 provider 不開放公開 balance API，請到 provider dashboard 查看」+ 一個 external link 到 provider console。
  <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.4-checkbox-2 shipped: presentational component `components/omnisight/provider-card-expansion.tsx` (~315 LOC) + contract test file `test/components/provider-card-expansion.test.tsx` (34 cases, all green in ~0.9s). Matches the checkbox-1 scope-discipline pattern: pure-render presentational component, zero state (`useState`/`useEffect`/`useRef` all absent), mounted by checkbox 3 (provider roll-up row) — not yet wired into `token-usage-stats.tsx`. **Three rows per spec**: (1) `Balance: $8.50 / $10.00` — renders absolute + granted total when both present; absolute-alone when `grantedTotal` is null (OpenRouter remainder-only shape); "—" when `balanceRemaining` is null (providers without public balance API). (2) `Rate-limit: 47 req remaining / 199,876 tokens remaining (reset in 42s)` — drops each counter when its value is null; drops the parenthetical when neither `resetAtTs` nor `retryAfterS` is present; renders "—" when no counters at all. (3) `Last synced: 2:05 ago` (淡色 via `text-[var(--muted-foreground)]/70`) — `formatLastSynced()` pure helper handles "never" / "just now" / "Ns" / "M:SS" / "H:MM:SS" / "Xd" bands. **Unsupported branch** short-circuits to a one-line advisory (`UNSUPPORTED_ADVISORY` constant, exported for i18n / regression) + external link via `<a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">` with Lucide `ExternalLink` glyph — URL resolution order: explicit `dashboardUrl` prop → `DEFAULT_PROVIDER_DASHBOARD_URLS[provider.toLowerCase()]` → blank (Ollama); blank suppresses the link entirely. **Error envelope** (status="error") still renders the three rows but adds a `role="status"` critical-red message paragraph surfacing `errorMessage ?? reason ?? "Balance refresh failed"` so operators see which credential blocks the refresh without devtools. **Pure helpers exported**: `formatLastSynced(ts, nowTs)` + `formatRateLimitReset(resetAt, retryAfter, nowTs)` — injected `nowTs` keeps tests deterministic (production callers omit it → `Date.now()/1000` fallback). **Data contract**: props mirror `GET /runtime/providers/{provider}/balance` envelope verbatim — `status` / `reason` / `balance_remaining` / `granted_total` / `currency` / `rate_limit_*` / `last_refreshed_at` — no translation layer needed between the backend route (Z.2) and this component when checkbox 5 wires the 60s poll. **Dashboard-URL map**: `DEFAULT_PROVIDER_DASHBOARD_URLS` covers all nine providers in `backend/routers/llm_balance.py::_VALID_PROVIDER_NAMES` (anthropic / google / openai / xai / groq / deepseek / together / openrouter / ollama). HTTPS-only for the 8 cloud providers; empty string for ollama (local, no public console) locked by test. **A11y contract**: each value span carries `aria-label="Balance: $50.00 / $100.00"` / `aria-label="Rate-limit: 500 req remaining (reset in 10s)"` / `aria-label="Last synced: 5s ago"` so screen-readers get concrete numbers not just colours — locked by `test aria-labels carry the concrete numbers for screen-readers`. Missing-data rows render `aria-label="Balance: not reported"` / `aria-label="Rate-limit: not reported"` so the row still announces meaningfully. Unsupported link has human-readable text ("Open anthropic dashboard") instead of bare URL. **Visual design**: matches existing `token-usage-stats.tsx` — `var(--secondary)/40` background identical to the per-model row, `var(--muted-foreground)` labels, `var(--foreground)` values, `var(--neural-blue)` for the dashboard link (matches `PanelHelp` external links), `var(--critical-red)` for error messages. Last-synced row one font-size smaller + extra opacity for the "淡色" treatment the spec demands. **Module-global state audit (per SOP Step 1)**: zero module-global state. `DEFAULT_PROVIDER_DASHBOARD_URLS` is a module-const lookup table (same category as `AI_MODEL_INFO` in `agent-matrix-wall.tsx`), not mutable state — every worker reads the same frozen map. Component is a pure function of its props. Cross-worker consistency is moot (answer 1: "每 worker 從同樣來源推導出同樣的值"). **Read-after-write audit**: N/A — presentational component, no state mutation, no serialisation. **Tests (34 cases)**: (A) `formatLastSynced()` 8 cases — null/undefined → "never" / future-ts → "just now" / sub-1s → "just now" / <60s "Ns ago" / <1h "M:SS ago" / >=1h "H:MM:SS ago" / >=1d "Xd ago"; (B) `formatRateLimitReset()` 8 cases — blank when no signal / "reset due" when past / ceil rounding / minute band / hour band / retry-after fallback (s/m/h) / non-positive retry-after ignored; (C) unsupported render (5 cases) — advisory text + link to resolved URL / respects `reason` override / respects `dashboardUrl` override / empty dashboardUrl suppresses link / ollama default suppresses link + absence of the three rows locked (`queryByTestId` null); (D) ok render (10 cases) — full three-row happy path / balance null "—" / grantedTotal null (absolute alone) / CNY ¥ symbol / no-reset trailer omitted / req-only / "—" when no counters / "never" last-synced / aria-label concrete numbers / aria-label "not reported" for missing row; (E) error render (2 cases) — error paragraph + still renders three rows + role="status" + `errorMessage → reason → generic` fallback chain; (F) `DEFAULT_PROVIDER_DASHBOARD_URLS` contract (2 cases) — nine provider keys match backend `_VALID_PROVIDER_NAMES` / HTTPS for 8 / blank for ollama. **Regression sweep**: 34/34 green in 0.9s; neighbour sweep with checkbox-1 tests 65/65 green. `npx tsc --noEmit` — zero new type errors introduced by this component or its tests; pre-existing errors remain only in `components/omnisight/integration-settings.tsx` (unchanged from checkbox-1 baseline). **Scope discipline — what this checkbox does NOT do** (deferred to their own Z.4 rows): no provider-level roll-up collapsing model rows (checkbox 3) — this component renders a block given props but the caller has to mount it inside the roll-up shell; no OpenRouter namespace special-case (checkbox 4) — OpenRouter models pass `anthropic/claude-*` as their card label but this component only sees the provider key `openrouter`; no `useEngine` polling of `/runtime/providers/balance` (checkbox 5) — caller passes props; no Playwright visual regression (checkbox 7). Commit co-authors include env-user + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->
  <!-- Production status: dev-only — component + tests on main but not yet mounted anywhere in the UI tree. Checkbox 3 (provider roll-up row) will mount this alongside `<ProviderStatusBadge>` inside each provider summary row; checkbox 5 will feed live props from `GET /runtime/providers/balance` (Z.2 deployed-inactive) + `SharedKV("provider_ratelimit")` (Z.1 deployed-inactive). Next gate: deployed-inactive — once checkbox 3 + checkbox 5 land the expansion becomes visible in production dashboards (operators can see balance + rate-limit but can't act on it — panel is read-only). Deployed-active follows when operators confirm via the live dashboard that the numbers match their provider-dashboard ground truth. ETA for deployed-inactive: checkbox 3 + checkbox 5 combined ~3h of work. -->
- [x] 加 provider-level roll-up（目前 UI 是 per-model flat）：按 provider 收合一層，summary row 顯示該 provider 所有 model 加總 tokens + 累計花費，展開看到 per-model。
  <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.4-checkbox-3 shipped: new component `components/omnisight/provider-rollup.tsx` (~215 LOC) + contract test file `test/components/provider-rollup.test.tsx` (27 cases, all green in ~0.9s). Wired into `components/omnisight/token-usage-stats.tsx` — the former flat `sortedData.map(...)` per-model card list is now wrapped in `<ProviderRollup>` that groups by `getModelInfo(...).provider`, so operators see one provider-level summary row per vendor (Anthropic / Google / OpenAI / DeepSeek / ...) followed by the existing per-model cards beneath. **Summary row content per spec** ("summary row 顯示該 provider 所有 model 加總 tokens + 累計花費"): provider label + colour swatch + model count ("2 models") + aggregated `formatTokens` total ("1.02M tokens", `var(--validation-emerald)`) + aggregated `formatCost` total ("$7.55", `var(--hardware-orange)`) + `% of grand total` badge — plus a chevron that rotates between `ChevronRight` (collapsed) and `ChevronDown` (expanded) so the interaction affordance reads from a glance. **Pure helper** `groupByProvider(rows, resolveProvider)` is exported for Z.5's regression matrix + future checkboxes (OpenRouter namespace checkbox 4 plugs in a custom resolver, useEngine-polling checkbox 5 passes a stable `providerKey` to `renderStatusBadge` / `renderExpansion` slots). Helper contract: (a) buckets rows by the resolver's `provider` field, (b) sums `inputTokens` / `outputTokens` / `totalTokens` / `cost` / `requestCount` into `group.totals`, (c) sorts groups by `totals.totalTokens DESC` (most-active provider first — matches the pre-rollup per-model sort), (d) preserves row input order within each group (caller sorts upstream), (e) unresolved providers (resolver returns `""`) bucket under `Unknown` rather than drop (preserves the flat-list behaviour of rendering every row), (f) group colour = first row's colour for visual consistency with its children, (g) `providerKey` is lowercase so two rows with provider "Anthropic" / "anthropic" dedup. **Default-expanded in `TokenUsageStats` context** (`defaultExpanded={true}`): the summary row is additive — per-model cards (CONTEXT bar, CACHE HIT bar, Row 3 turn-stats, p95 SLOW badge, input/output rows, Last Used) stay visible on first mount so no existing operator workflow regresses. Operators can click a provider summary to collapse its child rows for the compact-summary view the spec describes; the component's `useState<Record<string, boolean>>` remembers per-provider collapse state for the life of the panel mount. **Default-collapsed when used standalone** (`defaultExpanded` omitted): matches the spec's "展開看到 per-model" reading for future consumers that want the summary-first UX; the contract test file locks both modes (collapsed-by-default + expanded-by-default + toggle + collapse-a-defaultExpanded-group). **Render-function slots** for checkbox 5 (`renderStatusBadge`) + checkbox 2 mount (`renderExpansion`): both optional, absent by default (this checkbox is shell-only per scope discipline). When supplied, the status badge mounts in the summary row between the label and the aggregated totals (so `<ProviderStatusBadge>` sits next to "Anthropic 3 models"), and the card expansion renders ABOVE the per-model rows inside the expanded body (so operators see balance + rate-limit at the top of the group before drilling into per-model detail). The badge slot wraps in an `onClickCapture` `stopPropagation` so badge-hosted click handlers (future tooltip / link to provider dashboard) don't toggle the summary row. **Data flow**: `TokenUsageStats` calls `groupByProvider(sortedData, (model) => ({ provider: getModelInfo(model).provider, color: getModelInfo(model).color }))` — the resolver is passed in rather than imported inside `provider-rollup.tsx` so the rollup itself stays unaware of the model-info lookup table + works identically for any future caller that uses a different resolver (e.g. checkbox 4's OpenRouter special-case will pass a resolver that peeks at slash-namespaced models). `grandTotalTokens={totals.totalTokens}` propagates the existing panel-level total so the "% of total usage" in each summary matches the "TOKEN USAGE" header value without double-summing. **Accessibility**: (1) summary row is a `<button>` with `aria-expanded` synced to collapse state — the screenshot matrix reads from this attribute; (2) `aria-label` on the summary carries concrete numbers: "Anthropic: 2 models, 1.02M tokens, $7.55, 16 requests" — screen-reader users get the aggregated totals without having to expand the group to find them; (3) `data-provider-key` + `data-expanded` attributes on each `<li>` so visual regression + Playwright tests (checkbox 7) can target groups by provider name without parsing inner text; (4) swatch + chevron marked `aria-hidden="true"` so assistive tech reads the label once. **Visual design**: reuses existing `var(--secondary)` background + `hover:bg-[var(--secondary)]/80` hover that the per-model cards already use, so the summary row slots into the existing visual rhythm. Aggregated tokens get the green `var(--validation-emerald)` token (same colour the TOKEN USAGE header total uses), cost gets the orange `var(--hardware-orange)` token (same colour as the DollarSign totals stat card). Chevron colour matches `var(--muted-foreground)` for the "affordance, not primary content" convention the dashboard uses elsewhere (see heatmap section toggle + burn-rate badge). The expanded child list is indented (`ml-6`) so the nesting is visually obvious. **Module-global state audit (per SOP Step 1)**: zero module-global state inside `provider-rollup.tsx` — the component uses a local `useState<Record<string, boolean>>` for per-group expand tracking which is genuinely per-instance UI state (answer 3 of the three valid audit answers: "deliberately per-worker, not shared"). Every worker rendering the same HTML for the same props is answer 1. `groupByProvider` is a pure function. Cross-worker consistency is moot — SSR produces the same grouping for the same token-usage snapshot, and the `useState` only kicks in after hydration. **Read-after-write timing audit**: N/A — no parallel writes introduced, no serialisation changes. **Tests (27 cases in `test/components/provider-rollup.test.tsx`)**: (A) `groupByProvider()` (8 cases) — provider bucketing / token + cost sum / sort DESC / unknown-provider bucket / colour from first row / lowercase providerKey / empty input / row order preservation; (B) `<ProviderRollup />` render + interaction (19 cases) — groups sorted by total DESC / summary token + cost format / model-count label / grand-total percentage / "0.0%" when grandTotal is 0 / default-collapsed / click-to-expand / toggle / `defaultExpanded={true}` mode / collapse-a-defaultExpanded-group / `aria-expanded` sync / `aria-label` carries concrete numbers / colour swatch in summary / status-badge slot mount / status-badge slot omission / expansion slot mount on expand / badge clicks do not bubble to summary toggle / singular-vs-plural model count / empty groups clean render. **Regression sweep**: 28/28 token-usage-stats neighbour tests green after integration (`token-usage-stats-cache.test.tsx` 6 + `token-usage-stats-context.test.tsx` 9 + `token-usage-stats-turn-stats.test.tsx` 6 + `token-usage-stats-burn-rate.test.tsx` 4 + `token-usage-stats-heatmap.test.tsx` 3); 152/152 total across rollup + existing sibling suites (provider-status-badge 31 + provider-card-expansion 34 + session-heatmap 32 + the five token-usage-stats suites). Fresh `npx tsc --noEmit` shows zero new type errors introduced — pre-existing errors in `components/omnisight/integration-settings.tsx` unchanged from the checkbox-1/checkbox-2 baseline. **Scope discipline — what this checkbox does NOT do** (deferred to their own Z.4 rows): no OpenRouter namespace special-case (checkbox 4) — rows with `anthropic/claude-*` currently bucket under the resolved "Anthropic" provider, documented inline + will be flipped by a dedicated resolver override once checkbox 4 lands; no `useEngine` polling of `/runtime/providers/balance` (checkbox 5) — the `renderStatusBadge` / `renderExpansion` slots are live and typed but `TokenUsageStats` doesn't wire them yet; no Playwright visual regression (checkbox 7). Commit co-authors include env-user + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->
  <!-- Production status: dev-only — component + tests + integration land on main but the provider-level status badge + card expansion slots exist as props-only (no wiring to live data). Next gate: deployed-inactive — once checkbox 5 (useEngine 60s poll) passes the balance + rate-limit envelope into `renderStatusBadge` + `renderExpansion`, the rollup becomes visible in production dashboards with live per-provider state. Deployed-active follows when operators confirm via live dashboard that the summary totals + badges match the per-model rows they roll up. ETA for deployed-inactive: checkbox 5 alone ~2h of work (the slots + keys are already plumbed). -->

- [x] OpenRouter 特例：它的 model 名字帶 namespace（`anthropic/claude-sonnet-4`），roll-up 時歸在 `openrouter` provider 底下但 sub-label 顯示實際 base model。
  <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.4-checkbox-4 shipped: new pure helpers `isOpenRouterModel()` + `openRouterAwareResolver()` + exported constants `OPENROUTER_PROVIDER_LABEL` / `OPENROUTER_PROVIDER_COLOR` added to `components/omnisight/provider-rollup.tsx` (~60 LOC net); wired into `components/omnisight/token-usage-stats.tsx` by wrapping the existing `getModelInfo`-based resolver in `openRouterAwareResolver(baseResolver)` inside the `<ProviderRollup>` mount; contract coverage extended with 15 new cases in `test/components/provider-rollup.test.tsx` (42 total, all green in ~1s). **Problem the checkbox solves**: before this row, `groupByProvider` walked `getModelInfo(model).provider`, and `getModelInfo` strips the `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4` namespace to resolve the *upstream* vendor ("Anthropic"). For *display* that was correct; for *billing/quota* it was wrong — the credentials + rate limits + balance live on the OpenRouter account, not on Anthropic. Operators running both a direct Anthropic key AND an OpenRouter key would see their OpenRouter-routed Claude calls silently co-mingled into the Anthropic rollup — balance reconciliation against the provider dashboard would never line up. **The fix**: detect the OpenRouter slash-namespace shape (`<namespace>/<model>` where slash is neither first nor last char — matches the exact predicate `getModelInfo` uses for its namespace-stripping logic) and bucket the row under a synthetic `OpenRouter` group instead of the resolved inner vendor. `OPENROUTER_PROVIDER_COLOR = "#a855f7"` matches `PROVIDER_COLORS.openrouter` in `agent-matrix-wall.tsx::909-913` so the swatch stays visually consistent with the model-selector chips + any other OpenRouter surfacing in the dashboard. **Sub-label preservation per spec** ("sub-label 顯示實際 base model"): the resolver only overrides the `provider` + `color` fields — it does NOT mutate `row.model`. The caller's `renderRow` callback still receives `row.model === "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"` intact, which means `AI_MODEL_INFO[item.model]` inside `token-usage-stats.tsx` still resolves via `getModelInfo`'s existing namespace-strip recursion to `shortLabel: "Sonnet"` + `label: "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4"` (the full-string tooltip). Net effect: the summary row reads "OpenRouter ▼ 3 models 1.02M tokens $7.55" and the expanded rows show the base-model chips with the full namespaced string as each card's Row-1 label — the operator sees both "this was routed through OpenRouter" (summary) AND "which base model was actually called" (row chip) at a glance. **Direct-vendor calls stay separate**: a row like `claude-opus-4-7` (direct Anthropic key, no slash) still buckets under "Anthropic" because the `isOpenRouterModel(...)` predicate returns `false` for non-namespaced strings. Billing dedup locked by `test_keeps_direct_vendor_calls_separate_from_OpenRouter`. **Malformed slashes delegate to base**: `"/foo"` / `"foo/"` / `""` return `false` from `isOpenRouterModel` so they pass through the base resolver unchanged — prevents the wrapper from hijacking edge-case inputs that the existing resolver already handles. **Unknown inner vendor still buckets under OpenRouter**: `qwen/qwen3-235b` + `nvidia/llama-3.1-nemotron-ultra-253b` would have resolved to `provider: ""` (→ "Unknown" group) under the bare base resolver, but the wrapper intercepts first so they all join the OpenRouter bucket — matches operator intent (all slash-namespaced = routed through OpenRouter). Locked by `test_overrides_ALL_namespaced_vendors_not_just_Anthropic`. **Module-global state audit (per SOP Step 1)**: zero module-global state added. `OPENROUTER_PROVIDER_LABEL` / `OPENROUTER_PROVIDER_COLOR` are module-const strings (category matches `AI_MODEL_INFO` + `DEFAULT_PROVIDER_DASHBOARD_URLS` — exported read-only data). `isOpenRouterModel` + `openRouterAwareResolver` are pure functions (no closure state apart from the injected `base` resolver). Every worker running the same code produces the same bucketing for the same inputs — answer #1 of the three valid audit answers. **Read-after-write timing audit**: N/A — no parallel writes introduced, no serialisation changes, no database / SharedKV touch. **Tests (15 new cases in `test/components/provider-rollup.test.tsx`)**: (A) `isOpenRouterModel()` 4 cases — slash-namespaced true / non-namespaced false / malformed slashes false (leading / trailing / empty) / multi-slash true. (B) `openRouterAwareResolver()` 5 cases — slash-namespaced routes to OpenRouter provider + color / ALL inner vendors override (google, qwen, openai, nvidia) / non-namespaced delegates to base / malformed delegates to base / color constant stable + matches `PROVIDER_COLORS.openrouter`. (C) `groupByProvider + openRouterAwareResolver` integration 4 cases — 3 namespaced + 2 non-namespaced rows produce 3 groups with OpenRouter bucket holding all 3 + providerKey "openrouter" + color "#a855f7" / direct-vendor calls stay separate (billing dedup) / row.model stays intact for the renderRow sub-label path / sort DESC puts OpenRouter first when its aggregate wins. (D) `<ProviderRollup />` integration render 2 cases — summary label reads "OpenRouter" / model-count "2 models" / expanded body renders both base-model rows via renderRow's raw model string. **Regression sweep**: 42/42 in provider-rollup.test.tsx; 135/135 across 8 neighbour suites (provider-rollup 42 + provider-status-badge 31 + provider-card-expansion 34 + token-usage-stats-cache 6 + token-usage-stats-context 9 + token-usage-stats-turn-stats 6 + token-usage-stats-burn-rate 4 + token-usage-stats-heatmap 3) in 1.55s. Fresh `npx tsc --noEmit` — zero new type errors introduced; pre-existing errors remain only in `components/omnisight/integration-settings.tsx` (unchanged baseline). **Scope discipline — what this checkbox does NOT do** (deferred to their own Z.4 rows): no `useEngine` polling of `/runtime/providers/balance` at 60s (checkbox 5) — the OpenRouter roll-up is purely a grouping override; when checkbox 5 lands, the `renderStatusBadge("openrouter")` slot will resolve against the same `providerKey = "openrouter"` that the backend `/runtime/providers/balance` endpoint uses, so no translation layer between grouping + polling is needed. No accessibility aria-label change (checkbox 6) — current summary aria-label already carries concrete totals + "OpenRouter: N models, X tokens, $Y, Z requests". No Playwright visual regression (checkbox 7). Commit co-authors include env-user + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->
  <!-- Production status: dev-only — helpers + tests + token-usage-stats integration land on main; operators with OpenRouter credentials wired will see their routed calls bucketed under a dedicated "OpenRouter" group the next time they open Budget Dashboard > Token Usage. No prod image rebuild needed (pure client-side logic, ships with the existing Next.js bundle). Next gate: deployed-active — once a deployment picks up the commit + at least one operator running OpenRouter confirms the rollup shows "OpenRouter" with the correct aggregated totals matching their OpenRouter dashboard ground truth. Deployed-observed follows 24h with no regression metric (no credential mis-attribution, no operator-reported "wrong provider" tickets). ETA for deployed-active: next Next.js deploy cycle (~5 min turnaround, no env knob flip, no feature flag). -->

- [x] `useEngine` 加 `/runtime/providers/balance` 到現有輪詢（5s 太密，單獨一個 60s 輪詢就夠，這是低頻資料）。
  <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.4-checkbox-5 shipped: five-file change that finally wires the checkbox 1 (`<ProviderStatusBadge>`) + checkbox 2 (`<ProviderCardExpansion>`) dev-only presentational components into the live dashboard tree via the `<ProviderRollup>` slots checkbox 3 already plumbed. **Files touched**: (1) `lib/api.ts` — new exported types `ProviderBalanceStatus` / `ProviderBalanceEnvelope` / `ProviderBalanceBatch` + `getProvidersBalance()` async function; envelope fields match `backend/routers/llm_balance.py::resolve_all_balances` verbatim (status / provider / currency / balance_remaining / granted_total / usage_total / last_refreshed_at / source / raw / stale_since / reason / message) so there is no translation layer. (2) `hooks/use-engine.ts` — new `providerBalances: ProviderBalanceEnvelope[] | null` state (null pre-poll → badge renders gray loading tier), **dedicated 60 s `useEffect` + `setInterval(fetchBalances, 60_000)`** separate from the existing 10 s dashboard-summary tick, `cancelled` closure + `clearInterval` cleanup, transient fetch failure keeps last-known envelope array (no flicker to null — matches the backend provider 5xx stale-cache policy), exposed in the hook return object. (3) `components/omnisight/token-usage-stats.tsx` — new `providerBalances?: ProviderBalanceEnvelope[] | null` prop + `<ProviderRollup>` now passes `renderStatusBadge(providerKey) → <ProviderStatusBadge>` + `renderExpansion(providerKey) → <ProviderCardExpansion>` that look up by lowercase `providerKey` (matches `groupByProvider`'s key convention + backend `_VALID_PROVIDER_NAMES` alphabetical registry); missing envelope (first poll pending, or non-registry key like the "local" AI_MODEL_INFO emits for Ollama) falls through to gray loading badge + null expansion — no crash, no wrong attribution. Rate-limit props (`rateLimitRemainingRequests` / `rateLimitLimitRequests` / `resetAtTs` / `retryAfterS`) are left undefined: the balance endpoint does not carry rate-limit data, and the Z.1 `SharedKV("provider_ratelimit")` dict has no REST surface as of this checkbox — badge + expansion `graceful degrade` contract from checkbox 1 / 2 handles missing-signal correctly (balance-only providers still compute, not yellow-by-default). (4) `components/omnisight/orchestrator-ai.tsx` — thin prop forward, no transform. (5) `app/page.tsx` — `providerBalances={engine.providerBalances}` wired on both `<OrchestratorAI>` mount sites (main layout + sidebar variant). **Why 60 s and not folded into dashboard-summary** (spec "5s 太密、單獨一個 60s 輪詢就夠、這是低頻資料"): (a) backend `llm_balance_refresher` only rewrites the SharedKV cache every 10 min — a 10 s poll would hit cached-identical data 59 out of 60 times and pay the auth middleware cost for nothing; (b) cold-start / cache-miss branches inside `resolve_balance` trigger a **live** vendor fetch (see `backend/routers/llm_balance.py` L284-308), so a 10 s cadence would amplify cold-starts into 6 concurrent vendor requests per minute — DeepSeek + OpenRouter would rate-limit us for the dashboard that exists to display rate-limit health, absurd; (c) balance is deliberately **not** SSE-driven because 10-min-cadence data doesn't warrant a persistent channel slot. Lock-tested by test case #4 below (50 s advance with fake timers triggers zero extra fetch; only the initial mount call survives). **Module-global state audit (SOP Step 1)**: zero new module-global mutable state. `providerBalances` is pure `useState`; the interval / `cancelled` closure / handle are all per-instance React state; cleanup on unmount is verified by test case #6. Cross-worker consistency is moot — each frontend tab owns its own poll, the backend SharedKV is the source of truth, duplicate GETs on the same cache key are idempotent. SOP audit answer #3 ("intentionally per-tab, not shared"). **Read-after-write audit**: N/A — pure client-side GET poll, zero write path, no serialisation changes, no concurrent-write race. **Tests (6 new cases in `test/hooks/use-engine-provider-balances.test.tsx`, 42 ms total)**: (1) `providerBalances === null` on first mount before the initial fetch resolves — badge can correctly render loading tier without a misleading green default; (2) first tick populates the array verbatim (status / balance_remaining / granted_total / currency / reason all surface); (3) second + third fetches land after each 60 s interval advance — lock for the spec-mandated cadence; (4) **negative assertion** — 50 s advance triggers zero extra fetch, guarding against a future accidental refactor that folds this into the 10 s dashboard-summary tick; (5) transient rejection preserves the last-known envelopes (no flicker); (6) hook unmount followed by 180 s advance leaves the count at 1 — `clearInterval` cleanup verified. Pattern mirrors `decision-dashboard.test.tsx` — vitest 4 + testing-library's `waitFor` have a known race with fake timers (waitFor polls via real timers, component runs on fake), so tests use `act` + manual 5-iteration microtask-flush instead of `waitFor`. Mock `getProvidersBalance` is reset in `beforeEach` to isolate call counts between cases (vitest's `restoreAllMocks` resets implementations but fake-timer interaction across tests made mockImplementation leak without the explicit reset). The existing `test/hooks/use-engine.test.tsx` file adds only `getProvidersBalance: vi.fn()` to its mock factory so mounting the hook doesn't crash on `api.getProvidersBalance is not a function`. **Regression sweep**: 113/113 green across Z.4 quartet (`provider-rollup` 42 + `provider-status-badge` 31 + `provider-card-expansion` 34 + this checkbox 6) in 1.0 s; 28/28 green across 5 token-usage-stats neighbour suites (cache / context / turn-stats / burn-rate / heatmap) — the new `renderStatusBadge` / `renderExpansion` slots do not regress any existing per-model card behaviour. `npx tsc --noEmit` — zero new type errors introduced; pre-existing `components/omnisight/integration-settings.tsx` errors remain (same baseline as checkboxes 1-4). The 15 pre-existing failures in `test/hooks/use-engine.test.tsx` (all complain "No fetchTurnHistory export is defined on the @/lib/api mock") are verified pre-existing via `git stash && npx vitest run` — not introduced by this checkbox. **Provider-key alignment audit**: `groupByProvider` lowercases `getModelInfo(...).provider` to build `providerKey`; matches backend `_VALID_PROVIDER_NAMES` (anthropic / deepseek / google / groq / ollama / openai / openrouter / together / xai) for 8 of 9. Sole mismatch: AI_MODEL_INFO reports Ollama's provider as "Local" → lowercase "local", backend uses "ollama" — pre-existing label mismatch from Z.4 checkbox 3, this checkbox's `.find(e => e.provider === providerKey)` naturally falls through to gray loading badge without mis-attribution. Follow-up (not blocking): align AI_MODEL_INFO Ollama label to "Ollama" or add a resolver alias. OpenRouter via checkbox 4's `openRouterAwareResolver` already lowercases to "openrouter" — clean handoff. **Scope discipline — what this checkbox does NOT do**: (a) no rate-limit endpoint polling — backend has no `/runtime/providers/ratelimit` surface today; the `SharedKV("provider_ratelimit")` dict (Z.1) populates rate-limit fields but needs a REST route before it can light up the badge's rate-limit tier; deferred to its own row. (b) no Ollama label alignment — pre-existing, documented, non-blocking. (c) no a11y enhancement (checkbox 6 — `<ProviderStatusBadge>`'s `aria-label` already carries concrete balance dollars from checkbox 1; this checkbox just feeds live props). (d) no Playwright visual regression (checkbox 7). (e) no new backend route — `GET /runtime/providers/balance` shipped in Z.2 already. Commit co-authors include env-user + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->
  <!-- Production status: dev-only — five files land on master but no prod Next.js deploy has happened since the merge. Next gate: deployed-active — pure client-side logic, rides the next Next.js frontend deploy cycle (~5 min, no image rebuild, no env knob, no feature flag). Once deployed, any operator with DeepSeek or OpenRouter credentials wired will see a coloured health chip next to each provider summary row (green/yellow/red/gray per balance % tiers from checkbox 1) plus a Balance / Last synced block when they expand a group. Deployed-active flips once at least one such operator confirms the badge colour + balance numbers match their vendor dashboard ground truth. Deployed-observed follows 24 h of no regression metric (no mis-attribution tickets, no "badge colour flipping randomly" reports). ETA deployed-active: next Next.js deploy cycle. -->
- [x] 無障礙：`aria-label` 包含 balance 具體數字（不要只有顏色 + icon）。
  <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.4-checkbox-6 shipped: minor robustness patch to `describeProviderStatus()` in `components/omnisight/provider-status-badge.tsx` (~35 LOC net) + new dedicated accessibility lockdown test file `test/components/provider-a11y.test.tsx` (~290 LOC, 44 cases, all green in ~150ms). **Problem this checkbox solves** (independent of checkbox 1's partial a11y coverage): the aria-label contract in checkbox 1 already included balance dollars for green/yellow/red tiers, but three edge branches silently degraded to colour-only copy: (a) gray no-data when balanceRemaining was a concrete absolute but grantedTotal was null (OpenRouter remainder-only shape) AND no rate-limit signal was present — compute lands in gray "no data" and the aria-label said "no balance or rate-limit data available", lying to screen-reader users about data that DID arrive; (b) gray with rate-limit counter only (no limit ceiling, so reqPct is null) — raw remaining count was computed but not surfaced in aria-label when it fell through to gray; (c) unsupported short-circuit with stale-cache balance data — currently drops the $ amount and only surfaces the "provider does not expose a public balance API" sentence. The Z.4 spec is explicit: "aria-label 包含 balance 具體數字（不要只有顏色 + icon）" — so ANY path where a concrete number is available must surface it in the label. **The fix** — `describeProviderStatus` now has a unified "concrete data extraction" block at the top (`hasBalance` / `hasGranted` / `hasReq` / `hasTok` booleans derived once per call) then: (1) unsupported branch appends `balance $X.XX [/ $Y.YY]` trailer when hasBalance; (2) gray no-data branch only says "no balance or rate-limit data available" when genuinely no concrete data exists — otherwise it surfaces whichever of $ amount / req count / tokens count is available; (3) ok/error tiers keep their existing concrete-number output; (4) all numeric-inclusion branches go through the shared helper block so a future refactor can't forget one branch. **Net behavioural diff** — for fully-populated providers: zero change (same aria-label). For gray edge cases (balance absolute without granted, OR rate-limit counter without limit ceiling, OR unsupported envelope with stale cache): aria-label now contains the concrete number the backend did report, per spec. **Module-global state audit (SOP Step 1)**: zero new module-global mutable state added. `describeProviderStatus` is a pure function — reads props and the already-pure `ProviderStatusResult`, emits a string. Every worker running the same code produces the same label for the same inputs (answer #1 of the three valid SOP audit answers). `hasBalance` / `hasGranted` / `hasReq` / `hasTok` are function-scope const booleans, not shared state. **Read-after-write audit**: N/A — no writes, no serialisation, no parallel operations. Pure client-side render-time text composition. **Tests — 44 new cases in `test/components/provider-a11y.test.tsx`**: (A) **matrix sweep** (27 cases = 9 providers × 3 tiers): for every provider in `_VALID_PROVIDER_NAMES` (anthropic / openai / google / xai / groq / together / deepseek / openrouter / ollama) × every non-gray tier (green / yellow / red), assert the aria-label contains every expected `$X.XX` balance figure + `$Y.YY` granted figure + `NNN req remaining` count — so drift on any single cell surfaces with provider-named failure message. Each sweep row also guards against the badge collapsing to just tier word or just provider name (`label.length > tier.length + provider.length + 5`). (B) **currency + edge cases** (9 cases): ¥ symbol for CNY; sub-dollar balance uses 3-decimal `$0.500`; absolute-without-granted still surfaces the $ amount and does NOT match the `^provider: (tier)\.?$` collapse pattern; rate-limit-only (Anthropic) surfaces raw counts; **gray no-data with concrete balance** lockdown (balanceRemaining=12.34, grantedTotal=null, no rate-limit → gray tier but label contains `$12.34`); **gray no-data with concrete rate-limit count** lockdown (rateLimitRemainingRequests=42 alone, no limit → gray tier but label contains `42 req remaining`); **gray TRULY-no-data** legitimate fallback ("no balance or rate-limit data available" is correct when nothing arrived — so the anti-regression above doesn't over-fire); unsupported + stale-cache balance still surfaces $ in label; loading state aria-label includes the word "loading"; anti-regression — aria-label NEVER equals only the tier word or `PROVIDER: TIER.`. (C) **ProviderCardExpansion row-level** (6 cases): balance row `aria-label` contains `$42.00` + `$100.00` and is NOT `"Balance:"` bare; rate-limit row `aria-label` contains `500 req remaining` + `50,000 tokens remaining`; missing balance row says `"Balance: not reported"` not empty; CNY row surfaces `¥88.00`; error envelope still surfaces concrete numbers in row aria-labels (stale-cache-on-error path); last-synced row always carries concrete timing ("1:00 ago" / "never"). (D) **cross-component integration** (1 case): badge + expansion report the same balance number when given identical data — guards against a future refactor that changes the formatter in only one component. **Regression sweep** — 186/186 green across 10 sibling suites (provider-a11y 44 + provider-status-badge 31 + provider-card-expansion 34 + provider-rollup 42 + 5× token-usage-stats 28 + use-engine-provider-balances 6) in 1.72s. `npx tsc --noEmit` clean for all Z.4 + provider + a11y code — pre-existing errors remain only in `components/omnisight/integration-settings.tsx` (baseline unchanged since checkbox 1). **Scope discipline — what this checkbox does NOT do** (defers to checkbox 7): (a) no Playwright visual regression — that's the Z.4 final row, covers 9-provider-all-configured + all-empty + mixed scenarios; this checkbox locks the aria-label a11y contract at the unit-test level so checkbox 7 can focus on pixel diffs; (b) no i18n / locale catalogue extraction — strings remain English ("balance" / "no balance or rate-limit data available" / "Balance: not reported") matching the existing Z.4 sibling copy; when the dashboard picks up i18n it moves all these strings into the catalogue together; (c) no WCAG colour-contrast audit — that's covered by the shared `var(--validation-emerald)` / `var(--critical-red)` / `#eab308` tokens that have already been verified against `bg-card`; (d) no rate-limit % threshold adjustment — aria-label reports whatever compute produced, unchanged; (e) no backend envelope schema change. Commit co-authors include env-user + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->
  <!-- Production status: dev-only — one-component patch + one new test file land on master; no prod Next.js deploy has picked up the commit yet. Screen-reader impact: operators using VoiceOver / NVDA / JAWS will hear concrete balance dollars for every provider × every tier on any page that mounts `<ProviderStatusBadge>` or `<ProviderCardExpansion>` — today that's only `<TokenUsageStats>` via `<ProviderRollup>` (wired by checkbox 5). Next gate: deployed-active — rides the next Next.js deploy cycle (~5 min, no image rebuild, no env knob, no feature flag, no backend change). Deployed-active flips once at least one operator confirms via accessibility audit tool (axe-devtools / Lighthouse a11y / macOS VoiceOver) that the badge + expansion aria-labels carry concrete $ amounts, not just tier colour words. Deployed-observed follows 24h with no a11y regression metric (no screen-reader-user-reported "can't hear the number" tickets, no Lighthouse a11y score drop). ETA deployed-active: next Next.js deploy cycle. -->
- [x] 視覺測試：Playwright 截圖 9 家 provider 全配好的情況 + 全空的情況 + 混合狀況。
  <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.4-checkbox-7 shipped: three-artefact delivery that closes out Z.4's final row. (A) `e2e/z4-provider-rollup-visual.spec.ts` (~240 LOC) — Playwright spec with three `test()` blocks, one per scenario (`fully-configured`, `all-empty`, `mixed`). Each scenario drives the dedicated fixture page via `?scenario=` and asserts DOM shape (group count, `data-provider-key` attributes, per-tier expansion contents) before taking two screenshots: `rollup-<scenario>.png` (just the `<ProviderRollup>` element) + `page-<scenario>.png` (full viewport). (B) `app/e2e-fixtures/provider-rollup/page.tsx` (~195 LOC) — minimal-surface client-only page that mounts `<ProviderRollup>` + `<ProviderStatusBadge>` + `<ProviderCardExpansion>` directly with hard-coded scenario fixtures. No `useEngine`, no SSE, no dashboard hooks — the fixture page sidesteps the entire app shell so the visual test isolates the component rendering from the data-pipeline noise that was causing intermittent TypeError crashes in the full-dashboard approach (minified `.length` error in `useEngine`'s downstream consumers when routes returned defaults they didn't expect). (C) `playwright.visual.config.ts` (~70 LOC) — dedicated Playwright config that boots ONLY the Next.js frontend (no FastAPI backend) because every `/api/v1/**` request is stubbed at the browser layer via `page.route()`. The base `playwright.config.ts` insists on booting the FastAPI backend, which on developer workstations whose `.env` carries real production credentials trips strict-mode env checks + db_pool init that's unrelated to what this visual spec is testing. **Determinism**: `Date.now()` frozen to `FROZEN_NOW_MS = 1777887600000` (2026-04-25T10:00:00Z) via `page.addInitScript` that overrides `Date` globally in the browser — fixture page's `last_refreshed_at` values are offset backwards so `formatLastSynced()` renders stable "15s ago" / "1:00 ago" / "10:00 ago" labels identical across runs. **Stubbing strategy**: only three `/api/v1/**` routes need fixtures (whoami → synthetic admin, tenants → `[]`, events → SSE keepalive); the rest fall through to a 404 default so consumers' error branches fire cleanly rather than the routes returning `{}` (which previously triggered `map is not a function` crashes in unrelated dashboard hooks — this failure mode is why the spec navigates to the isolated fixture page instead of `/?panel=orchestrator`). **Three scenarios locked**: (1) **fully-configured** — all 9 backend-registry balance envelopes (anthropic / deepseek / google / groq / ollama / openai / openrouter / together / xai), every one `status: "ok"` with healthy > 20% balance → all badges green. Assertions: 7 groups render (Groq + Together have no `AI_MODEL_INFO` resolver path + Ollama's "Local" label doesn't match backend's "ollama" envelope key — documented drift from checkbox 5 gets locked here); 6 expansions render (the `local` group has no matching envelope); Anthropic balance reads `$85.00 / $100.00`; DeepSeek renders the ¥ CNY symbol. (2) **all-empty** — balance envelope array is empty (`[]`, same shape as first-poll-yet-to-land), usage rows still present. Assertions: same 7 groups render (rollup shape is driven by model rows, not balance data); zero `<ProviderCardExpansion>` paints (renderExpansion returns null when envelope absent); every status slot still mounts (gray "no data" badge). (3) **mixed** — one envelope per tier: Anthropic green ($72), OpenAI yellow ($18 / $100), DeepSeek red ($6 / $200 CNY), xAI unsupported, OpenRouter error (401 Unauthorized), Ollama null/null green, Google/Groq/Together absent. Assertions: green/yellow/red balance values in expansion; xAI shows the unsupported-advisory block; OpenRouter shows the error-message paragraph; Google falls through to loading-badge + null expansion. **Module-global state audit (SOP Step 1)**: zero new module-global mutable state. The fixture page is a pure function of its search-params + hard-coded fixtures; the spec's `page.addInitScript` + `page.route` state is per-browser-context (scoped to each test's page instance, reset via `test.beforeEach(prepPage)`). Every spec run against the same commit produces the same rendered HTML → same pixel output → answer #1 of the three valid SOP audit answers. **Read-after-write timing audit**: N/A — visual regression spec, zero writes, zero parallel operations, zero serialisation. **Regression sweep**: 3/3 specs green in ~10s wall-clock on second run (no `next build` needed when the production build is cached); first run takes ~90s including `next build`. Six PNG artefacts land in `test-results/z4-provider-rollup-visual-*/` — two per scenario (rollup-only + full-page). `npx tsc --noEmit` clean for the new files (playwright config + spec file + fixture page all type-check; `e2e/` is already in `tsconfig.json::exclude` so spec doesn't enter the main compile). **Discovered + documented by this checkbox** (fix deferred — not a regression, pre-existing behavioural correctness): the `local` → `ollama` key mismatch already noted in checkbox 5's HANDOFF is now locked by a spec assertion (`test(fully-configured) … localGroup.getByTestId("provider-card-expansion").toHaveCount(0)`), so any future AI_MODEL_INFO realignment that flips "Local" to "Ollama" will red-fail the test and force an intentional update — the documented mismatch won't drift silently into + out of the UI. **Scope discipline — what this checkbox does NOT do**: (a) no `toHaveScreenshot()` / pixel baseline — the repo has no existing snapshot baseline in git and managing binary pixel diffs across CI runners (font rendering, sub-pixel AA) is out of scope; DOM assertions catch real regression without pixel fragility. The produced PNGs are human-review artefacts uploaded as `test-results/` in CI, not diff-tested. (b) no full dashboard path coverage — the fixture page bypasses the `/?panel=orchestrator` route; the dashboard's integration of `<ProviderRollup>` is already locked by the 42-case `test/components/provider-rollup.test.tsx` + 28-case `token-usage-stats-*` sibling suites from checkboxes 3/5. (c) no OMNISIGHT_PW_LIB_DIR shim in the spec — operators running on dev boxes without system-wide libnspr4 / libnss3 need to set it themselves (documented in spec header + playwright.config.ts); CI `npx playwright install --with-deps` has the libs by default. (d) no i18n / locale sweep — English strings only; when the dashboard picks up i18n every Z.4 visual test moves together. (e) no Playwright browser matrix (Firefox / WebKit) — chromium only, matching the base `playwright.config.ts` default; matrix expansion happens repo-wide via `OMNISIGHT_PW_BROWSERS=all`. Commit co-authors include env-user + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->
  <!-- Production status: dev-only — spec + fixture page + dedicated playwright config land on master; produces artefacts locally but no CI workflow references the new `playwright.visual.config.ts` yet. Next gate: deployed-inactive — add a GH Actions workflow step `playwright test --config=playwright.visual.config.ts` that uploads `test-results/z4-provider-rollup-*/` as a CI artefact (runtime ~10s on cached build, ~90s on cold), so PR diffs that touch `provider-rollup.tsx` / `provider-status-badge.tsx` / `provider-card-expansion.tsx` surface the three scenario screenshots for human review in the PR checks tab. Deployed-active follows when the workflow runs at least once per PR against Z.4/Z.5 sibling changes. ETA deployed-inactive: follow-up CI task (~15 min) — not required for Z.4 closure since the spec runs green locally and proves the UI renders correctly in all three data states today. -->

預估：**1 day**

### Z.5 Tests + 文件 + 支援度矩陣 (#294)

收尾階段：regression test + operator 文件。

- [x] `backend/tests/test_llm_balance.py`：DeepSeek + OpenRouter 各 3 test（ok / auth fail / 5xx），unsupported provider 1 test，SharedKV cache hit / miss 邏輯。
- [x] `backend/tests/test_pricing.py`：YAML load / reload / fallback / cross-worker sync 各一條。
  <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.5-checkbox-2 shipped: added `TestZ5PricingRegressionNet` (4 tests, ~150 LOC net) at the end of the existing `backend/tests/test_pricing.py` file (which already carries 69 Z.3 checkbox-3/4/5/6 tests — load / reload / fallback / cross-worker / boot-resilience / historical-cost preservation). **Scope of this checkbox**: the Z.5 spec says "YAML load / reload / fallback / cross-worker sync 各一條" — one canonical lock per dimension. The comprehensive matrices already live in the surrounding Z.3 classes; this slim net exists so a future contributor running `pytest -k TestZ5PricingRegressionNet` gets a sub-second smoke subset that trips on any of the four load-bearing invariants. **Mirrors the Z.5 `test_llm_balance.py` pattern**: slim canonical regression file that references the comprehensive suites exist elsewhere but locks the exact regression matrix from the Z.5 checkbox wording. **The four dimensions**: (1) **YAML load** — `test_yaml_load_parses_live_config`: `_load_pricing()` against the live `config/llm_pricing.yaml` must produce `_loaded_from_yaml=True`, exactly 9 registered providers (anthropic / openai / google / xai / groq / deepseek / together / openrouter / ollama), Opus 4.7 at ($5, $25) bit-identical to pre-Z.3, global defaults at ($1, $3) — catches schema drift, silently-routed-through-hardcoded regressions. (2) **reload** — `test_reload_picks_up_yaml_edits`: prime cache with live YAML, `monkeypatch` `_PRICING_PATH` to a temp file with novel rates, call `pricing.reload()` (non-HTTP path), verify `get_pricing("zvendor", "z-model-v1")` returns new rate AND the old Anthropic rate is gone (new global defaults kick in) — catches regressions where the cache is only half-invalidated or a second layer gets missed. (3) **fallback** — `test_fallback_to_hardcoded_when_yaml_unavailable`: point `_PRICING_PATH` at a non-existent file, call `reset_cache_for_tests()`, verify `_load_pricing()` does NOT raise (returns degraded cache with `_loaded_from_yaml=False` + empty providers map), Opus 4.7 + DeepSeek chat still bill at historical rates via `_HARD_CODED_FALLBACK`, unknown provider+model falls through to `_HARD_CODED_GLOBAL_DEFAULT = (1.0, 3.0)` — catches the two historical bug shapes (FileNotFoundError propagation, pre-Z.3 models silently collapsing to global defaults). (4) **cross-worker sync** — `test_cross_worker_sync_invalidates_peer_cache`: locks three sub-properties in one test — (a) `_on_pricing_reload_event` is registered in `shared_state._pubsub_callbacks` at import time, (b) `PRICING_RELOAD_EVENT == "pricing_reload"` (wire-format string); (c) dispatching a `pricing_reload` event through the full callback list (exactly what `start_pubsub_listener` does) nulls `_PRICING_CACHE` on the peer worker; paired negative — unrelated events (`"sse"`, `"_halt"`, `"_resume"` all ride the same bus) must NOT disturb the cache. **Module-global state audit (SOP Step 1)**: zero new module-global mutable state; tests use the autouse `_reset_cache` fixture (already present from Z.3) that calls `reset_cache_for_tests()` before + after each test, so `_PRICING_CACHE` and `_FALLBACK_WARN_TS` start + end pristine. Cross-test isolation matches the per-worker audit answer #1 ("derived from shared static source"): every test that loads the YAML does so from a known-input file (live + tmp) and observes the same deterministic parse.
