The cross-worker callback list (`shared_state._pubsub_callbacks`) is intentionally module-global and shared across the test process — the test treats it as the production fan-out surface it is, consistent with the per-worker registration semantics. **Read-after-write audit**: N/A — no writes to any shared state, pure cache read/invalidate contract. **Pre-commit fingerprint grep** (SOP Step 3): clean — 0 hits on `_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]`. **Test result**: 73/73 green in 4.24s on the full `test_pricing.py` file (69 pre-existing + 4 new); 157/157 green across pricing + context_limits + shared_state neighbour suites in 4.42s — 0 regressions. `test_llm_balance.py` collection error is pre-existing (`respx` missing from `backend/requirements.txt`) and unrelated to this checkbox. **Scope discipline — what this checkbox does NOT do** (deferred to sibling Z.5 rows): (a) no `test_ratelimit_capture.py` changes (Z.5 row 3, separate checkbox); (b) no `docs/integrations/llm-observability.md` support-matrix docs (Z.5 row 4, separate checkbox); (c) no HANDOFF before/after table (Z.5 row 6, separate checkbox); (d) no new module-level API in `backend/pricing.py` — the spec calls for tests only, not production surface changes. The comprehensive Z.3 suite kept in the same file (69 tests spanning TestFallbackStrategy / TestFallbackWarningEmission / TestFallbackWarningThrottle / TestPriceNeutralityAcrossFallbacks / TestCrossWorkerCallbackRegistration / TestReloadPricingEndpoint / TestPricingTableHelper / TestPricingSnapshotEndpoint / TestYamlCorruptBootResilience / TestCrossWorkerReloadIntegration / TestHistoricalCostPreservation) remains the authoritative contract surface; the Z.5 regression net is the canonical smoke subset. Commit co-authors include env-user + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->

- [x] `backend/tests/test_ratelimit_capture.py`：四家 provider 的 header 解析、TTL 過期、unknown provider 跳過。
  <!-- 2026-04-25 Z.5-checkbox-3 shipped: added `TestZ5RatelimitRegressionNet` (3 tests, 6 cases via parametrize, ~150 LOC net) at the end of the existing `backend/tests/test_ratelimit_capture.py` file (which already carries 18 Z.1 checkbox-5 cases — 4-provider end-to-end normalise + SharedKV mirror + 60 s TTL + unknown-provider skip across 5 classes: TestFourProviderNormalise / TestFourProviderSharedKVWrite / TestFourProviderCoexistence / TestFourProviderTTLExpiry / TestUnknownProviderSkipsEndToEnd). **Scope of this checkbox**: the Z.5 spec says "四家 provider 的 header 解析、TTL 過期、unknown provider 跳過" — one canonical lock per dimension. The comprehensive matrices already live in the surrounding Z.1 classes; this slim net exists so a future contributor running `pytest -k TestZ5RatelimitRegressionNet` gets a sub-second smoke subset that trips on any of the three load-bearing invariants. **Mirrors the Z.5 `test_pricing.py` + `test_llm_balance.py` pattern**: slim canonical regression class that references the comprehensive suites exist elsewhere but locks the exact regression matrix from the Z.5 checkbox wording. **The three dimensions**: (1) **Header parse (4 providers)** — `test_four_provider_header_parse`, parametrised over the 4 providers Z.5 calls out (anthropic / openai / deepseek / openrouter) feeding realistic vendor-shaped headers (Anthropic native `anthropic-ratelimit-*` + ISO 8601 absolute reset, OpenAI duration-string `"14ms"` / `"8.64s"`, DeepSeek bare numeric-seconds, OpenRouter minute-duration `"1m"`); asserts (a) the unified `{remaining_requests, remaining_tokens, reset_at_ts, retry_after_s}` contract on `cb.last_ratelimit_state` AND (b) byte-identical mirror under `SharedKV("provider_ratelimit")[provider]` via `fresh_kv.get_with_ttl(provider) == cb.last_ratelimit_state` — catches mapping schema drift (`_PROVIDER_RATELIMIT_HEADERS` vendor rename, accidental drop of one of the 4 providers, in-memory↔KV divergence). (2) **TTL expiry** — `test_ttl_expiry_60s_end_to_end`, `monkeypatch.setattr(time, "time", lambda: 1000.0)` write via real `on_llm_end` round-trip with Anthropic headers; live at t+30s (`get_with_ttl(now=1030.0) is not None`); pruned at t+61s (`get_with_ttl(now=1061.0) is None`); destructive-prune assert (`get(default="__missing__") == "__missing__"`); locks the constant `_RATELIMIT_TTL_SECONDS == 60.0` to catch a silent retune (someone bumps it to 5 min for "convenience" and breaks the dashboard's freshness guarantee). (3) **Unknown provider skip** — `test_unknown_provider_skip_preserves_siblings`, Ollama turn (provider absent from `_PROVIDER_RATELIMIT_HEADERS`) interleaved between live anthropic + openai turns; (a) `set(snap.keys()) == {"anthropic", "openai"}` — no key under "ollama"; (b) sibling payloads intact with their correct `remaining_requests` (987 for anthropic / 9998 for openai). Catches both "wrote {} under unknown key" and "wiped sibling on every turn" regressions. **Module-global state audit (SOP Step 1)**: zero new module-global mutable state; tests reuse the existing `fresh_kv` fixture (per-test `monkeypatch.setattr(_llm_mod, "_ratelimit_kv_singleton", None)` + purge in-memory residue) + `silent_track_tokens` fixture (per-test `monkeypatch.setattr(_sys, "track_tokens", ...)` stub). `time.time` monkeypatch is scoped to the individual test via pytest's `monkeypatch` fixture (auto-rolled-back at teardown) — no cross-test clock pollution. Per the SOP rubric: answer #1 ("derived from shared static source") for the static `_PROVIDER_RATELIMIT_HEADERS` mapping every worker re-derives, and answer #3 ("deliberately per-worker") for the in-memory SharedKV fallback the test exercises (Redis-coordinated in production per the SharedKV contract). **Read-after-write audit**: N/A — single-process test; the SharedKV writes are read by the same test thread via `get_with_ttl` / `get_all_with_ttl` on the same KV instance; no asyncio gather, no cross-worker timing. **Pre-commit fingerprint grep** (SOP Step 3): clean — 0 hits on `_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]`. **Test result**: 24/24 green in 0.50s on the full `test_ratelimit_capture.py` (18 pre-existing Z.1 + 6 new Z.5); 165/165 green across the five rate-limit sibling suites (`test_ratelimit_capture` + `test_ratelimit_header_extract` + `test_ratelimit_header_normalize` + `test_ratelimit_kv_write` + `test_ratelimit_boundaries`) in 0.79s — 0 regressions. **Scope discipline — what this checkbox does NOT do** (deferred to sibling Z.5 rows): (a) no `docs/integrations/llm-observability.md` support-matrix docs (Z.5 row 4, separate checkbox); (b) no operator UI / reload / budget-alarm docs (Z.5 row 5, separate checkbox); (c) no HANDOFF before/after table for Z completion (Z.5 row 6, separate checkbox); (d) no production code changes — the spec calls for tests only, no new module-level API in `backend/agents/llm.py`. The comprehensive Z.1 suite kept in the same file (18 cases spanning the 5 classes above) remains the authoritative contract surface; the Z.5 regression net is the canonical smoke subset.   | xAI        | ❌          | ✅                 | |
  | Groq       | ❌          | ✅                 | |
  | DeepSeek   | ✅          | ✅                 | /user/balance |
  | Together   | ❌          | ⚠️                | |
  | OpenRouter | ✅          | ✅                 | /auth/key 含 usage + credit_balance |
  | Ollama     | N/A         | ❌                 | 本地部署無餘額概念 |

The doc's intro explicitly flags "Downstream checkboxes in Z.5 layer an operator how-to on top — see Z.5 row in TODO.md for sibling scope boundaries" so a future reader lands on the right row without re-discovering the decomposition. **Matrix fidelity — cross-reference against live runtime registries**: (1) `_PROVIDER_RATELIMIT_HEADERS` in `backend/agents/llm.py:117-165` carries exactly 7 providers — anthropic / openai / xai / groq / deepseek / together / openrouter — matching the ✅ + ⚠️ rows in the matrix. Google is intentionally absent (inline comment at `backend/agents/llm.py:160-164` explains langchain-google-genai adapter does not surface x-goog-quota-* headers) — this is what the ⚠️ in the matrix's Google row refers to. Ollama is absent (local deployment, no remote rate) — matches the ❌ in the matrix's Ollama row. (2) `SUPPORTED_BALANCE_PROVIDERS` in `backend/llm_balance.py:342-348` carries exactly 2 providers — deepseek / openrouter — matching the ✅ rows in the matrix's Balance API column; the `is_balance_supported()` predicate at `backend/llm_balance.py:351-358` is the runtime check that drives the `{status: "unsupported"}` envelope the router returns for the other 7. (3) `config/llm_pricing.yaml:30-93` carries exactly 9 providers — the 7 ratelimit + google (no ratelimit) + ollama (no remote anything) — matching the 9 rows of the matrix. **Section 2 ("What the two columns actually mean")** is essential because the raw matrix is ambiguous without definitions: ✅ / ⚠️ / ❌ / N/A each need a runtime criterion a future maintainer can apply when adding a provider. ✅ = "API-key auth to a public HTTP endpoint" (the registry gate); ⚠️ = "signal appears for some models / SDK versions / request shapes"; ❌ = "provider exposes the data but only behind auth path OmniSight does not implement" (session cookie, GCP OAuth, dashboard scrape — cross-references the TODO Non-goals); N/A = "signal is definitionally meaningless" (local provider). **Section 3 ("How the matrix maps to the dashboard")** bridges the developer-visible matrix to the operator-visible UI: Balance column → Providers panel Balance column; Rate-Limit column → Providers panel Rate-limit badge; non-grey rows count toward the Z.4 roll-up tile denominator (Ollama excluded so local-only deployments do not see permanent "1/9 healthy" red). **Section 4 ("Adding a new provider")** gives the 4-step recipe a future contributor needs: pricing YAML → rate-limit headers mapping → balance fetcher → matrix row. Ordered the same as the Z.1 / Z.2 / Z.3 checkbox sequence so "add a provider" is a recognisable mini-Z. **Section 5 ("Related files")** lists the 7 files a reader might want to cross-reference — the two registries (authoritative truth), the pricing YAML, the two lifespan-scoped components (refresher + endpoint router), the two test files. **Module-global state audit (SOP Step 1)**: N/A — pure docs, no production code, no tests. **Read-after-write audit**: N/A. **Pre-commit fingerprint grep** (SOP Step 3): clean — 0 hits on `_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]` (expected — it's a markdown file). **Consistency with existing docs**: matches the style of `docs/integrations/jira-automation.md` (the only other file under `docs/integrations/`) — H1 title, "> Y-prep.3 (#289) — operator-facing reference" lead-in pattern, numbered H2 sections, tables with leading/trailing pipes, back-ticks for code identifiers. Used the same front-matter-style `> Z.5 (#294) checkbox 4 — canonical reference` so operators can locate the provenance without grepping. **Doc discipline — what the 165 LOC deliberately does NOT become**: (a) no line-by-line walkthrough of `_extract_response_headers` parse logic (that's the test file's job); (b) no example `curl` calls against each provider's balance endpoint (vendor-specific, drift-prone — the fetcher source code is canonical); (c) no per-model latency / cost benchmarks (future Z-follow-up if ever); (d) no vendor dashboard URL clickthrough list (deferred to Z.5 row 5 "常見 provider dashboard 連結清單" — a deliberate rough edge so the next checkbox has a concrete deliverable). Last-verified timestamp + instruction to re-verify on provider add/rename/remove is the ONE operator burden the doc creates — everything else is cross-referable from the runtime registries. Commit co-authors include env-user (Agent-software-beta) + global-user per CLAUDE.md. -->

預估：**0.5 day**

**Z 總預估**：**3.5 day**

**出貨節奏建議**：
- Day 1：Z.1（半天）+ Z.2（半天）— rate-limit header 開始收集，DeepSeek + OpenRouter 兩家 balance 可查
- Day 2：Z.3（半天）+ Z.4（一半）— YAML 上線，UI 開始顯示 balance badge
- Day 3：Z.4 剩半天 + Z.5 tests + 文件 — 收尾

Day 1 結束就已經比現況好一大截（operator 能看到兩家的真實餘額 + rate-limit），後面三天主要補 UI 完整度 + 保護網。

**Non-goals**（明確不做）：
- ❌ Dashboard HTML scraping / session cookie 拿更多 provider 的 balance（fragile + ToS 風險）
- ❌ 預測性自動加值（留給 T 系列 billing 做）
- ❌ Provider 之間自動 arbitrage 切換（留給 decision engine 做）
- ❌ 跨 tenant 的 provider 憑證隔離（Y4 + Phase 5b 才做）
- ❌ Budget 消耗預測（需要 time-series model，目前只做現值顯示）

**和 Priority Y / T 的關係**：
- Z.2 的 `/providers/balance` endpoint 將是 T（Billing）計費決策的**輸入源**之一——T4 可以觀察「某 tenant 把某 provider credit 燒完了」發通知。
- Z.4 的 UI roll-up 在 Y4 project 層落地後，會擴充成「project-level provider spend breakdown」，但 Z 自身只做 global view。

---

## 🆉🆉 Priority ZZ — Claude-Code-Style Agent Observability（把 agent 黑盒開成玻璃盒）

> 背景（2026-04-22 operator audit）：operator 評估了 [lis186/ccxray](https://github.com/lis186/ccxray)（Claude Code transparent proxy + 儀表板，把 Claude Code 的 LLM 通訊 intercept 下來當「玻璃盒」展示：per-turn 卡片、context window bar、cache hit %、inter-turn gap、tool failure、system prompt diff、session heatmap、burn rate）——請求把所有適用的功能搬進 OmniSight。OmniSight 目前只有 `components/omnisight/token-usage-stats.tsx` 的 per-model 卡（input / output / total / cost / req count / avg latency / last used），看不到單一 turn 的故事、看不到 cache 效率、看不到 context 接近上限。
>
> ZZ 和 Z（LLM Provider Observability，餘額 / rate-limit）是姊妹：**Z 解「provider 那邊還剩什麼」，ZZ 解「我這端怎麼燒」**。兩者共用既有 `TokenTrackingCallback` + `SharedTokenUsage` infra，但輸出維度不同——Z 是 per-provider summary、ZZ 是 per-turn timeline + session heatmap + prompt diff。
>
> **落地位置**：擴充既有 `components/omnisight/orchestrator-ai.tsx`（ORCHESTRATOR AI 面板，已 mount `<TokenUsageStats>`，是 dashboard 上 "看 LLM 思考 + token" 的主要視窗），而非新開獨立 panel——避免和 TokenUsageStats 重複。
>
> **相依**：`TokenTrackingCallback`（backend/agents/llm.py）✅ / `SharedTokenUsage`（shared_state.py）✅ / `prompt_versions` table（SP-5.2 已落地）✅ / `GraphState.tool_results`（agents/graph.py）✅ / I2 RLS ContextVar ✅。硬相依零。
>
> **明確排除的 ccxray 功能**（Non-goals）：
> - ❌ **Plan detection (Pro / Max 5x / Max 20x)**——OmniSight 用 API key 直連 provider，沒有「Claude Code 訂閱 plan」概念；類似「還有多少 credit」的需求由 Z.2 (DeepSeek / OpenRouter balance API) 涵蓋。
> - ❌ **瀏覽器頁籤 pulse + desktop notif**——UX 裝飾，非 core observability；若後續想加就走 Priority Q 或獨立小項。
> - ❌ **鍵盤導覽 cheatsheet**——通用好點子但不屬 ZZ 範圍；已有 `components/omnisight/command-palette.tsx` 處理部分這類需求。
> - ❌ **Transparent proxy / 外部 HTTP intercept**——ccxray 的招牌架構只在「我不控制 agent，要從外部觀察」時有意義；OmniSight 本來就擁有 agent 執行流，直接從 `TokenTrackingCallback` 埋點比 proxy 更準確、無流量開銷。

### ZZ-Wave-A Wave A — 現成資料、前端顯示 (#303)

後端 response 裡已經有、只是前端沒渲染的四個維度。做完後 `<TokenUsageStats>` 卡片多四條資訊且一眼看得懂。

#### ZZ.A1 Cache read / create 拆分 (#303-1)
- [x] `backend/agents/llm.py::TokenTrackingCallback.on_llm_end()`：改讀 response body 的 `usage.cache_read_input_tokens` + `usage.cache_creation_input_tokens`（Anthropic 欄位名，OpenAI 類的走 `usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens` — 雙 provider shape normalise 在 callback 內）。
- [x] `SharedTokenUsage.track()` 欄位擴充：`cache_read_tokens` + `cache_create_tokens` + `cache_hit_ratio = cache_read / (input + cache_read)`；pg migration 加欄位到 `token_usage` 表，既有 row `cache_read = NULL` 代表 pre-ZZ 資料。 <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A1 checkbox 2: alembic `0024_token_usage_cache.py` adds three NULLABLE columns (`cache_read_tokens` INTEGER / `cache_create_tokens` INTEGER / `cache_hit_ratio` REAL|DOUBLE) dialect-safely (PG `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`, SQLite PRAGMA-probe); pre-ZZ rows keep NULL as the "no data" marker per spec. `SharedTokenUsage.track()` gains two kwargs (`cache_read_tokens=0` / `cache_create_tokens=0`) — defaulting to 0 keeps every existing caller compiling; `_apply_token_delta` accumulates lifetime totals and recomputes `cache_hit_ratio = cache_read / (input + cache_read)` with guard for denom==0; `_normalize_token_entry` preserves NULL on pre-ZZ Redis payloads so `get_all()` surfaces `None` (distinguishes "no data" from "genuine zero hits"); fresh entries via `_fresh_token_entry` start at 0 (ZZ-era semantics authoritative from turn 1); the first ZZ-era track() on a legacy row upgrades NULL→numeric. `backend/routers/system.py::track_tokens` gains matching kwargs + mirror accumulation in the in-memory dict + plumbs through to SharedTokenUsage. `backend/agents/llm.py::TokenTrackingCallback.on_llm_end` now passes `cache_read` / `cache_create` from `_extract_cache_tokens` into `track_tokens`, closing the loop from provider response → Postgres `token_usage.cache_*` columns. `backend/db.py`: schema SQL + `upsert_token_usage` signature extended to 11 positional params with per-field `int_or_none` / `float_or_none` helpers so None round-trips as SQL NULL. 7 new regression tests in `test_shared_state.py::TestSharedTokenUsage` (ZZ-era record / accumulate / zero-default / zero-denom guard / NULL-on-pre-ZZ / upgrade path / fresh-zero) + 2 new tests in `test_db_token_usage.py` (cache column round-trip + NULL preservation). Smoke: end-to-end Anthropic (cache_read=800, cache_create=120 → hit_ratio 0.8) and OpenAI (cached_tokens=300 → hit_ratio 0.230769) response shapes both land canonically in `_token_usage`. 68/68 shared_state + token_budget + migrator_schema_coverage tests green, 0 regression. Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- [x] `GET /runtime/tokens` response schema 加新欄位；前端 `token-usage-stats.tsx::ModelTokenUsage` interface 擴充對應欄位。 <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A1 checkbox 3: backend `/runtime/tokens` endpoint (`backend/routers/system.py::get_token_usage`) now declares `response_model=list[TokenUsageEntry]`; new Pydantic model `TokenUsageEntry` in `backend/models.py` exposes the 8 canonical fields plus the three ZZ.A1 prompt-cache fields (`cache_read_tokens` / `cache_create_tokens` / `cache_hit_ratio`) as `Optional[int|float]` defaulting to `None` — preserves the pre-ZZ "NULL = no data" contract end-to-end while letting ZZ-era rows publish lifetime totals. `scripts/dump_openapi.py` regenerated `openapi.json` (now advertises the schema; `lib/generated/api-types.ts::components.schemas.TokenUsageEntry` auto-emitted via `openapi-typescript`). Frontend `lib/api.ts::TokenUsage` interface extended with the matching three snake_case fields typed `number | null`; `components/omnisight/token-usage-stats.tsx::ModelTokenUsage` interface extended with camelCase equivalents (`cacheReadTokens` / `cacheCreateTokens` / `cacheHitRatio`, same nullable semantics). Placeholder rows in `token-usage-stats.tsx` (synthesised for configured-but-unused providers, 2026-04-21 behaviour) seed the three new fields to `null` so the yet-to-be-built cache UI (ZZ.A1 checkbox 4) renders "—" instead of a misleading 0% badge for wired-but-silent models. `app/page.tsx` (both call-sites for `<OrchestratorAI tokenUsage=…>`) plumbs the three fields through the `input_tokens → inputTokens` snake→camel mapper. `tsc --noEmit` baseline 3 pre-existing errors in `integration-settings.tsx`; post-change: still 3 — 0 new type errors. 65/65 `test_shared_state.py + test_token_budget.py + test_db_token_usage.py` tests green. Round-trip smoke (`TokenUsageEntry(**fresh/legacy/populated entries)`): fresh ZZ entry → `cache_*=0` + `hit_ratio=0.0`, pre-ZZ legacy → `cache_*=None` + `hit_ratio=None`, populated ZZ (800 read / 120 create / 1000 input) → `hit_ratio=0.444444`. SOP fingerprint grep clean (no `_conn()` / `conn.commit()` / SQLite-isms). Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- [x] UI 卡片 Row 2 新增兩條進度條：「CACHE HIT 72%」（綠 > 50% / 黃 20-50% / 紅 < 20%）+ tooltip 顯示 `read: 12.5k / write: 1.2k`；raw 數字放 hover。 <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A1 checkbox 4: `components/omnisight/token-usage-stats.tsx` gains a new "Row 3b" cache block per model card rendered after the existing usage bar (Row 3) and before Input/Output Tokens (Row 4) — keeps cache info in the visual hierarchy "percent-of-total → cache effectiveness → raw counts". **Bar 1 (CACHE HIT)**: width = `cacheHitRatio * 100`, color banded per spec — `> 50` `var(--validation-emerald)`, `>= 20` `#eab308` (yellow-500), `< 20` `var(--critical-red)` (boundary semantics: strict `>` at 50 puts the 50.0 edge on yellow; `>= 20` keeps 20.0 on yellow, matches "綠 > 50 / 黃 20-50 / 紅 < 20" literal reading). Label reads "CACHE HIT" + right-aligned `N%` percent in the band color. **Bar 2 (CACHE WRITE)**: width = `cache_create / (input + cache_read + cache_create) * 100` capped at 100 — surfaces "cache-creation overhead paid this window" in `var(--hardware-orange)/70` (neutral-warm, non-alarm). Label reads "CACHE WRITE" + right-aligned K-formatted raw count. **NULL degradation** (pre-ZZ legacy rows + synthesised `configuredProviders` placeholder cards): all three cache fields come through as `null` → percent renders as literal em-dash `"—"` instead of `0%`, color falls back to `var(--muted-foreground)`, both bars render at `0%` width on an empty rail. This makes "no data" visually distinct from a real zero hit-rate per ZZ.A1 contract (the whole reason the backend preserves NULL on pre-ZZ rows). **Tooltips**: container-level `title` carries the verbose raw form (`cache_read_tokens=12512 cache_create_tokens=1203 cache_hit_ratio=0.7200`) satisfying "raw 數字放 hover"; per-bar `title` carries the K-formatted summary (`read: 12.5k / write: 1.2k`) matching the spec example — operators hovering anywhere on the section get the exact counts, hovering on a bar gets the quick glance. `data-testid` attributes (`cache-hit-section` / `cache-hit-pct` / `cache-hit-bar` / `cache-hit-bar-rail` / `cache-write-bar` / `cache-write-bar-rail`) seed the not-yet-written frontend vitest (next checkbox row) without introducing a test dep now. No API schema change — reads `item.cacheHitRatio` / `item.cacheReadTokens` / `item.cacheCreateTokens` already present on `ModelTokenUsage` from checkbox-3. `tsc --noEmit` baseline 3 pre-existing errors in `integration-settings.tsx` (TS2322 `GitForgeTokenTestResult` vs `TestResult` index-signature mismatch, unrelated); post-change: still 3 — 0 new type errors in `token-usage-stats.tsx` or any consumer. Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->

- [x] 測試：`test_token_usage_shared.py` 新 3 顆（cache shape normalise Anthropic vs OpenAI、NULL 相容、hit ratio 計算）；前端 vitest 新 2 顆（badge 顏色 ×3 區間、NULL 降級顯示「—」）。 <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A1 checkbox 5 (tests row): new file `backend/tests/test_token_usage_shared.py` scopes the three ZZ.A1 regression guards the spec calls out — (1) `TestCacheShapeNormalise::test_normalise_anthropic_vs_openai_shape` feeds both provider response shapes (Anthropic flat `cache_read_input_tokens`/`cache_creation_input_tokens` and OpenAI nested `prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens`) through `TokenTrackingCallback._extract_cache_tokens` and asserts both land as int-typed `(read, create)` tuples (OpenAI's missing creation side normalises to 0 so downstream callers don't branch); (2) `TestNullCompat::test_get_all_surfaces_null_for_pre_zz_row` seeds a legacy Redis-shaped dict with no `cache_*` keys and confirms `SharedTokenUsage.get_all()` returns `None` for all three cache fields (not 0 — that's the whole point of the "distinguish legacy from genuine-zero" contract, so the UI can render "—"); (3) `TestHitRatioCalculation::test_hit_ratio_anthropic_and_openai_land_same_formula` locks `hit_ratio = cache_read / (input + cache_read)` end-to-end for both providers — Anthropic 800/(200+800)=0.8, OpenAI 300/(1000+300)≈0.230769 — so a future formula refactor can't silently drift. New frontend file `test/components/token-usage-stats-cache.test.tsx` scopes the two UI regression guards — (1) `CACHE HIT badge colour bands` renders three `ModelTokenUsage` rows (0.72/0.35/0.10 → 72% green > 50 / 35% yellow 20-50 / 10% red < 20) and asserts both the `[data-testid='cache-hit-pct']` text + `style.color` and the matching `[data-testid='cache-hit-bar']` fill colour land on `var(--validation-emerald)` / `rgb(234,179,8)` (jsdom-normalised #eab308) / `var(--critical-red)` per band — boundary semantics `>50` + `>=20` matches the spec reading; (2) `NULL degradation renders em-dash` feeds a pre-ZZ row with `cacheReadTokens/cacheCreateTokens/cacheHitRatio = null`, asserts the pct label renders literal "—" in `var(--muted-foreground)` (explicitly NOT any of the three band colours — "no data" must not masquerade as a bad hit rate), the fill bar width degrades to `0%`, and the section-level `title` attribute carries the "no cache data" tooltip. **Test results**: 3/3 backend tests green, 2/2 frontend tests green. Full `test_token_usage_shared + test_shared_state + test_token_budget + test_db_token_usage` suite: 68 passed, 10 skipped (PG-only `test_db_token_usage` — unchanged baseline), 0 regression. Frontend `metric-sparkline + token-usage-stats-cache` co-run: 6 passed. SOP Step 4 (unit tests + fingerprint grep) clean — no `_conn()` / `conn.commit()` / SQLite-isms in new files. No prod code touched in this row — all lifting is in the two new test files alone. Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- 預估：**0.5 day**

#### ZZ.A2 Context window usage bar (#303-2)
- [x] 新 `config/context_window_limits.yaml`（和 Z.3 的 pricing YAML 同位置），覆蓋 9 家主要 model 的 context 上限（e.g. Claude 4 = 1M, GPT-4o = 128k, Gemini Pro = 2M, DeepSeek = 64k, Groq = 32k, Ollama = 模型相依）。 <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A2 checkbox 1: new file `configs/context_window_limits.yaml` (placed alongside `configs/provider_pricing.yaml` per "和 Z.3 的 pricing YAML 同位置"; spec text says `config/` but the actual project dir is `configs/` — chose the real path so the forthcoming `backend/context_limits.py` can mirror `backend/forecast.py::_PRICING_PATH = _PROJECT_ROOT / "configs" / "provider_pricing.yaml"` pattern exactly, else we get asymmetric YAML locations nobody remembers). Structure is `provider → model_id → int | null` with a per-provider `default` row so the next-row `get_context_limit(provider, model) -> int | None` can do a two-step lookup (exact → provider-default → None). **All 9 providers from provider_pricing.yaml covered** (anthropic / openai / google / xai / groq / deepseek / together / ollama / openrouter) keeping the two YAMLs in 1:1 sync so nobody has to reconcile "this provider exists in one file but not the other" later. **Values authoritative as of 2026-04 per vendor docs**: Claude 4.x family (opus-4-7 / sonnet-4-6 / haiku-4-5) = 1M; Claude 3.x = 200k; GPT-5 family = 256k; GPT-4o family = 128k; o1/o3 reasoning = 200k; Gemini 2.5 Pro / 1.5 Pro = 2M; Gemini 2.5 Flash / 2.0 Flash / 1.5 Flash = 1M; Grok 4 = 256k, Grok 3/2 = 131072; Groq-hosted Llama-3.3/3.1-versatile = 131072, Mixtral-32768 = 32768, Gemma = 8192; DeepSeek V3/R1 = 65536; Together hosts multi-vendor OSS, common routes listed (Llama 3.x = 131072, Qwen 2.5 = 32768, DeepSeek V3 = 65536, Mixtral = 32768); Ollama local models listed with upstream max (llama3.3/3.1 = 131072, llama3 = 8192, mistral = 32768, qwen2.5-coder = 32768, etc.) **with `default: null`** because local `num_ctx` is operator-configurable — matches spec "Ollama = 模型相依" and lets UI render "—" instead of a fake %; OpenRouter same pattern (`default: null`) because it's a pass-through proxy and the real limit depends on the routed-to model, listed common routes (anthropic/openai/google upstream) so the happy path still gets a real %. **NULL semantics**: `default: null` → callee (ZZ.A2 checkbox 2's `backend/context_limits.py::get_context_limit`) returns `None` → UI (ZZ.A2 checkbox 4's Row 3 progress bar) must degrade to "—" in muted-foreground per the same NULL-vs-genuine-zero contract ZZ.A1 already established for cache fields. **Env-var escape hatch**: Ollama block comment flags `OMNISIGHT_OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LIMIT` as the per-deployment override knob so operators running tighter `num_ctx` than upstream max don't get a misleading bar — exact env-var plumbing lands in ZZ.A2 checkbox 2 (where the Python loader has the code surface to honour it). **Validation**: 9/9 expected providers present, every entry `int > 0` or (for `default`) `int > 0` or literal `None`, per-vendor sanity-checked against spec (`claude-opus-4-7 == 1_000_000`, `gpt-4o == 128_000`, `gemini-2.5-pro == 2_000_000`, `deepseek.default == 65_536`, `groq.default == 32_768`, `ollama.default is None`). Total entries: 92 across 9 providers (anthropic 12, openai 13, google 8, xai 7, groq 9, deepseek 6, together 9, ollama 14, openrouter 14). No Python loader or UI consumer wired yet — intentionally scope-limited to "data file only", per SOP "only complete the marked checkbox, no scope creep". Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- [x] `backend/pricing.py`（Z.3 建立的）加 sibling `backend/context_limits.py::get_context_limit(provider, model) -> int | None`。 <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A2 checkbox 2: new file `backend/context_limits.py` (~130 LOC) — spec text says "sibling to backend/pricing.py" but the actual pricing loader lives in `backend/forecast.py::_load_pricing()` (Z.3 never factored out a standalone `pricing.py`), so "sibling" is read as "same pattern, same YAML directory convention, same cache discipline" — module sits next to forecast.py under `backend/`, mirrors the `_PROJECT_ROOT / "configs" / "*.yaml"` path anchor, and reuses the lazy-global cache idiom (`_LIMITS_CACHE` filled on first call, reset_cache_for_tests hook for test isolation). **Public API**: single function `get_context_limit(provider: str | None, model: str | None) -> int | None` with the two-step lookup the YAML header documents — exact model-id under provider → provider-level `default` → None. Unknown provider, empty/None provider, `default: null`, and blank inputs all route to `None` so downstream callers can unambiguously render "—" per the ZZ.A1-established NULL-vs-genuine-zero contract. Provider key is lowercased so `Anthropic` / `ANTHROPIC` / `anthropic` all resolve (real-world callers hand us env-var casing). Model key is case-sensitive — `together` / `openrouter` use slash-path keys like `meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-Turbo` whose case matters to the upstream API, matching the YAML as authored. **Ollama env override**: `OMNISIGHT_OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LIMIT` (flagged in `configs/context_window_limits.yaml` comment as the per-deployment escape hatch) is honoured in `get_context_limit` — when set on `provider=='ollama'`, the env value beats both the YAML's per-model entry and the `default: null`. Invalid values (empty / non-numeric / ≤ 0) silently fall through to YAML so a typoed knob does not zero-out the UI. Override is strictly scoped to `provider=='ollama'` — asserts no leakage to anthropic/openai in the test suite. **Loader robustness**: missing YAML, corrupt YAML, and null entries all coerce to `None` rather than raising — the forecast loader's same defensive posture ("pricing.yaml missing should not kill the forecast endpoint" → "limit lookup missing should not kill the turn_metrics SSE emitter"). Module-global state audit per SOP Step 1: `_LIMITS_CACHE` is module-level but derived purely from a bundled, read-only YAML — each uvicorn worker computes the same value from the same file, matches the "derived from shared static source" escape hatch (answer 1 of the three valid answers), docstring records the decision. **Tests** (`backend/tests/test_context_limits.py`, 25 cases in 6 TestCase classes): exact-match for the four headline models (claude-opus-4-7=1M, gpt-4o=128k, gemini-2.5-pro=2M, quoted-slash keys for together + openrouter) + provider-default fallback (unknown model, empty model, None model) + NULL semantics (ollama unknown, openrouter unknown, unknown-provider, empty/None provider) + case-insensitive provider (Anthropic/OPENAI) + Ollama env override (beats known model, beats NULL default, scoped to ollama, garbage ignored, empty ignored, zero ignored) + YAML loader robustness (missing-file degrades to None, corrupt YAML degrades to None, null entries coerce to None via a custom tmp_path YAML). All 25/25 green. Neighbour sanity: `test_forecast.py` 6/6 co-green, 31/31 total. Pre-commit fingerprint grep (`_conn()|await conn.commit()|datetime('now')|VALUES.*?[,)]`): 0 matches in both new files. **Scope discipline**: file is strictly the lookup helper — next-row ZZ.A2 checkbox 3 will wire it into `TokenTrackingCallback` + SSE `turn_metrics`; checkbox 4 into the UI progress bar. No wiring in this commit. Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- [x] per-turn 記錄 `tokens_used / context_limit` → 當前 turn 的 context usage % 推回 SSE event `turn_metrics`。 <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A2 checkbox 3: new SSE event `turn_metrics` wires the just-landed ZZ.A2 checkbox-1 YAML (`configs/context_window_limits.yaml`) + checkbox-2 loader (`backend/context_limits.py::get_context_limit`) into the ZZ.A1 token-callback pipeline. **Schema** (`backend/sse_schemas.py`): new `SSETurnMetrics` Pydantic model with 10 payload fields (`provider` / `model` / `input_tokens` / `output_tokens` / `tokens_used` / `context_limit` / `context_usage_pct` / `latency_ms` / `cache_read_tokens` / `cache_create_tokens`) + `timestamp` auto-stamped by the bus. `tokens_used` is the pre-computed `input + output` sum so the UI progress bar numerator is one field-read away; `context_usage_pct` is the pre-computed `round(tokens_used / context_limit * 100, 2)` percentage. Registered under `SSE_EVENT_SCHEMAS["turn_metrics"]` so the runtime schema export (`GET /api/v1/runtime/sse-schema`) advertises it for the frontend codegen. **Emit helper** (`backend/events.py::emit_turn_metrics`): matches the existing `emit_*` family signature — explicit `broadcast_scope=None` default + `_resolve_scope("emit_turn_metrics", ..., "global")` legacy fallback so post-Q.4 strict mode (`OMNISIGHT_SSE_SCOPE_STRICT=1`) stays green when the callback passes `broadcast_scope="global"` explicitly. **NULL-vs-genuine-zero contract**: `context_limit=None` (unknown provider/model / Ollama without env override / OpenRouter pass-through) → `context_usage_pct=None` (NOT 0.0 — the whole point of the NULL contract ZZ.A1 established for prompt-cache fields is that the UI can render "—" distinguishable from a real 0% turn). `context_limit <= 0` also degrades to `None` to defend against ZeroDivisionError / nonsense percentages. **Callback wire-up** (`backend/agents/llm.py`): `TokenTrackingCallback.__init__` gains optional `provider: str | None = None` kwarg (back-compat — legacy `TokenTrackingCallback(model_name)` fixtures keep working, limit lookup returns None); `get_llm` callsite passes the resolved provider through so post-failover (`chain[fallback_provider]`) the provider string is authoritative. `on_llm_end` after the existing `track_tokens(...)` call: imports `get_context_limit` + `emit_turn_metrics` late (avoids import-cycle with `routers.system`), computes the limit, emits with `broadcast_scope="global"`. Emit is wrapped in its own try/except with `logger.debug` on failure — an emit hiccup must never break the LLM turn. **Tests** (`backend/tests/test_turn_metrics_sse.py`, 6 cases): canonical payload shape (happy path, 10 fields + timestamp) / NULL context_limit degrades pct to None / zero-limit defensive branch / end-to-end callback via stubbed `track_tokens` + fake LLMResult (claude-opus-4-7 → 1M limit → 25.00% pct) / back-compat callback without provider (limit=None, pct=None, tokens_used still populates) / schema registry drift guard (`SSE_EVENT_SCHEMAS["turn_metrics"] is SSETurnMetrics` + 10 required field names present). **Schema drift guard** (`backend/tests/test_schema.py::TestSSESchemaExport::test_all_events_have_schemas`): expected-set updated to include `turn_metrics` (and the pre-existing Q.3-SUB family `workflow_updated` / `notification.read` / `preferences.updated` / `integration.settings.updated` / `chat.message` which were pre-existing drift — without them the test was already red before this change, listing them here is the minimum needed to keep the drift guard green after the new entry lands). **Test results**: 6/6 new tests green; neighbour sanity 55/55 across `test_turn_metrics_sse + test_token_usage_shared + test_context_limits + test_events_bus + test_token_budget + test_schema.py::TestSSESchemaExport`; 0 regression. Smoke: `get_sse_schema_export()` round-trip includes `turn_metrics` with `SSETurnMetrics` title + 11 properties keyed; `emit_turn_metrics("claude-opus-4-7", input_tokens=10000, output_tokens=2000, latency_ms=1234, provider="anthropic", context_limit=1_000_000, broadcast_scope="global")` lands `context_usage_pct=1.20` on the bus. `scripts/dump_openapi.py --check` clean — SSE schema export is runtime-only (not part of OpenAPI), so no snapshot regen needed. Module-global state audit per SOP Step 1: no new module-global state introduced — `emit_turn_metrics` is stateless, `SSETurnMetrics` is a Pydantic class definition, `TokenTrackingCallback.provider` is an instance field (per-request scoped), `SSE_EVENT_SCHEMAS` is a read-only registry dict initialised at import. SOP Step 3 fingerprint grep (`_conn()|await conn.commit()|datetime('now')|VALUES.*?[,)]`): 0 matches in `backend/agents/llm.py`, `backend/events.py`, `backend/sse_schemas.py`, `backend/tests/test_turn_metrics_sse.py`. Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- [x] UI 卡片 Row 3 加水平 progress bar + 百分比：<50% 綠 / 50-75% 黃 / 75-90% 橙 / >90% 紅脈動動畫；hover 顯示「123k / 200k tokens」。 <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A2 checkbox 4: `components/omnisight/token-usage-stats.tsx` gains a new "Row 3a (context window)" block per model card, rendered between the existing Row 3 (`% of total usage` cross-model proportion bar) and Row 3b (cache hit/write bars from ZZ.A1 checkbox 4). Self-contained data flow — no use-engine wiring needed: the component subscribes to the shared `EventSource` via `subscribeEvents(...)` in a `useEffect` (cleaned up on unmount via `handle.close()`) and maintains a local `Record<modelLowercase, { tokensUsed, contextLimit, contextUsagePct }>` keyed by the same lowercased-model convention `usedModels` already uses. **Snapshot semantics**: backend emits `turn_metrics` SSE per-turn (ZZ.A2 checkbox 3), and the component keeps only the *latest* per model — lifetime totals would balloon as the agent keeps talking and lose the "is THIS turn near the cap" signal the bar exists to surface. **Color bands per spec**: `pct < 50` → `var(--validation-emerald)` (green) / `< 75` → `#eab308` (yellow-500) / `< 90` → `var(--hardware-orange)` (orange) / `>= 90` → `var(--critical-red)` (red) with Tailwind `animate-pulse` on the fill div for the >= 90 band — boundary semantics use strict `<` upper / inclusive `>=` lower so 50.0 → yellow, 75.0 → orange, 90.0 → red, mirroring the cache-bar band reading. **Hover tooltip**: rail-level `title` carries the spec-mandated `"123k / 200k tokens"` form via `formatTokens(tokensUsed) / formatTokens(contextLimit) tokens` (e.g. `"45.0K / 1.0M tokens"` for a Claude turn at 45k/1M); when `context_limit` is `null` (Ollama local without env override / OpenRouter pass-through / unknown provider) the tooltip degrades to `"N tokens used (context limit unknown for this provider/model)"` so operators see the limit-unknown reason rather than an em-dash; when no `turn_metrics` SSE has landed yet (fresh card / configured-provider placeholder) the tooltip says `"no context data — no turn_metrics seen yet"`. **NULL degradation**: percent label renders literal `"—"` in `var(--muted-foreground)` and the bar fill renders `0%` width on an empty rail — same NULL-vs-genuine-zero contract ZZ.A1 established for cache fields, so a "no data" card is visually distinct from a real 0% turn (e.g. an immediately-after-reset card where the agent literally sent zero tokens). **SSE wiring** (`lib/api.ts`): new `turn_metrics` entry added to both the `SSEEvent` discriminated-union (carries `provider/model/input_tokens/output_tokens/tokens_used/context_limit/context_usage_pct/latency_ms/cache_read_tokens/cache_create_tokens/timestamp` matching `backend/sse_schemas.py::SSETurnMetrics` field-for-field, with the two `*_limit/_pct` fields nullable) and the `SSE_EVENT_TYPES` whitelist (`addEventListener` only registers the listed types; without this entry the shared `EventSource` would silently drop `turn_metrics` frames before any subscriber sees them). **`data-testid` attributes** seeded for the next-checkbox-row vitest (`context-usage-section` / `context-usage-pct` / `context-usage-bar` / `context-usage-bar-rail`) following the same convention the cache section uses for `token-usage-stats-cache.test.tsx`. **Type baseline**: `tsc --noEmit` shows the same 3 pre-existing `integration-settings.tsx` errors as the ZZ.A1 baseline (TS2322 `GitForgeTokenTestResult` vs `TestResult` index-signature mismatch, unrelated); 0 new type errors in `token-usage-stats.tsx` or `lib/api.ts`. Cache test (`test/components/token-usage-stats-cache.test.tsx`, 2/2) co-runs green — no regression. Module-global state audit per SOP Step 1: `_sharedES` / `_sseListeners` in `lib/api.ts` are existing module-globals (not new) — they're per-frontend-process only (browser tab), not per-uvicorn-worker, so the SOP escape hatch doesn't apply (the worker-fan-out concern is backend-only). Component-local `contextByModel` is per-mount React state (per-tab, not global). Scope discipline: warning icon on >90% (next checkbox) and frontend tests for color bands + NULL degradation (checkbox after) intentionally NOT touched — strict "only complete the marked checkbox, no scope creep". Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- [x] 卡片頂部如果任一最近 turn > 90% context 用量，加警告 icon + tooltip「Context 接近上限，agent 可能 truncate」。 <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A2 checkbox 5: `components/omnisight/token-usage-stats.tsx` gains a card-top warning icon next to the "TOKEN USAGE" header text. **Logic**: scans the component-local `contextByModel` state (populated by the ZZ.A2 checkbox-4 `turn_metrics` SSE subscriber) and collects models whose latest-turn `contextUsagePct >= 90`; when at least one matches, renders an `AlertTriangle` (lucide-react) in `var(--critical-red)` with Tailwind `animate-pulse` so the signal is visible at a glance and even when the panel is collapsed (the existing Row 3a bar also pulses red on >=90 per checkbox 4, but a collapsed panel hides it — the header icon is the always-visible analogue). **Tooltip** (`title` attr, ``Context 接近上限，agent 可能 truncate — <offending models>``) matches spec string literally and appends the offending model list so operators know which card to expand; `aria-label` carries the English translation for a11y. **NULL degradation**: `contextUsagePct === null` (Ollama without env override / OpenRouter pass-through / unknown provider / no SSE seen yet) is explicitly excluded from the trigger — "no data" MUST NOT fire the alarm, same NULL-vs-genuine-zero contract ZZ.A1 / ZZ.A2 established for cache + context fields. **Placement**: inside the header `<button>` so it tracks the expand-toggle click target (clicking the icon still toggles the panel, matches operator intuition "click anywhere on the header row"); wrapped in a `<span>` carrying the `title` + `data-testid` because `title` on SVG elements has inconsistent browser tooltip behaviour. **`data-testid="context-critical-warning"`** seeded for next-checkbox-row test (ZZ.A2 checkbox 6 `警告 icon 條件`). `AlertTriangle` added to the lucide-react import list. `tsc --noEmit` baseline 3 pre-existing errors in `integration-settings.tsx`; post-change: still 3 — 0 new type errors. Neighbour test `test/components/token-usage-stats-cache.test.tsx` 2/2 co-green (no DOM shuffle in header cache selectors). Module-global audit per SOP Step 1: no new module-global state — `criticalContextModels` / `hasCriticalContext` are per-render derived values; `contextByModel` is existing component-local `useState` from checkbox 4 (per-mount React state). Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- [x] 測試：limit lookup fallback、NULL model 處理、UI 顏色區間、警告 icon 條件。 <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A2 checkbox 6: backend + frontend test coverage matrix for ZZ.A2 locked. **Backend (limit lookup fallback + NULL model處理)**: `backend/tests/test_context_limits.py` (25 cases, landed w/ ZZ.A2 checkbox 2) already covers `TestExactMatch` (4 headline models + slash-path keys) + `TestProviderDefaultFallback` (unknown-model/empty/None-model falls to provider-level `default`) + `TestNullSemantics` (`default: null` → None, unknown provider → None, None/empty provider → None) + `TestProviderCaseInsensitive` (Anthropic/ANTHROPIC) + `TestOllamaEnvOverride` (6 cases: beats known, beats null, scoped to ollama, garbage/empty/zero fall-through) + `TestYamlLoaderRobustness` (missing/corrupt/null-entry YAML). `backend/tests/test_turn_metrics_sse.py` (6 cases, landed w/ ZZ.A2 checkbox 3) covers the SSE emit contract under NULL limit. Scope discipline: this row does NOT duplicate those — they exist and pass. **Frontend (UI 顏色區間 + 警告 icon 條件)**: new file `test/components/token-usage-stats-context.test.tsx` (8 cases in 3 describe blocks). `TokenUsageStats — context-window bar colour bands` (2): 4-band sweep `25/60/80/95 → green/yellow/orange/red` with pulse check (only the 95% row carries `animate-pulse`; green/yellow/orange must NOT pulse else the alarm signal loses meaning) + boundary-sentinel sweep `50/75/90 → yellow/orange/red` locking strict `<` upper-edge semantics (50 is NOT green, 75 is NOT yellow, 90 is NOT orange — the same "right edge moves up a band" reading ZZ.A1 used for cache). `TokenUsageStats — context-window NULL degradation` (2): Ollama-shape `context_limit=null, context_usage_pct=null` renders literal `"—"` in `var(--muted-foreground)` (explicitly NOT any of the four band colours — NULL must never masquerade as a real band), bar width degrades to `0%`, bar does NOT pulse, rail tooltip surfaces `"context limit unknown"` + fresh-card (no SSE seen yet) renders `"—"` with tooltip `"no turn_metrics seen yet"`. `TokenUsageStats — card-top warning icon trigger condition` (4): 89.99% + 45% sibling → NO icon (inclusive `>=90` means 89.99 is still safe + near-miss sentinel guards a future off-by-0.01 regression) / 92% + 45% mixed → icon fires + tooltip names only the offending model (ZZ.A2-5 contract: operator must know WHICH card to expand, tooltip must NOT cry wolf over a comfortable sibling) / NULL `pct=null` with 10M raw tokens → NO icon (NULL-vs-genuine-zero contract: "no data" MUST NOT fire the alarm, the whole point of preserving None in the backend) / drop-back semantics: 95% → icon fires → next snapshot 30% → icon clears (latest-turn snapshot semantics, not lifetime — verifies the `setContextByModel` state updates drive the derived `hasCriticalContext`). **Infra pattern**: uses `test/helpers/sse.ts::primeSSE` (same pattern as `mode-selector.test.tsx` / `integration-settings-sse.test.tsx`) to mock `@/lib/api::subscribeEvents`, push synthesised `turn_metrics` events via the listener capture, wrapped in `act(...)` so React 18 batches the state updates before assertion. Model rows are seeded via `externalUsage={rows}` + `totalTokens` ordering tricks so the DOM order matches `pctNodes[0..3]` assertions (component sorts by totalTokens DESC). **Test results**: 8/8 new tests green. Co-run with ZZ.A1 cache test (`token-usage-stats-cache.test.tsx`): 10/10 passed, 0 regression. Backend neighbour sanity: `test_context_limits + test_turn_metrics_sse` 31/31 green (25 context_limits + 6 turn_metrics). **Module-global audit per SOP Step 1**: no new module-global state introduced by the test file — `vi.mock("@/lib/api", ...)` hoists per-file, `primeSSE` captures listeners in a closure per `primeSSE(api)` call, `beforeEach` resets the mock between cases. Component-local `contextByModel` is existing per-mount React state from ZZ.A2 checkbox 4 (not module-global). **Fingerprint grep** (`_conn()|await conn.commit()|datetime('now')|VALUES.*?[,)]`): 0 matches in the new test file. Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- 預估：**0.5 day**

#### ZZ.A3 Inter-turn gap + tool call stats (#303-3)
- [x] `TokenTrackingCallback.on_llm_start` 記 turn 起點 timestamp；`on_llm_end` 記終點；兩者差即「LLM 運算時間」。兩個 turn 之間的差是「tool 執行 + event bus 排程 + 等 context gather」時間——併 `SharedTokenUsage.track()` 的 `turn_started_at` / `turn_ended_at`。 <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A3 checkbox 1: threaded per-turn LLM boundary stamps (`turn_started_at` / `turn_ended_at`, ISO-8601 UTC microsecond precision) from `TokenTrackingCallback` through `track_tokens` → `SharedTokenUsage.track` → in-memory / Redis / PG row end-to-end. **Callback** (`backend/agents/llm.py`): `__init__` gains `self._start_ts_utc = ""`; `on_llm_start` sets it via `datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()` alongside the existing `self._start` float (keeps back-compat for `latency_ms` computation); `on_llm_end` captures `turn_ended_at` as close to the `track_tokens()` call as possible (after `_extract_cache_tokens`, before the ZZ.A2 SSE emit) so the stored stamp reflects "LLM call completed", not "bookkeeping done". Both stamps land as kwargs on `track_tokens(..., turn_started_at=..., turn_ended_at=...)` — `self._start_ts_utc or None` ensures a callback that never saw `on_llm_start` degrades to None (partial-knowledge-caller contract) rather than fabricating `""`. **Router** (`backend/routers/system.py::track_tokens`): two new optional kwargs with `None` default; the in-memory `_token_usage[model]` fresh dict adds `turn_started_at: ""` / `turn_ended_at: ""` (fresh ZZ-era row, no turn yet); the apply-delta block OVERWRITES (not accumulates) when a kwarg is provided — last-turn-snapshot semantics, essential for the downstream gap computation (`this_turn.started - last_turn.ended`). `None` kwargs leave the stored value alone so legacy callers (rule-based fallback, test fixtures) don't clobber a previously populated stamp. Plumbs through to `_token_usage_shared.track(...)`. **SharedTokenUsage** (`backend/shared_state.py`): (1) `_fresh_token_entry` adds `turn_started_at: ""` / `turn_ended_at: ""` — fresh ZZ rows start `""` (authoritative from turn 1). (2) `_normalize_token_entry` applies the same NULL-vs-genuine-zero contract ZZ.A1 established for `cache_*` fields: absent on legacy payloads → `None` (dashboard renders "—"), present → preserved. (3) `_apply_token_delta` gains two kwargs; overwrites iff not None — same partial-knowledge semantics as router. (4) `SharedTokenUsage.track` signature extended with both kwargs, plumbed identically through the Redis and in-memory branches. **Persistence** (`backend/db.py`): `token_usage` schema CREATE TABLE adds `turn_started_at TEXT` / `turn_ended_at TEXT` (NULLable, no DEFAULT — same discipline as ZZ.A1 cache columns); `upsert_token_usage` bumped to 13 positional placeholders with `_str_or_none` helper that coerces empty-string → SQL NULL so pre-first-turn fresh rows persist cleanly rather than polluting the column with sentinel `""`. **Migration** (`backend/alembic/versions/0025_token_usage_turn_timestamps.py`): dialect-safe ADD COLUMN (PG `IF NOT EXISTS`, SQLite PRAGMA-probe) mirroring ZZ.A1's `0024_token_usage_cache.py` exactly; downgrade is a defensive no-op (dropping loses accumulated data + breaks hot workers). **Module-global state audit per SOP Step 1**: no new module-global state. `_token_usage` (routers/system.py) pre-existing; per-worker PG-reload-on-startup via `load_token_usage_from_db` already established (escape hatch #1 — derived from shared PG source). `SharedTokenUsage._local` is instance-scoped (not module-global). `TokenTrackingCallback._start_ts_utc` is instance-scoped per request. **Tests** (`backend/tests/test_turn_boundary_stamps.py`, 6 cases in 4 TestCase classes): `TestCallbackCapturesBothBoundaries` (2) — fresh instance starts empty-string + `on_llm_start` sets parseable tz-aware ISO-8601 UTC; end-to-end callback plumbs both stamps to track_tokens via monkeypatched `track_tokens` + stubbed `LLMResult` (Anthropic shape), asserts `end >= start` + both tz-aware. `TestSharedTokenUsageOverwriteSnapshot` (1) — two consecutive track() calls leave latest pair in the entry; canonical counters (input_tokens/request_count) still ACCUMULATE (proves split contract holds). `TestNullCompatPreZzRow` (2) — legacy payload (no turn_* keys) surfaces None through get_all() while cache_* ZZ.A1 fields stay intact; fresh ZZ row starts `""` (not None), upgrades to real ISO stamp on first stamped track(). `TestPartialKnowledgeCallerPreservesPriorStamp` (1) — stamped call followed by no-stamp call preserves prior stamps (not clobbered to None) while counters still increment. **Test results**: 6/6 new tests green. Neighbour sanity: `test_shared_state + test_token_usage_shared + test_token_budget + test_turn_metrics_sse + test_turn_boundary_stamps + test_schema + test_context_limits + test_db_token_usage` → 121/121 green, 13 PG-only skipped (unchanged baseline). `test_llm_adapter + test_llm_errors + test_hmi_llm + test_migrator_schema_coverage` → all green (one pre-existing unrelated bootstrap test failure confirmed via `git stash` bisect — `db_pool.get_pool called before init_pool` in `test_bootstrap_llm_provision.py`, unrelated to this change). **SOP Step 3 fingerprint grep** (`_conn()|await conn.commit()|datetime('now')|VALUES.*?[,)]`): 0 matches in `shared_state.py` / `routers/system.py` / `agents/llm.py` / new migration / new test file. The pre-existing `db.py` hits are all other tables' `(datetime('now'))` DEFAULTs + pre-existing `_conn()` compat wrapper — none touched by this change (new `turn_*` columns are NULLable with no DEFAULT, consistent with ZZ.A1 discipline). **Scope discipline**: `TokenUsageEntry` pydantic model + `/runtime/tokens` response schema + frontend consumer NOT touched — that's ZZ.A3 checkbox 3's UI "avg gap 320ms" column; this checkbox scopes strictly to "record in SharedTokenUsage.track()" per spec wording. ZZ.A3 checkboxes 2-5 (GraphState tool stats, UI row 3 mini-columns, p95 outlier, matrix test) intentionally untouched. Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- [x] `GraphState.tool_results`（`agents/graph.py`）統計 `tool_call_count` + `tool_failure_count`（`result.error is not None`），每 turn 結束時推 SSE event 附帶。 <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A3 checkbox 2: new SSE event `turn_tool_stats` wires turn-end tool-execution aggregates out of `GraphState.tool_results` to the frontend. **Schema** (`backend/sse_schemas.py::SSETurnToolStats`): 5-field payload (`agent_type` / `task_id` / `tool_call_count` / `tool_failure_count` / `failed_tools`) + bus-auto `timestamp`. Registered under `SSE_EVENT_SCHEMAS["turn_tool_stats"]` so `/runtime/sse-schema` export advertises it for frontend codegen. **Emit helper** (`backend/events.py::emit_turn_tool_stats`): matches the existing `emit_*` family signature — explicit `broadcast_scope=None` default + `_resolve_scope("emit_turn_tool_stats", ..., "global")` legacy fallback so `OMNISIGHT_SSE_SCOPE_STRICT=1` stays green when the summarizer passes `broadcast_scope="global"` explicitly. `failed_tools` preserved in insertion order + with duplicates (a 3-retry loop on the same tool surfaces as 3 entries, matches the red "failed N" badge to actual retry attempt count, not a de-duped set). Logs at `warn` level when `tool_failure_count > 0`, `info` otherwise, so REPORTER VORTEX surfaces the failing-turn badge. **Wire-up** (`backend/agents/nodes.py::summarizer_node`): computes `tool_call_count = len(state.tool_results)` + `failed_tools = [r.tool_name for r in state.tool_results if not r.success]` at the top of the node (before the pass-through early-return), then emits. **Semantic mapping**: spec text says `result.error is not None` but the actual `ToolResult` class (`backend/agents/state.py`) models failure as `success: bool` (the tool executor sets `success=False` on any `[ERROR]` / `[BLOCKED]` / `[TIMEOUT]` prefix or raised exception) — the LangGraph shape's equivalent of `error is not None`, documented in the schema docstring + emit helper + test file so future readers understand the mapping. **Zeroed snapshot on pass-through**: the emit runs BEFORE the `if state.answer and not state.tool_results: return {}` early-return so a conversational turn after a failing tool turn still pushes `tool_call_count=0 / tool_failure_count=0` — clears any stale "failed N" badge the UI card might be carrying from a prior turn (without this, the red badge would stick across turn boundaries on the same agent card). **Turn semantics**: "turn end" = `summarizer_node` execution (last node before `END` in `backend/agents/graph.py::build_graph`), runs exactly once per `run_graph` call via the `context_gate → summarizer → END` edge. **Module-global state audit per SOP Step 1**: no new module-global state introduced — `emit_turn_tool_stats` is stateless, `SSETurnToolStats` is a Pydantic class at module import, `SSE_EVENT_SCHEMAS` is a read-only registry dict. Summarizer-side computation is per-call (derived purely from the passed-in `GraphState` instance). **Emit hardened**: wrapped in try/except with `logger.debug` on failure — an emit hiccup must never break the turn's answer synthesis. **Tests** (`backend/tests/test_turn_tool_stats_sse.py`, 5 cases): emit helper canonical payload shape / summarizer end-to-end with mixed success+failure batch (4 tools, 2 failures, in-order `failed_tools`) / zeroed snapshot on pass-through (no tools, 0/0 payload) / duplicate-preserving `failed_tools` (3× `run_bash` failure surfaces as 3 entries) / schema registry drift guard (`SSE_EVENT_SCHEMAS["turn_tool_stats"] is SSETurnToolStats` + 5 required field names present). Plus `backend/tests/test_schema.py::TestSSESchemaExport::test_all_events_have_schemas` expected-set updated to include `turn_tool_stats`. **Test results**: 5/5 new tests green. Neighbour sanity: `test_turn_tool_stats_sse + test_turn_metrics_sse + test_schema::TestSSESchemaExport + test_nodes + test_events_bus + test_verification_loop + test_permission_errors + test_platform_tags_for_rag` → 96/96 green, 0 regression. **SOP Step 3 fingerprint grep** (`_conn()|await conn.commit()|datetime('now')|VALUES.*?[,)]`): 0 matches across `backend/sse_schemas.py`, `backend/events.py`, `backend/agents/nodes.py`, new test file, updated test file. **Scope discipline**: UI Row 3 mini-columns ("tools 5 / failed 1" badge, ZZ.A3 checkbox 3) + p95 outlier highlight (ZZ.A3 checkbox 4) + matrix test (ZZ.A3 checkbox 5) intentionally untouched — strict "only complete the marked checkbox, no scope creep". Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- [x] UI 卡片 Row 3 右側加迷你兩欄：「avg gap 320ms」+「tools 5 / failed 1」（紅字 badge 若 failed > 0）。 <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A3 checkbox 3: landed the Row 3 companion mini-stats block on the per-model TokenUsageStats card. **Frontend SSE plumbing** (`lib/api.ts`): new `turn_tool_stats` entry in both the `SSEEvent` discriminated-union (carries `agent_type` / `task_id` / `tool_call_count` / `tool_failure_count` / `failed_tools` / `timestamp`, mirroring `backend/sse_schemas.py::SSETurnToolStats` field-for-field, `task_id` nullable) and the `SSE_EVENT_TYPES` whitelist — without the whitelist entry the shared `EventSource` `addEventListener` loop would silently drop the frame before any component saw it (same gotcha that bit `turn_metrics` in ZZ.A2 checkbox 4). **Component** (`components/omnisight/token-usage-stats.tsx`): `useRef` added to imports; extended the existing `useEffect` subscription (landed in ZZ.A2 checkbox 4 for `turn_metrics`) to also track per-model turn history + attribute `turn_tool_stats` to the most-recently-seen model. Two new state maps + one ref: `turnHistoryByModel: Record<modelLowercase, {ts, latency}[]>` — rolling buffer of the last `HISTORY_DEPTH=10` turns' parsed `timestamp` epoch-ms + `latency_ms`, used for avg gap; `toolStatsByModel: Record<modelLowercase, {callCount, failureCount, failedTools}>` — latest `turn_tool_stats` per model; `lastMetricsModelRef` — the model from the most-recent `turn_metrics`, used to attribute the immediately-following `turn_tool_stats` (summarizer doesn't carry a model field; since summarizer_node fires after on_llm_end in the same graph run the ordering holds — best-effort heuristic, documented in the source as "would need backend SSETurnToolStats.model to tighten up with concurrent agents"). **Avg gap formula** per ZZ.A3 checkbox 1 spec: `gap_i = (t[i].ts - t[i-1].ts) - t[i].latency_ms` → "tool exec + event bus scheduling + context gather" time; averaged over the buffer; needs ≥2 turns to produce a value, else renders "—" (NULL-vs-genuine-zero contract ZZ.A1 established). Negative-gap guard (`g >= 0`) filters clock-skew / out-of-order deliveries. **Render** (after Row 3 usage bar, before Row 3a context): new right-aligned flex row `[avg gap Xms | tools N / failed M]` in `text-[9px] font-mono text-[var(--muted-foreground)]` matching the existing Row 2 micro-metadata typography. **Red badge**: the "failed M" digit is wrapped in a `<span>` that gains `text-[var(--critical-red)] font-semibold` iff `hasToolStats && failureCount > 0` — matches spec "紅字 badge 若 failed > 0". A zero-failure turn (like the pass-through zeroed snapshot emitted by summarizer after a conversational turn) renders "failed 0" in muted-foreground, NOT red — so the badge doesn't cry wolf on green turns, and a stale red badge from a prior failing turn gets cleared on the next pass-through (the zeroed-snapshot behaviour ZZ.A3 checkbox 2 specifically designed for). **NULL degradation**: pre-ZZ cards with no SSE data yet (synthesised `configuredProviders` placeholders / fresh cards / legacy payloads) render `avg gap —` + `tools — / failed —` muted. **`data-testid` attributes** seeded for the ZZ.A3 checkbox 5 vitest (`turn-mini-stats` / `turn-avg-gap` / `turn-tool-count` / `turn-failed-count`) — tests for gap formula + failure-count red badge + p95 outlier land in the next checkbox row. **Tooltips**: `avg gap` carries "Average gap between LLM turns (tool exec + event bus + context gather)" — matches the spec's verbatim framing so ops know what the number represents. `tools / failed` carries the failed-tool-name list inline when `failureCount > 0` (e.g. "tools 5 / failed 1 — run_bash") so a hover on the card tells the operator WHICH tool retried, not just a count. When no `turn_tool_stats` has landed yet, tooltip reads "no turn_tool_stats seen for this model yet" — distinct from "data arrived but counts are zero". **Module-global state audit per SOP Step 1**: no new module-global state. `turnHistoryByModel` / `toolStatsByModel` are component-local `useState` (per-mount); `lastMetricsModelRef` is component-local `useRef` (per-mount). Existing `_sharedES` / `_sseListeners` in `lib/api.ts` are pre-existing module-globals scoped to the browser tab (not per-uvicorn-worker) — the SOP escape hatch doesn't apply (worker-fan-out is a backend concern). **`tsc --noEmit`**: baseline 3 pre-existing errors in `integration-settings.tsx` (TS2322 `GitForgeTokenTestResult` vs `TestResult` index-signature mismatch, documented in prior ZZ.A1 / ZZ.A2 checkbox notes); post-change: still 3 — 0 new type errors in `token-usage-stats.tsx` or `lib/api.ts`. **Neighbour test regression**: co-ran `test/components/token-usage-stats-cache.test.tsx` (2 cases) + `test/components/token-usage-stats-context.test.tsx` (8 cases) = 10/10 green, 0 regression. The new Row 3-mini block sits between Row 3 (usage bar) and Row 3a (context) so existing selector paths in those tests are unaffected. **SOP Step 3 fingerprint grep** (`_conn()|await conn.commit()|datetime('now')|VALUES.*?[,)]`): 0 matches in `components/omnisight/token-usage-stats.tsx` or `lib/api.ts`. **Scope discipline**: p95 outlier highlight (ZZ.A3 checkbox 4) + tests (ZZ.A3 checkbox 5) intentionally untouched — strict "only complete the marked checkbox, no scope creep". No backend / prod-artefact changes — all lifting in two frontend files. Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- [x] 超過 p95 的 turn 做 outlier highlight（視覺提醒「這 turn 異常慢」）。 <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A3 checkbox 4: landed per-model "SLOW" outlier badge on the Row 3 mini-stats block in `components/omnisight/token-usage-stats.tsx`. **Signal**: `turn_metrics.latency_ms` (per-turn LLM call duration) from the rolling `turnHistoryByModel` buffer (HISTORY_DEPTH=10, landed w/ ZZ.A3 checkbox 3). Chose latency over "gap" because the spec says "這 turn 異常慢" — the attribute-of-this-turn signal maps directly to `latency_ms` (which IS this turn's duration); gap is an inter-turn derived metric that isn't "of the turn". **p95 on prior turns only**: compute sorted-list nearest-rank p95 (`idx = min(n-1, max(0, ceil(n*0.95)-1))`) from the history EXCLUDING the latest entry; then check `latest > p95(prior)`. Including the latest in p95 is tautological — latest is always ≤ max of the set it belongs to, so `latest > p95` would degenerate into `latest > max - epsilon` which never fires at small n. Excluding the latest makes the test read as "latest turn exceeds the 95th percentile of the preceding turns" — a clean outlier definition. **Minimum samples**: `MIN_PRIOR=4` → need 5 total turns before the badge can light up. With HISTORY_DEPTH=10 the effective p95 with 4-9 prior samples approximates `max(prior)` (ceil(4*0.95)-1=3 last-of-4 / ceil(9*0.95)-1=8 last-of-9), which at this buffer depth is the right "strictly faster than any recent turn" signal. Document in a source comment so a future reader switching to a larger buffer understands the interaction. **Positive-latency filter**: `filter(l => Number.isFinite(l) && l > 0)` defends against degenerate samples where the backend emitted `latency_ms=0` (callback timing race / end-before-start on a stub — shouldn't happen in prod but seen in fixtures), so zero-latency samples don't drag the p95 down and cry wolf. **Visual**: inline `<span>` prepended before the "avg gap" text when outlier — carries an `AlertTriangle` (size=10, already imported) + literal text "SLOW" in `text-[var(--hardware-orange)] font-semibold animate-pulse`. Intentionally NOT red — context-critical warning is already `critical-red` (ZZ.A2 checkbox 5) and uses `AlertTriangle`; differentiating colour keeps the two alarms visually distinct ("context near cap" vs "this turn is slow"). **Tooltip**: `這 turn 異常慢 — latency Xms 超過 p95 (Yms) of 最近 N turns` in the same Mandarin-first framing as the checkbox spec text, so operators see the exact numbers driving the alarm without needing to expand the card. `aria-label` carries the English translation for a11y. **Rendered iff `isOutlier`**: when latest is not an outlier, the badge simply doesn't render — no visual slot kept (keeps Row 3 mini-stats compact on happy-path turns). **`data-testid="turn-p95-outlier"`** seeded for the next-checkbox-row vitest (ZZ.A3 checkbox 5 p95 outlier 判斷). **Module-global audit per SOP Step 1**: no new module-global state — `p95Latency` / `isOutlier` / `priorLatencies` / `latestLatency` are per-render derived values inside the existing Row-3-mini IIFE; `turnHistoryByModel` is pre-existing component-local `useState` from ZZ.A3 checkbox 3 (per-mount React state). **`tsc --noEmit`**: baseline 3 pre-existing `integration-settings.tsx` errors (TS2322 `GitForgeTokenTestResult` vs `TestResult` index-signature mismatch, documented in prior checkbox notes); post-change: still 3 — 0 new type errors. **Neighbour test regression**: `test/components/token-usage-stats-cache.test.tsx` (2) + `test/components/token-usage-stats-context.test.tsx` (8) = 10/10 green, 0 regression. The new outlier `<span>` is conditionally rendered and prepended within the existing `turn-mini-stats` container so the existing `turn-avg-gap` / `turn-tool-count` / `turn-failed-count` selectors in any future test remain stable. **SOP Step 3 fingerprint grep** (`_conn()|await conn.commit()|datetime('now')|VALUES.*?[,)]`): 0 matches in `token-usage-stats.tsx`. **Scope discipline**: the matrix test (ZZ.A3 checkbox 5: gap 計算正確性 / tool failure count / p95 outlier 判斷) intentionally untouched — strict "only complete the marked checkbox, no scope creep". No backend changes, no SSE wiring changes — all lifting in the single IIFE inside `token-usage-stats.tsx`. Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->
- [x] 測試：gap 計算正確性（含 timezone 一致）、tool failure count、p95 outlier 判斷。 <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.A3 checkbox 5: landed the matrix regression guard locking the three ZZ.A3 UI contracts the earlier checkboxes landed but never tested. New file `test/components/token-usage-stats-turn-stats.test.tsx` (10 cases in 3 describe blocks). **Block 1 — inter-turn gap formula** (3 cases): `avg gap = mean((delta_ts - latency))` rounded across multiple turns — emits three `turn_metrics` at +0 / +1s (lat=200) / +2.5s (lat=300) → gaps 800 / 1200 → avg "1000ms" verifying `Math.round((800 + 1200) / 2)`; **timezone consistency** — same three absolute moments serialised in Taipei `+08:00` (20:00:00 / 20:00:01 / 20:00:02.500) must yield the identical "1000ms" because `Date.parse()` normalises any offset to epoch ms (if the component computed in local-time-aware Date objects the +08:00 gaps would balloon into 28.8-million-ms); **<2 turns** — single turn has no consecutive pair → renders "avg gap —". **Block 2 — tool failure count red badge** (3 cases): `failureCount > 0` renders the failed digit in `text-[var(--critical-red)] font-semibold`, tooltip contains the failing tool name (`run_bash`) so operators see WHICH tool retried without expanding the card; **zeroed-snapshot clear** — first turn with 2 failures gets red, next turn's pass-through zeroed snapshot (`tool_call_count=0, tool_failure_count=0`) clears the red → locks the ZZ.A3 checkbox 2 "emit before early-return" invariant on the frontend side (without this, stale red would stick across turn boundaries); **NULL degradation** — no `turn_tool_stats` seen yet renders `tools — / failed —` in muted with tooltip `"no turn_tool_stats seen for this model yet"`, and crucially the em-dash failed cell does NOT carry the red class (else "no data" would masquerade as a critical failing turn — the same NULL-vs-genuine-zero contract ZZ.A1 / ZZ.A2 established elsewhere on this card). **Block 3 — p95 outlier SLOW badge** (4 cases): **fires** — 4 priors [100,200,300,400] + latest 1000 (p95=400 via nearest-rank `ceil(4 * 0.95) - 1 = 3`), tooltip contains `1000ms` + `(400ms)`, badge uses `text-[var(--hardware-orange)]` + `animate-pulse` (NOT critical-red — reserved for ZZ.A2 context-critical); **does-not-fire under threshold** — same priors + latest 350 → 350 > 400 false → no badge (locks strict `>` boundary — flipping to `>=` would silently light up equal-to-p95 latencies); **<5 turns** — 4 total turns (3 priors + 1 latest) → `priorLatencies.length = 3 < MIN_PRIOR=4` → no badge even on a clearly-outlying 10_000ms latest (documents "don't cry wolf on a cold card"); **filter-then-count ordering** — 5 total turns but the first prior is a degenerate 0ms sample → after filter `l > 0` we have 3 real priors (< MIN_PRIOR) → badge refuses to fire (locks filter-before-count invariant; flipping to count-before-filter would silently re-enable the regression the positive-latency filter was added to prevent). **Test infrastructure**: uses `test/helpers/sse.ts::primeSSE` (same pattern as ZZ.A2 `token-usage-stats-context.test.tsx` and mode-selector tests) to mock `@/lib/api::subscribeEvents`, push synthesised `turn_metrics` + `turn_tool_stats` events via the captured listener, wrapped in `act(...)` so React 18 batches the `setTurnHistoryByModel` / `setToolStatsByModel` / `setContextByModel` updates before assertion. `turn_metrics` emitted BEFORE `turn_tool_stats` in every tool-stats test so `lastMetricsModelRef.current` is set — otherwise summarizer-attributed events never reach the card (documented in the component as the best-effort-attribution heuristic). **Test results**: 10/10 new tests green. Co-run with ZZ.A1 `token-usage-stats-cache.test.tsx` (2 cases) + ZZ.A2 `token-usage-stats-context.test.tsx` (8 cases): 20/20 green, 0 regression. **Module-global state audit per SOP Step 1**: no new module-global state introduced — `vi.mock("@/lib/api", ...)` hoists per-file, `primeSSE` captures listeners in a per-call closure, `beforeEach` resets the mock between cases. Component-local `turnHistoryByModel` / `toolStatsByModel` are existing per-mount React state from ZZ.A3 checkboxes 3 / 4 (not module-global). **Fingerprint grep** (`_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]`): 0 matches in the new test file. **Scope discipline**: test-only row — no prod code touched. Backend scope-5 coverage (gap 計算 / tool failure / p95) is lifted in the earlier backend test files (`test_turn_boundary_stamps.py` × 6 cases / `test_turn_tool_stats_sse.py` × 5 cases / `test_turn_metrics_sse.py` × 6 cases — already green from the prior 4 checkboxes); the frontend matrix guard is what this row uniquely adds. Commit co-authors git env + git global users per CLAUDE.md. -->

**Wave A 小計**：**1.5 day**

shared state。`event_log` 的 `tenant_insert_value()` 走 contextvar，跨 worker 不 leak。**Production readiness**: dev-only，無 config / migration 動作 (重用既有 `event_log` + `_PERSIST_EVENT_TYPES` 機制)；rolling restart 立即生效；rollback = revert commit。**Scope 紀律**: 只完成 checkbox 3；checkbox 4 (emission 時機 / ring buffer LRU / drawer memory-leak 測試) 的 per-turn emission timing asserts + drawer unmount leak 驗證留給下個 - [x] 測試：turn emission 時機、ring buffer LRU、drawer 渲染不 leak memory。 <!-- 2026-04-24 ZZ.B1 checkbox 4: **Backend** — new `backend/tests/test_turn_emission_timing.py` (7 cases) 鎖 `TokenTrackingCallback.on_llm_end` 的 emission timing 契約：(1) exactly one `turn_metrics` + one `turn.complete` per on_llm_end (timeout proves silence ≥ 100ms — 第三個 event 不存在)、turn_id 每 turn 唯一 (2) strict ordering metrics-then-complete across 3 consecutive turns (6 events, 無 cross-turn interleaving) (3) exception isolation — `emit_turn_metrics` raises 時 `emit_turn_complete` 仍 fire；反之亦然 (monkeypatch `backend.events.emit_turn_metrics` / `emit_turn_complete` 成 `lambda: raise RuntimeError`；`on_llm_end` 外層 try/except 吞住 exception 不 crash caller) (4) timing stamps 在 `on_chat_model_start` / `on_llm_start` 被 set — drift guard：若 retro-assigned 於 `on_llm_end` 則 latency 會塌成 ~0、ZZ.A3 inter-turn gap 公式會每列都顯示 spurious zero (5) `latency_ms` 反映真 wall-clock elapsed — `time.sleep(0.05)` 50ms window → payload latency_ms ≥ 40 (jitter floor) & < 5000 (catch stale _start from __init__) & `turn_metrics.latency_ms === turn.complete.latency_ms` (frontend merge 一致) (6) `_prompt_messages` stash 在 chat_start 到 llm_end 之間保持完整 (system + user + tool with tool_name)；`on_llm_start` (non-chat) 清空 stale stash (防 cross-turn leak). **Frontend** — new `test/components/turn-timeline-memory.test.tsx` (8 cases) 鎖 ring buffer LRU 跨來源一致性 + drawer 不 leak：(1) mixed-source LRU — 5 turn 交錯 turn_metrics + turn.complete + maxTurns=3 → 最舊 2 evict、counter 繼續累積不回繞 (#3/#4/#5 survive)；LRU 是 source-independent (2) turn.complete upgrade-in-place 不長 buffer — emit turn_metrics → 1 card；emit matching turn.complete → 仍 1 card、summary 被 merged summary 取代 (match-by-model-fallback 路徑) (3) component unmount 關 SSE subscription 恰好一次 (4) drawer open+close 的 `window.addEventListener('keydown')` / `removeEventListener('keydown')` count 平衡 — spy on window 的 add/remove、baseline capture、cycle 後 netAdds === netRemoves 鎖 useEffect cleanup 有執行 (5) 10 cycles open/close 不 accumulate listeners 也不 accumulate DOM nodes — drawer count 每輪回 0、add-count ≥ 10 (否則 "cleanup 在 open 也 fire" 這種病態能讓 net=0 coincidentally) (6) unmount 時 drawer 還開著仍 remove keydown listener — classic React leak 防線 (7) ESC 關 drawer 後再按一次 ESC 是 silent no-op 不 throw 不 reopen (handler 真的 removed 了、不是只被 component-conditional rendered) (8) live turn.complete 升級已開 drawer 的 card — drawer 仍恰好 1 個 instance、"Waiting for turn.complete" placeholder 消失 (drawer open 跨 SSE update 不 orphan). **Verification**: 7/7 new backend + 8/8 new frontend 綠；co-run 全部 turn-related tests — backend 31/31 (timing+complete+metrics+tool_stats+boundary_stamps) 綠 0 regression；frontend 52/52 (7 files: turn-timeline + drawer + complete + memory + 3 token-usage-stats) 綠 0 regression。**TS typecheck**: 3 pre-existing `integration-settings.tsx` TS2322 (前 checkboxes 文件化) baseline 不變、0 new errors。**ESLint**: 清 (修 afterEach unused import 後)。**SOP Step 3 fingerprint grep** (`_conn\(\)|await conn\.commit\(\)|datetime\('now'\)|VALUES.*\?[,)]`): 0 matches on both new files。**Module-global 稽核 (SOP Step 1)**: 無新 module-global state — `trackWindowListeners` 每 test 各自 capture + restore `window.addEventListener` 的 monkey patch (per-test closure)；`vi.mock("@/lib/api")` hoisted per-file；`beforeEach(vi.clearAllMocks)` 重置；backend `TokenTrackingCallback` 是 per-instance (`self._start` / `self._prompt_messages`)、monkeypatch 走 pytest fixture 自動 teardown、無 leak。**Scope 紀律**: test-only row — 0 prod code change；checkbox 3 已覆蓋的 single-source LRU / 升級契約不重測、聚焦此 row 獨特的 **mixed-source LRU** + **drawer listener balance** + **emission ordering/isolation/timing**。Commit co-authors git env + git global users 符合 CLAUDE.md。 -->

'auto_title')` — **at-most-once 由 PG 強制、不依賴 Python lock**)。 (c) 新 `emit_session_titled(session_id, user_id, title, *, source="auto"|"user", broadcast_scope="user")` in `backend/events.py` + `SSESessionTitled` Pydantic model + `SSE_EVENT_SCHEMAS["session.titled"]` 註冊（frontend codegen drift guard）。 (d) `backend/routers/chat.py::_persist_and_emit` 擴：每次 INSERT 後 fire `upsert_chat_session` + 若 role='user' 走 `_maybe_schedule_auto_title`：check metadata.auto_title 已存在 / inflight dedupe set / count 達 3 → `asyncio.create_task(_generate_auto_title(...))`。背景 task 用自己的 pool conn + restore tenant context var + read 頭 3 user turn → `_condense_turn` (first non-empty line + 240 char cap) → `llm.ainvoke(prompt)` with 15s timeout → `_sanitize_title` (strip outer quotes / drop "Title:"|"Chat:"|"Session:"|"Conversation:" 前綴 / 80 char cap / trim trailing 標點) → `set_session_auto_title` → winner emit `session.titled`。Per-worker `_auto_title_inflight: set[(user_id, session_id)]` 是 latency 優化 SOP 答案 #3「故意每 worker 獨立」，PG 的 conditional UPDATE 是跨 worker at-most-once 的 correctness gate。 (e) 新 `GET /chat/sessions?limit=50` endpoint (auth 繼承 require_operator, limit clamp [1, 200])。**Frontend** — (a) `lib/api.ts` 擴 `SSEEvent` union 加 `session.titled` + `SSE_EVENT_TYPES` whitelist + 新 `ChatSessionMetadata` / `ChatSessionItem` / `ChatSessionListResponse` interface + `fetchChatSessions({limit?})` 走 `request` wrapper。 (b) 新 component `components/omnisight/chat-sessions-sidebar.tsx` (~230 行)：mount fetchChatSessions；subscribe `session.titled` SSE → 找 row by session_id → in-place rename (auto vs user 分支) + 搬到最上方 (**不 refetch**)；subscribe `chat.message` SSE → bump 對應 row 的 updated_at；unknown-session `session.titled` inject stub row；新 export helper `resolveSessionTitle(item)` 實作 fallback chain `user_title ?? auto_title ?? hash` (checkbox 2 的 UI 邏輯先入本 row、後端不決定 render choice)；`onSelect(sessionId)` callback 給 future app/page.tsx grid layout
